Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 20 BBY :: The Onderonian Civil War , fought between the Separatist-backed Royal Court of Onderon and the Republic-backed Onderon rebels , ends after two years with the death of King Sanjay Rash and the re-installation of King Ramsis Dendup .
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 20 BBY :: A funeral is held for Steela Gerrera , the leader of the Onderon rebels , after she is killed in the final battle against Onderon's droid garrison.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 20 BBY :: Jedi younglings Byph , Ganodi , Gungi , Katooni , Petro and Zatt , escorted by Ahsoka Tano, travel to the planet Ilum to claim the kyber crystals they would use to construct their lightsabers, following the Jedi ritual known as The Gathering .
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 20 BBY :: The Ohnaka Gang attack the Paladin -class corvette Crucible while it was transporting the Jedi younglings who had undergone the Gathering back to Coruscant, attempting to seize their kyber crystals and sell them on the black market. Although Ahsoka Tano drives the pirates off the ship, she is taken captive.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 20 BBY :: Grievous attacks a Republic fleet under the command of Obi-Wan Kenobi. Launching a surprise assault, Confederate battleships and droid starfighters eliminate the Republic Star Destroyers and their outmatched starfighter squadrons. Kenobi mounts a defense of his flagship, the Negotiator , with Commander Cody and clone troopers from the 212th Attack Battalion . Yet they are forced to evacuate from the Negotiator and conced it to Grievous's control.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 20 BBY :: The Jedi younglings attempt to rescue Ahsoka Tano from the Ohnaka Gang on Florrum . They infiltrate Ohnaka's camp and successfully retrieve her. However, when returning to the Crucible , it is shot down by a WLO-5 battle tank and Ahsoka and the younglings are recaptured.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 20 BBY :: Still angered by his capture at the hands of Hondo Ohnaka, Dooku orders for a Separatist invasion of Florrum , nearly crippling the Ohnaka Gang.

Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 20 BBY :: Shortly after yet another naval battle against Separatist forces, the Republic prepares to convene a conference over Carida . The Republic also assembles D-Squad , their secret weapon to steal the Confederacy's encryption module , which scrambled all Separatist communication in the region.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 20 BBY :: D-Squad steals the encryption module aboard Aut-O's flagship .
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 20 BBY :: Apailana is elected Queen of Naboo, succeeding Queen Neeyutnee, after being supported in her campaign by Padmé Amidala.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 20 BBY :: Despite having stolen the encryption module, D-Squad are left stranded on Abafar .
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 20 BBY :: With the help of the amnestic clone CC-5576-39 "Gregor," D-Squad escape from Abafar .
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 20 BBY :: As the Republic strategy conference is held aboard the space station Valor , the Carida incident occurs as the result of a plot by the Confederacy to load the Venator -class Star Destroyer Renown with enough rhydonium to destroy the space station. The plot is discovered and foiled by D-Squad.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: Kay Vess is born in the city of Canto Bight on the planet Cantonica to Riko Vess .
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: Maul and Opress duel two Jedi at an Outer Rim spaceport , killing both of them.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: Maul and Opress raid the Cybloc Transfer Station and depart by commandeering an Aurore -class freighter .
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: A skirmish takes place on the world of Florrum between the pirate forces of Hondo Ohnaka , aided by the Jedi Order , and renegade pirate forces under the command of Maul and Opress. Jedi High Council member Adi Gallia is killed by Savage Opress.

Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: Determined to conquer Mandalore, Maul recruits allies to form the Shadow Collective .
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY > Determined to conquer Mandalore, Maul recruits allies to form the Shadow Collective . :: Maul and Opress travel to Mustafar with their new allies of Death Watch to convince Black Sun to join them in conquering Mandalore. After they refuse, Opress beheades most of the Black Sun's leadership, and the sole surviving crime Vigo , Ziton Moj , agrees to supply Death Watch.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY > Determined to conquer Mandalore, Maul recruits allies to form the Shadow Collective . :: Maul and the Shadow Collective travel to Nal Hutta to gain the backing of the Grand Hutt Council . After Maul's threats, Jabba orders their bounty hunters to kill him, causing a fight to break out. The bounty hunters and few surviving guards flee.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY > Determined to conquer Mandalore, Maul recruits allies to form the Shadow Collective . :: Landing on the grounds of Jabba's Palace on Tatooine , Death Watch warriors attack Jabba's men and enter the main chamber, Jabba concedes defeat and gives the Hutt Clan 's backing to Death Watch's coup on Mandalore.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY > Determined to conquer Mandalore, Maul recruits allies to form the Shadow Collective . :: Maul renders the influencial Morubas Crime Family powerless, thereby eliminating their potential complication in the expansion of his Shadow Collective.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: The Shadow Collective takes over Mandalore from the pacifist New Mandalorian government of Duchess Satine Kryze; a Mandalorian civil war erupts between Maul loyalists and a Mandalore resistance .

Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY > The Shadow Collective takes over Mandalore from the pacifist New Mandalorian government of Duchess Satine Kryze; a Mandalorian civil war erupts between Maul loyalists and a Mandalore resistance . :: Maul betrays Pre Vizsla of Death Watch and kills him, taking the Darksaber with the intent to rule over Mandalore. He captures Duchess Satine Kryze in order to lure his nemesis and her lover, Obi-Wan Kenobi; though Maul murders Kryze and takes Kenobi captive during the Battle of Mandalore , the Jedi is freed by Death Watch dissidents led by Satine's sister, Bo-Katan Kryze, who continues resisting against the Shadow Collective.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY > The Shadow Collective takes over Mandalore from the pacifist New Mandalorian government of Duchess Satine Kryze; a Mandalorian civil war erupts between Maul loyalists and a Mandalore resistance . :: Darth Sidious secretly travels to Mandalore, killing Savage Opress and seizing his former apprentice, Maul.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: The planet Cato Neimoidia , housing the headquarters of the Trade Federation, is invaded by the Confederacy of Independent Systems. The Republic launches a defense of the planet, after having taken control of it previously. Anakin Skywalker and Ahsoka Tano take part of the battle, but are recalled to Coruscant following the bombing of the Jedi Temple hangar.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: A funeral is held for the victims of a bombing of the Jedi Temple. Various Jedi and Republic dignitaries are in attendance, including Padawan Barriss Offee, who is later revealed to be behind the attack.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: After her arrest for the murder of Letta Turmond , Ahsoka Tano manages to effect an escape from the Republic military base where she was being held.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: Ahsoka Tano is captured in the Coruscant underworld by Coruscant Guard shock troopers under the command of Plo Koon and Anakin Skywalker.

Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: Ahsoka Tano is put on trial for her alleged role in the bombing of the Jedi Temple hangar . She is represented by Padmé Amidala, who seaks desperately to find a way to clear her client, but with little success. Ultimately, Tano is cleared of the charges as Anakin Skywalker discovered that the crime had been committed by Barriss Offee, disillusioned with the behavior of the Jedi Order during the Clone Wars.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: After Anakin Skywalker learned that Offee framed his Padawan for the bombing of the Jedi Temple hangar, he fights her in the Jedi Temple and brings her to Tano's trial.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: Separatist Admiral Trench captures Ringo Vinda 's orbital graving docks , prompting a Republic effort to retake the world. Republic forces advance on Trench's command center, but lose their momentum when clone trooper Tup abruptly executes Jedi General Tiplar due to a malfunction in his behavioral modification biochip causing him to carry out Order 66 prematurely.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: ARC trooper Fives escapes from Tipoca City on Kamino while trying to uncover the mystery of why his friend Tup had suddenly killed a Jedi Master, and after discovering he was in danger of memory-wiping for what he knew about the chip he had discovered in Tup's head.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: Fives escapes from the Grand Republic Medical Facility on Coruscant, where he had been taken for evaluation by Supreme Chancellor Palpatine. He is pursued by several Republic clone troopers and Jedi.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: Anakin Skywalker and Rex go to Coruscant's Level 1325 to find Fives, who is on the run after being framed for an attempted attack on Palpatine. Fives is eventually killed by Clone Commander Fox of the Coruscant Guard , responding to a report from a probe droid.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: By this point, the Midorian Era had occurred, with some academics believing that a trade relationship was established between the Vaks and Garwians during that time.

Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: General Yiv the Benevolent , the leader of the Nikardun Destiny nation of the Unknown Regions, begins a series of conquests against neighboring states.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY > General Yiv the Benevolent , the leader of the Nikardun Destiny nation of the Unknown Regions, begins a series of conquests against neighboring states. :: Two Paccosh refugee starships escape from their homeworld of Rapacc while it is under blockade by General Yiv, rendezvousing in a quadruple star system , but one is unable to travel further. The working ship enters the territory of the Chiss Ascendancy , but the Nikardun locate it at its exit point in the Dioya system due to its Void Guide navigator and destroy it . The Chiss witness the incident, but have their attention drawn away when the Nikardun orchestrate an attack on the Chiss homeworld of Csilla . Later, Senior Captain Mitth'raw'nuruodo , looking into the attack on Csilla, investigates the wreckage in the Dioya system, leading to his discovery of the Nikardun conquests. The attack helps cover up the Sunrise's civil war orchestrated by Lord " Jixtus " of the Grysk .
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY > General Yiv the Benevolent , the leader of the Nikardun Destiny nation of the Unknown Regions, begins a series of conquests against neighboring states. :: A battle is fought between the forces of the Chiss Ascendancy and three attacking warships from the Paataatus Hiveborn , though they are unidentified at the time of the attack. The Hiveborn have been ordered to attack the Chiss homeworld and capital of Csilla by their Nikardun conquerers, to distract from the massacre of refugees at the Dioya system at the Ascendancy's edge. In turn, the Nikardun have conquered the Paataatus and other nations near the Ascendancy at the behest of the Grysk Hegemony , who seek to encircle, undermine, and ultimately invade the Chiss Ascendancy. The attackers are quickly defeated by the Chiss Defense Force . However, the attack happens during a legislative session of the Chiss Syndicure , sending the Ascendancy's ruling Aristocra into an uproar. The Syndics become single-minded about identifying and punishing the attackers, as no-one had dared to attack Csilla in decades. Supreme General Ba'kif of the Chiss Expansionary Defense Fleet thinks the question of why Csilla had been attacked is of more interest than who had attacked it. Nevertheless, Ba'kif consents to a retaliatory strike.

Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY > General Yiv the Benevolent , the leader of the Nikardun Destiny nation of the Unknown Regions, begins a series of conquests against neighboring states. :: The Chiss Expansionary Defense Fleet conducts a retaliatory strike on Nettehi , the homeworld of the Paataatus, shortly after the attack on Csilla. At the time, the [hiss Syndicure does not have definitive proof that the Paataatus government is behind the attack on Csilla, but justifies the attack as a means to punish the Paataatus for proven encroachments into Ascendancy territory. The Paataatus military is far more prepared to defend their homeworld than most officers of the Chiss Picket Force Six anticipate. Only Senior Captain Thrawn expected their level of readiness, he disobeys the orders of Admiral Ar'alani , breaking off and drawing most Paataatus forces away from the bulk of the Chiss force. In doing so, Thrawn buys the other Chiss warships time to regroup and eliminate most of the Paataatus fighters, securing a Chiss victory.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY > General Yiv the Benevolent , the leader of the Nikardun Destiny nation of the Unknown Regions, begins a series of conquests against neighboring states. :: Senior Captain Thrawn undertakes a mission in order to investigate the point of origin of the refugee starship destroyed by the Nikardun Destiny in Chiss territory. After locating a second derelict vessel, Thrawn identifies the vessels' origin in the Rapacc system . With the assistance of Admiral Ar'alani and the Vigilant , Thrawn manages to discreetly maneuver the heavy cruiser Springhawk into the system, boarding an abandoned mining station with the caregiver Mitth'ali'astov . The Nikardun , having noticed the Vigilant ' s intrusion, send a group of subjugated Paccosh to board the station. Thrawn speaks with the Paccosh, learning the identity of the Nikardun and their leader, General Yiv. The Paccosh let Thrawn leave without reporting him to the Nikardun, allowing Thrawn to pursue further investigation into Nikardun activities. While aboard the asteroid mining station, Thrawn and Thalias meet the Paccosh leader Uingali foar Marocsaa , who gives Thrawn a double ring depicting a chimaera .

Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY > General Yiv the Benevolent , the leader of the Nikardun Destiny nation of the Unknown Regions, begins a series of conquests against neighboring states. :: Thrawn orchestrates a battle in order to capture a blockade frigate of the Nikardun Destiny in the Rapacc system , as he wishes to study them further. Thrawn hires the Pathfinder navigator Qilori to bring the Springhawk into the system near the Nikardun starships. Once there, Thrawn distracts all but one of his pursuers with an automatic shuttle, managing to disable and capture the remaining vessel.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY > General Yiv the Benevolent , the leader of the Nikardun Destiny nation of the Unknown Regions, begins a series of conquests against neighboring states. :: The Ascendancy is asked to send Ambassador Ilparg in a diplomatic mission to the planet Urch , capital of the Urchiv-ki , with whom the Ascendancy have a treaty , and the syndics Thurfian and Zistalmu choose Admiral Ar'alani's Nightdragon man-of-war Vigilant to escort the ambassador. The Syndics insist on sending Thrawn on the mission to prevent him from visiting the Lioaoin Regime as part of his investigations into the Nikardun conquests. However, the invitation is a trap, as the Urchiv-ki have been subjugated by the Nikardun, refusing to allow the Chiss to land. Thrawn notices the presence of a Lioaoin vessel and a Nikardun frigate. In retribution for Thrawn's earlier capture of a Nikardun blockade frigate, allied Lioaoi, Urchiv, and Nikardun ships triy to set up a web to ensnare and capture the Vigilant . However, the Vigilant evades the Urchiv, Lioaoi and Nikardun ships, escaping into hyperspace.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY > General Yiv the Benevolent , the leader of the Nikardun Destiny nation of the Unknown Regions, begins a series of conquests against neighboring states. :: Due to the Lioaoin presence at Urch, Ambassador Ilparg demands that the Vigilant visit the Lioaoin heartworld for an explanation. Upon the Chiss arrival at the heartworld, the Lioaoi deny involvement, while a Nikardun Battle Dreadnought approach the Vigilant . Thrawn identifies himself to the Lioaoi, sparking a brief reaction from several Lioaoin gunboats, that use a pincer maneuver , a decidedly non-Lioaoin tactic associated with with the Nikardun military . These factors allow Thrawn to deduce that the Lioaoi are allied with the Nikardun Destiny and have standing orders regarding him. Thrawn, now convinced that the Nikardun Destiny have been conquering, coercing, and courting alien species near the Ascendancy, devises a plan to trick Yiv into believing he has inadequate information about the Chiss.

Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY > General Yiv the Benevolent , the leader of the Nikardun Destiny nation of the Unknown Regions, begins a series of conquests against neighboring states. :: A diplomatic reception is hosted by the Vak Combine on the Vak homeworld of Primea . Primea serves as a hub for trade and diplomacy in the region, and as such, many nations send representatives to the reception. General Yiv seeks to conquer the Vak Combine, and uses the event to make political inroads with the planet's leaders while also maintaining a fleet of warships in orbit to intimidate his hosts and their guests. Meanwhile, the Garwian Unity sends representatives to attend the event, with Second Defense Overlord Frangelic accompanying them. Thrawn attends the reception under the alias of "Artistic Master Svorno", Chief curator of the Nunech Art Collection , accompanied by Thalias to act as Thrawn's family hostage . Thrawn had made up the concept of Chiss family hostages in the hopes that word of the supposed cultural practice would reach Yiv and cause the Nikardun to question his knowledge of Chiss culture. To Thrawn's pleasant surprise, Yiv is actually present at the reception, and Thrawn asks Qilori of Uandualon to introduce the two men.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY > General Yiv the Benevolent , the leader of the Nikardun Destiny nation of the Unknown Regions, begins a series of conquests against neighboring states. :: After Thrawn travels aboard a Garwian Unity diplomatic mission to Primea to look into General Yiv's attempts at subjugating the planet, Yiv intends to orchestrate Thrawn's death as the Garwians leave Primea. Unplanned for by Yiv, Thrawn and Thalias remain on the planet when the Garwian vessel departs, and the Chiss Ascendancy sends Admiral Ar'alani's Vigilant to collect them. Syndic Irizi'stal'mustro , an Irizi family official wary of Thrawn's inquisition into the Nikardun activities, insists on coming along on the mission. The Vigilant arrives three days after the Garwian diplomat's departure, and is confronted by several Vak patrol ships. One of the starfighters has been commandeered by Thrawn, who uses modulated laserfire to make contact with the Vigilant , sending a schematic of the Vak fighters in case Ar'alani needd to disable them without harming the occupants. However, before the Chiss can take action, four cruisers of the Lioaoin Regime , being sent in place of Nikardun forces by General Yiv, emerge from hyperspace and open fire on the Vigilant . Thrawn attacks one of the cruisers in his Vak fighter, and the Lioaoin respond too late, unsure whether to open fire on a Vak ship. The Vigilant brings Thrawn's fighter aboard, and jumps to hyperspace.

Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY > General Yiv the Benevolent , the leader of the Nikardun Destiny nation of the Unknown Regions, begins a series of conquests against neighboring states. :: The Chiss Ascendancy launches an attack on the heartworld of the Lioaoin Regime in response to the attack against the Chiss warship Vigilant at Primea. Upon arrival at the heartworld, the Vigilant and Thrawn's Springhawk are confronted by twelve Lioaoin warships, four of which directly attack the Chiss vessels. The Vigilant deploys two decoy shuttles, one hidden behind the other, toward the Lioaoin, a tactic that Thrawn had previously used against the Nikardun. The Lioaoin recognize the tactic and prevent the Vigilant from remotely controlling the shuttle; however, Thrawn had intended for them to do so, firing at the shuttle and releasing the breacher missiles hidden within it. The acid from the missiles damages the hull]s of the Lioaoin vessels, and the Vigilant destroys the second shuttle, similarly releasing breaching missiles into the Lioaoin. The Lioaoin refuse to concede, and the four damaged ships fall back, replaced by two more. The Chiss are ultimately victorious, though not without damage to their own vessels.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: Anakin Skywalker and Padmé Amidala conduct a mission to Batuu . Skywalker encounters Senior Captain Mitth'raw'nuruodo of the Chiss Ascendancy, whom he joins forces with.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: Padmé Amidala and Anakin Skywalker, travel to the planet Mokivj to investigate and later neutralize a Separatist droid factory . Thrawn aids them, with the objective to learn about the Clone War and whether technology or nations in the wider galaxy may be able to aid the Chiss in their war against the Nikardun Destiny. During the mission, they discover that the Separatists are manufacturing the C-B3 cortosis battle droid , a cortosis -armored model of B2-series super battle droids , on the planet, using cortosis sourced to the nearby cortosis mine . During their attack on the factory, Thrawn procures a deflector shield generator for the Ascendancy. The mission concludes with a catastrophe. In an effort to collapse the Separatists' cortosis mine on Mokivj, Skywalker accidentally sends lava, ash, and smoke into the planet's best cropland and water, as he did not account for the cortosis in the mine redirecting the heat of the explosions. As a result, the explosive force sends down the planet's crust, triggering volcanic activity and leading to widespread devastation, deforestation, and desertification on Mokivj.

Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: End of the Nikardun Conquests
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY > End of the Nikardun Conquests :: Thrawn sends the caregiver Mitth'ali'astov and the sky-walker Che'ri to return the Vak fighter he had previously commandeered in the escape from Primea, along with a message to the Vak leadership. Vak forces aligned with the Nikardun organize for the capture of the two Chiss and instead deliver a false message, both of which are expected by Thrawn. Yiv sends a threat to the Ascendancy, demanding that Thrawn deliver him a ransom for the issues he has caused. The Ascendancy's Chiss Syndicure decides that the capture of a sky-walker requires a military response. Thrawn travels to Yiv's location in an unarmed freighter with the Pathfinder navigator Qilori , who, having been allied with Yiv, changes the arrival coordinates from above Primea to further out in the system. As Yiv confronts Thrawn with four Nikardun Battle Dreadnoughts , including the flagship Deathless , five Chiss warships, led by Admiral Ar'alani arrive at Primea, informing the Vaks of the locations of orbiting Nikardun vessels, as Thrawn had offered to do in his message. The Vaks allow Ar'alani to fire on the Nikardun, and Yiv sends his three dreadnought escorts to assist his blockade of Primea. With the Springhawk taking damage, the vessel goes dark, and Admiral Ar'alani requests humanitarian aid from nearby Vak patrol boats. The Vak comply, but the Nikardun vessels shoot down the patrol ships, turning the Vak Combine against the Nikardun. Yiv attempts to pull Thrawn's freighter in with a tractor beam, but Thalias and Che'ri release tava mist into the bridge of the Deathless , allowing Thrawn to break free of the tractor beam and instead crash his freighter into the viewport, with his ship rendered unharmed due to the energy shield acquired from Mokivj. Thrawn boards the bridge, rescuing the two Chiss prisoners and capturing Yiv, who becomes a prisoner of the Ascendancy. Following Yiv's disappearance, the Nikardun Destiny is left without a suitably competent successor, and their plans of conquest fall apart.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: Padmé Amidala travels to the planet Scipio to uncover evidence of corruption within the InterGalactic Banking Clan . Amidala and Baron Rush Clovis are targeted by the bounty hunter Embo , who was hired by Darth Sidious to intimidate and drive Clovis off-world.

Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: Having helped uncover corruption in the InterGalactic Banking Clan, Rush Clovis is appointed as the new head of the bank, only to be coerced by Count Dooku into raising interest rates for the Republic, at which point Separatist forces occupy Scipio to cement their position. The Republic responds with its own invasion .
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: Mace Windu and Jar Jar Binks are dispatched to the planet Bardotta to investigate the mysterious disappearance of the people of Queen Julia , eventually determining that Mother Talzin is responsible for the disappearances.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: Mace Windu and Jar Jar Binks travel to the moon Zardossa Stix to rescue Queen Julia of Bardotta , who had been kidnapped by the Frangawl Cult . They defeat High Seneschal Peteen and Mother Talzin.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: Obi-Wan Kenobi and Anakin Skywalker travel to the planet Oba Diah to investigate the Pyke Syndicate and what they may have known about the death of Jedi Master Sifo-Dyas . They discover that the Pykes had killed Sifo-Dyas at the behest of the Sith . Count Dooku, arrives on Oba Diah and engages in lightsaber combat with the Jedi. During the duel, the Jedi learn that Dooku is the man named Tyranus who selected Jango Fett as the clone template , and that the Sith had been involved in the creation of the clone army.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: Yoda undertakes a mission to Moraband , while on a journey to discover the secrets of eternal consciousness within the Force. Upon his arrival, he is confronted by illusions of Sith wraiths, as well as the specter of Darth Bane , the ancient Sith Lord who created the Rule of Two . After facing his fears as part of his trials he is deemed worthy to learn the secrets of immortality.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: Obi-Wan Kenobi and Anakin Skywalker travel to the planet Utapau to investigate the death of the Jedi Master Tu-Anh whilst she was on the planet. They discover a Separatist conspiracy to create a powerful weapon using a massive kyber crystal. They pursue and eventually destroy the crystal.

Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: A funeral is held for Jedi Master Tu-Anh , who had perished as part of an arms deal between the Confederacy and the Sugi crime lord Endente .
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: Ahsoka Tano meets the Martez sisters at their repair shop on Level 1313 .
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: After being stationed on Coruscant with the Open Circle Fleet , Anakin Skywalker takes part in the Outer Rim Sieges campaign.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: As the Outer Rim Sieges begins to turn the Clone Wars in the Republic's favor, the Separatist Bracca Invasion occurs.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: Begining of the Battle of Anaxes , an engagement that would last for several months. The Harch Separatist Admiral Trench launches an assault on Anaxes , which serves as one of the Republic's strongholds and the location of a crucial shipyard for the Republic during the war.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: Clone Force 99 end an insurrection on Yalbec Prime . Under the command of Clone Sergeant Hunter , the clone commandos Tech , Crosshair and Wrecker kill the Yalbec queen .
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: Anakin Skywalker leads Captain Rex and the Bad Batch to a unsanctioned operation to the planet Skako Minor , which is neutral in the Clone Wars, to rescue ARC trooper Echo from the Techno Union in the city of Purkoll .
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: The Confederacy targets the planet Mahranee for its plentiful resources, laying waste to it's capital city . Separatst forces kill all the Mahran refugees who were escaping from their embattled homeworld, in what is later known as the atrocity on Mahranee .
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: Asajj Ventress captures Moregi , a Volpai with a bounty from the Rang Clan on his head, on the planet Pantora with the aid of undercover Jedi Master Quinlan Vos.

Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: Asajj Ventress and Quinlan Vos rescue the family of Marg Krim , the new head of the Pyke Syndicate , from Black Sun captivity on Mustafar . Black Sun retaliates by attacking the Pyke Syndicate's stronghold on Oba Diah .
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: Asajj Ventress and Quinlan Vos go on mission to assassinate Count Dooku, who is attending a gathering on the Confederate capital world of Raxus Secundus . Dooku defeates Vos and takes him prisoner. Ventress escapes to warn the Jedi of Vos' capture.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: Maul escapes from Darth Sidious' facility on Stygeon Prime to a Shadow Collective camp on Zanbar with the assistance of the Mandalorian super commandos Rook Kast and Gar Saxon . Through Maul's escape, Sidious hopes to track him to the Nightsisters' Mother Talzin and end her threat to the Sith.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY > Maul escapes from Darth Sidious' facility on Stygeon Prime to a Shadow Collective camp on Zanbar with the assistance of the Mandalorian super commandos Rook Kast and Gar Saxon . Through Maul's escape, Sidious hopes to track him to the Nightsisters' Mother Talzin and end her threat to the Sith. :: Separatist forces led by General Grievous battles the Shadow Collective at the latter's camp on Zanbar. The Collective is defeated and retreats to Ord Mantell.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY > Maul escapes from Darth Sidious' facility on Stygeon Prime to a Shadow Collective camp on Zanbar with the assistance of the Mandalorian super commandos Rook Kast and Gar Saxon . Through Maul's escape, Sidious hopes to track him to the Nightsisters' Mother Talzin and end her threat to the Sith. :: Anticipating a major Separatist strike spearheaded by Count Dooku and General Grievous, the Shadow Collective prepares a trap for the two pair. As a Battle of Ord Mantell ensues, Maul springs his trap, capturing both Dooku and Grievous.

Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY > Maul escapes from Darth Sidious' facility on Stygeon Prime to a Shadow Collective camp on Zanbar with the assistance of the Mandalorian super commandos Rook Kast and Gar Saxon . Through Maul's escape, Sidious hopes to track him to the Nightsisters' Mother Talzin and end her threat to the Sith. :: A team of Jedi, led by Mace Windu and Obi-Wan Kenobi, carriy out an assault on the supply outpost Vizsla Keep 09 in an effort to capture Maul and Count Dooku. Jedi General Tiplee is killed, and after Death Watch forces incapacitate the Jedi, Maul and Dooku are able to escape.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY > Maul escapes from Darth Sidious' facility on Stygeon Prime to a Shadow Collective camp on Zanbar with the assistance of the Mandalorian super commandos Rook Kast and Gar Saxon . Through Maul's escape, Sidious hopes to track him to the Nightsisters' Mother Talzin and end her threat to the Sith. :: The Second Battle of Dathomir occurs; Darth Sidious, Dooku, and Grievous kill Mother Talzin, who sacrifices herself for Maul to live.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: Maul adds Dryden Vos ' Crimson Dawn to the Shadow Collective's ranks.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: Asajj Ventress hires Boba Fett's syndicate to assist her in the rescue of Quinlan Vos from Dooku's Serenno fortress . Shattered by Dooku's well-woven words and confused by the lying of Ventress that occurred prior, Quinlan Vos furiously battles his lover instead, joining Dooku and forcing the infiltrators to escape.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: The Jedi, assisted by Asajj Ventress, rescue Quinlan Vos from Dooku's Providence -class Dreadnought , without knowing that he had already embraced the dark side and become Dooku's apprentice.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: The Republic mounts a mission to capture a strategic Separatist supply storage base containing medications, weapons, ship repair materials, and foodstuffs vital to worlds in need. However, the facility and all of its supplies are destroyed by Quinlan Vos, secretly serving Dooku.

Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: The Republic launches an attack on a Confederate listening post on the planet Vanqor . However, Vos leaked intelligence about the operation to the Separatists and they are able to evacuate before Republic forces arrive.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: A battle on Christophsis occurs; Asajj Ventress guides Quinlan Vos to return to the light with her sacrifice.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: Obi-Wan Kenobi and Quinlan Vos brings the recovered body of Asajj Ventress to the Nightsister lair on Dathomir. Under Kenobi's watch, Vos lets go of Ventress and returns her to her kin, resting his lover's body in the waters of the Nightsister cavern, leaving her to the embrace of green waters and voices of her sisters.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: A bombing at the Jedi Temple is conducted by the Separatist Captain Rackham Sear . However, Jedi Master Depa Billaba and initiate Caleb Dume sense the explosives as they go off, and they foil the bombing and Sear perishes in the process. Depa Billaba decides to take Caleb Dume as his Padawan.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: Depa Billaba and her battalion are sent to the planet Kardoa , where they fight Separatist forces led by Colonel Coburn Sear . After the sacrifice of clone trooper CT-1157 to save Caleb Dume, the Separatists are forced to retreat.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: The planet Mygeeto , which was aligned with the InterGalactic Banking Clan , became host to a Confederate military presence, with Grievous and Coburn Sear overseeing a detachment of the Separatist Droid Army. The Republic leads an invasion , but its troops become outnumbered and require the reinforcements of Depa Billaba and her battalion , who are helped by Skull Squadron , a Mandalorian unit led by Fenn Rau . Caleb Dume beheades Sear and Grievous escapes.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: A funeral is held for clone trooper Stance on Mygeeto by Depa Billaba, Caleb Dume, and Stance's fellow clone soldiers.

Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: Anticipating the fulfillment of Darth Sidious' Grand Plan with Anakin Skywalker as the Sith Lord's apprentice, Maul enters the Mandalore city of Sundari in an attempt to lure Obi-Wan Kenobi and Anakin Skywalker and prevent the Plan's completion by killing them.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: The Battle of Yerbana concludes. Lady Bo-Katan Kryze and Ahsoka Tano request assistance from Kenobi and Skywalker to capture Maul and take Mandalore.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: The Battle of Coruscant begins. General Grievous storms the Galactic City and kidnaps Chancellor Palpatine. Republic forces led by Obi-Wan Kenobi and Anakin Skywalker are recalled from the Outer Rim Sieges to rescue the Chancellor.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: Ahsoka Tano and Rex lead a division of Skywalker's 501st Legion to Mandalore, initiating the Siege of Mandalore .
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: Maul tells the Shadow Collective leaders to go into hiding, and the syndicates soon split up.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: Kenobi and Skywalker rescue Chancellor Palpatine at the Battle of Coruscant, where Skywalker executes Count Dooku.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: Senators concerned with the Chancellor's growing powers join the Delegation of 2,000 and take part in the Cantham House meetings hosted by Senator Bail Organa. Key figures at Cantham House include Padmé Amidala and Mon Mothma , who sign the Petition of 2,000 requesting Chancellor Palpatine's relinquishment of his emergency powers.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: The Jedi Council assigns Anakin Skywalker to secretly spy on Palpatine.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: Maul attempts to recruit Ahsoka Tano as an ally against Darth Sidious, revealing his insight that Anakin Skywalker would become his new apprentice, but instead Tano defeats and captures Maul.

Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: Bo-Katan Kryze's Mandalorians and the clones take Sundari from Maul's forces, capturing Rook Kast and Gar Saxon.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: The Siege of Saleucami , one of the longest lasting campaigns of the Outer Rim Sieges, comes to an end. Jedi General Quinlan Vos moves his troops to Boz Pity , the siege of which was one of the most intense of the Clone Wars. Stass Allie , a Jedi High Council member and cousin to the late Adi Gallia, leads the 91st Mobile Reconnaissance Corps in securing Saleucami.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: The Battle of Kashyyyk occurs. Jedi Generals Luminara Unduli , Quinlan Vos, and Yoda lead Republic forces in defending Kachirho from the Separatists alongside Wookiee warriors , who are led by Grakchawwaa , King of Kashyyyk, and Chieftain Tarfful.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: The Battle of Utapau occurs. General Grievous, now leader of the Separatists, is killed by Obi-Wan Kenobi shortly after the other leaders are sent to the Klegger Corp Mining Facility at the order of their secret master, Darth Sidious.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: The three-year long Mygeeto Campaign continues with the Fourth Battle of Mygeeto , with Marshal Commander Bacara and the 21st Nova Corps eventually capturing the planet.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: Aayla Secura, Commander Bly , and the 327th Star Corps are deployed to the planet Felucia to capture Shu Mai , Presidente of the Commerce Guild and member of the Separatist Council , and to rescue Ormo Hyn , a relative of a Felucian leader who supported the Republic that had been taken hostage by Shu Mai.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: Plo Koon led the 442nd Siege Battalion in the capture of the Neimoidian Purse World of Cato Neimoidia , headquarters of the Trade Federation .
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: The conquest of Kaller occurs under the leadership of the Jedi Depa Billaba, CC-10/994 "Grey," and Billaba's Padawan, Caleb Dume.

Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: With the end of the Clone Wars in sight, the Jedi Council instructs Anakin Skywalker to confront Palpatine about his emergency powers and term as Chancellor.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: Ahsoka Tano meets with the Jedi Council, but decides not to relay Maul's words about Sidious' plans. Tano is tasked with escorting the captive Maul to Coruscant.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: The duel in Palpatine's office occurs; realizing that Palpatine himself was Darth Sidious but promised the power to save Padmé Amidala, Skywalker disobeys the Jedi Council and sides with the Sith Lord.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: Anakin Skywalker is anointed Darth Vader, Dark Lord of the Sith.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: Per the Supreme Chancellor's command, Order 66 is executed by clones throughout the galaxy, spelling the end of nearly all Jedi.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: Maul, Ahsoka Tano, and Rex escape from the clone forces sent to Mandalore, with the latter two faking their own deaths.
Timeline of galactic history > Republic Era (c. 1000 – 19 BBY) > Fall of the Republic (32–19 BBY) > Clone Wars (22–19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: Snoke , created by Darth Sidious, had memories reaching back to at least this point.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Clone Wars (continued; 19 BBY) :: 19 BBY
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Clone Wars (continued; 19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: Archduke Poggle the Lesser instigates an uprising on Geonosis where Geonosian drones riot, allowing him to escape the custody of the Republic and flee to the Confederacy. The riot also sabotages the top-secret construction project in the orbit of Geonosis.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Clone Wars (continued; 19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: A battle begins on the planet of Lokori between the Bright Nebula Fleet of the Republic Navy and a Separatist invasion force. The Erso family gets caught up in the invasion.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Clone Wars (continued; 19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: The Sith Empire is restored as the Republic is reorganized into the First Galactic Empire by Supreme Chancellor Palpatine—secretly the Dark Lord of the Sith Darth Sidious—who declares himself Galactic Emperor and restrains the powers of the new Imperial Senate .

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Clone Wars (continued; 19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: The mission to Mustafar occurs; Darth Vader wipes out the Separatist leaders on Mustafar and orders the deactivation of the droid armies at the orders of the Emperor, thus ending the Clone Wars.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Clone Wars (continued; 19 BBY) > 19 BBY :: Yoda and Obi-Wan Kenobi infiltrate the Jedi Temple, which had fallen to the 501st Legion during the execution of Order 66 and Operation: Knightfall .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Clone Wars (continued; 19 BBY) > 19 BBY > Yoda and Obi-Wan Kenobi infiltrate the Jedi Temple, which had fallen to the 501st Legion during the execution of Order 66 and Operation: Knightfall . :: Kenobi changes the beacon luring Jedi to the Temple into a message warning them away.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Clone Wars (continued; 19 BBY) > 19 BBY > Yoda and Obi-Wan Kenobi infiltrate the Jedi Temple, which had fallen to the 501st Legion during the execution of Order 66 and Operation: Knightfall . :: The ambush in the Coruscant system occurs, with Caleb Dume receiving Kenobi's message and then retreating back to Kaller.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Clone Wars (continued; 19 BBY) > 19 BBY > Yoda and Obi-Wan Kenobi infiltrate the Jedi Temple, which had fallen to the 501st Legion during the execution of Order 66 and Operation: Knightfall . :: Kenobi and Yoda learn Anakin Skywalker has fallen to the dark side.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) :: 19 BBY (continued)
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 19 BBY (continued) :: Darth Vader fights Obi-Wan Kenobi on the planet Mustafar. Vader is severely wounded, requiring the use of a cybernetic suit of armor for the remainder of his life.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 19 BBY (continued) :: A lightsaber duel is fought between Darth Sidious and Yoda within the heart of the Senate Chamber . Ultimately, Yoda retreats and goes into exile on Dagobah .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 19 BBY (continued) :: Skywalkers Luke and Leia are born to Padmé Amidala on Polis Massa. Amidala dies after childbirth due to heartbreak at her lover's fall to the dark side, though she remains adamant that there is still good in her husband. In order to protect the Skywalker twins from the Sith, Obi-Wan Kenobi leaves Luke with Anakin's stepbrother Owen Lars on Tatooine while Bail Organa adopts Leia into the Alderaanian royal house .

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 19 BBY (continued) :: A funeral for Padmé Amidala is held on Naboo, where she is mourned by thousands of Naboo citizens . Her body is made to still look pregnant in order to keep Luke and Leia safe from Vader.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 19 BBY (continued) :: After the funeral, Bail Organa secretly meets Ahsoka Tano and gives her a line of contact in the case of emergency. Tano then departs with Rex, and the pair soon part ways.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 19 BBY (continued) :: Obi-Wan Kenobi and Yoda go into exile on Tatooine and Dagobah respectively. Kenobi is tasked with ensuring the future of the Jedi by watching over Luke Skywalker, while Yoda uses Dagobah's strength in the Living Force to ponder ancient Jedi texts and the mysteries of the Force, as well as to communicate with other Jedi exiles and monitor the Skywalker twins.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 19 BBY (continued) :: Ezra Bridger is born to Mira and Ephraim Bridger on Lothal.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 19 BBY (continued) :: Rex meets Cut Lawquane on Saleucami, speaking about inhibitor chips.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 19 BBY (continued) :: Ahsoka Tano settles on the world Thabeska with the powerful and wealthy Fardi clan , working for them as a courier and taking on the name "Ashla."
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 19 BBY (continued) :: With the Jedi Order's overseer of Kamino's clone army facilities, Shaak Ti, having been murdered by Darth Vader in the Jedi Temple, Governor Tarkin arrives in Tipoca City to assess the viability of using clones in the New Order.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 19 BBY (continued) :: A public rally is held on Coruscant to celebrate the destruction of the Jedi Order. Led by Grand Vizier Mas Amedda , who presents a speech to a massive audience on the steps of the Jedi Temple, shows the incineration of the Jedi's lightsabers, including Yoda's.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 19 BBY (continued) :: Darth Vader recaptures a starship gifted to him by the Emperor on the planet Gattering , after it was stolen by a group of enslavers and held at their base .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 19 BBY (continued) :: Vader attacks the Jedi Order's space station Brighthome in order to access its archives and discover the location of Jedi Master Kirak Infil'a .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 19 BBY (continued) :: Clone Force 99 is dispatched to end an insurgency on Onderon. However, the clones let go of the insurgents, who were mainly refugees led by Saw Gerrera, and decide to desert the new Empire.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 19 BBY (continued) :: Vader travels to the river moon of Al'doleem , where he finds and kills Jedi Kirak Infil'a. He takes his lightsaber in order to bleed its kyber crystal.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 19 BBY (continued) :: Clone Force 99 returns to Kamino and, with the exception of Crosshair , who remained loyal to the Empire, rescue Omega with the assistance of Kaminoan cloner Nala Se . Wary of the Empire's intentions, the Kaminoans keep secret the clones' defection.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 19 BBY (continued) :: Aboard the Marauder , the clones escape to meet Cut Lawquane on Saleucami. They help the Lawquane's family escape the Empire.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 19 BBY (continued) :: Seeing that the most effective of clone troopers were disloyal to the Empire, Vice Admiral Edmon Rampart 's proposes a new army of loyal, enlisted soldiers, to the dismay of Kaminoan Prime Minister Lama Su . Intrigued, Governor Tarkin trials Rampart's proposal, which is named Project War-Mantle . Elite Squad , led by CT-9904, is thus sent to handle Saw Gerrera's rebellion on Onderon.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 19 BBY (continued) :: The Kaminoans hire bounty hunter Fennec Shand to track down Omega. The Bad Batch and Omega escape Pantora and ask informant Ciddarin Scaleback to identify the hunter on their tail. In return, the clones retrieve the rancor Muchi for Cid, who in turn gives Muchi to Jabba's majordomo , Bib Fortuna , for a payment.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 19 BBY (continued) :: The Bad Batch and the Martez sisters undertake a mission to Corellia at the behest of the information broker Ciddarin Scaleback to steal the head of a tactical droid from a decommissioning facility in Coronet City .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 19 BBY (continued) :: Vader and the Grand Inquisitor are sent by Palpatine to hunt the Jedi Master Jocasta Nu so she could tell him the location of the Kyber memory crystal list of Force-sensitive children. After she reveals the Emperor's plans to Vader, he kills her and destroys the crystal.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 19 BBY (continued) :: The Bad Batch are sent by their informant Ciddarin Scaleback to retrieve a lizard from the Rhokai gang of thieves, who had stolen it from the criminal Roland Durand .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 19 BBY (continued) :: The clones escape from Elite Squad on Bracca, but Omega is captured by Cad Bane, hired by the Kaminoans.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 19 BBY (continued) :: Prime Minister Lama Su sends Taun We to obtain Omega's genetic material and then terminate the clone. However, Nala Se hires Fennec Shand to rescue Omega from Taun We and Cad Bane at the Bora Vio cloning facility . Ultimately, Omega is rescued by the Bad Batch at Bora Vio .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 19 BBY (continued) :: The Batch extract Senator Avi Singh from the Imperial- occupied Raxus Secundus , the former Separatist capital.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 19 BBY (continued) :: On Imperial-occupied Ryloth, Admiral Rampart frames the freedom fighter Cham Syndulla for attempting to assassinate Senator Orn Free Taa , and sends Captain Howzer to pursue Syndulla's daughter, Hera. Together with Howzer and the Bad Batch, Hera and the Ryloth freedom fighters rescue Cham and Eleni Syndulla .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 19 BBY (continued) :: Roland Durand, a former client of Scaleback's, attempting to make it in the criminal underground gets involved with the Pykes , taking over Cid's Parlor in the process. In retalliation, Cid and the Bad Batch steal sansanna spice Durand was keeping in his office, which he owed to the Pyke Syndicate , in order to get him in trouble with them.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 19 BBY (continued) :: Rex sends the Bad Batch to rescue Gregor from an Imperial installation on Daro . While Gregor is rescued, Hunter is captured by the Empire's Clone Commandos and new stormtroopers and brought before Crosshair.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 19 BBY (continued) :: The Empire cancels all its contracts with the Kaminoans and mobilizes its clone forces for departure from Kamino. Fearing their own doom, the Kaminoans attempt to evacuate Kamino with some of their clones as well, but are caught by Rampart's forces. Rampart thus takes away Nala Se for her expertise and deposes Lama Su.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 19 BBY (continued) :: The Bad Batch rescue Hunter and confront Crosshair in Tipoca City. While Crosshair maintains his belief that they must serve the Empire, he turns on the Elite Squad, wishing to join his brothers-in-arms.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 19 BBY (continued) :: As per Tarkin's command, Rampart's forces destroy Tipoca City's cloning facilities and other Kaminoan cities. The Bad Batch flee the city alongside Crosshair, Omega, and AZI-345211896246498721347 .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 19 BBY (continued) :: Secure as Emperor, Darth Sidious begins to delve deep into dark side arcana, including the hunt for immortality. (exact chronology undefined)

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 19 BBY (continued) :: Shortly after the rise of the Empire, entire populations of one species or planet are enslaved as manufacturing worlds such as Riosa of the Inner Rim are annexed and pushed to produce components for Imperial military projects, including the Death Stars . Meanwhile, dissent and resistance emerges with the beginning of the Ryloth Insurgency .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 19 BBY (continued) :: Tarkin Massacre occurs. During a protest in the capital city of Palmo , Moff Wilhuff Tarkin attempted to land his cruiser in a crowded plaza . Despite the presence of peaceful demonstrators attempting to block the landing, Tarkin landed his cruiser on top of the crowd and killed five hundred unarmed civilians.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) :: Between 19 BBY and 18 BBY
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > Between 19 BBY and 18 BBY :: The Bad Batch take a job retrieving a package for Ciddarin Scaleback from the planet Aynaboni , facing a pod of aggrocrabs in the process. They succeed and deliver the package to Scaleback's contact, Phee Genoa .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > Between 19 BBY and 18 BBY :: Amid the Empire's occupation of Serenno , the Bad Batch launch a mission attempting to steal part of the late Count Dooku's war chest from Castle Serenno before the Empire could confiscate the wealth for itself.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > Between 19 BBY and 18 BBY :: The Empire dispatches forces, led by Commander Cody and his squad , to the former Separatist planet Desix to rescue Grotton , the Imperial-appointed governor of the planet, from the Separatist holdout commanded by the original governor Tawni Ames .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > Between 19 BBY and 18 BBY :: The Safa Toma Classic , a major Riot Racing event is held on the planet Serolonis . The Dowutin gangster Grini Millegi oversees the event, and personally backs the Nosaurian racer Jet Venim . After Cid loses a bet with Millegi and he holds her as ransom, Tech runs in and wins the race, winning a bet that releases Cid.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > Between 19 BBY and 18 BBY :: Clone Force 99 and the pirate Phee Genoa discover an ancient superweapon walker , long believed to be a city built by the Ancients containing a valuable crystal called the Heart of the Mountain .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > Between 19 BBY and 18 BBY :: Kashyyyk's Wookiees face persecution by Trandoshan hunters who are aided by Imperial clones. The Bad Batch safely return the Wookiee Jedi Gungi to Kashyyyk.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) :: 18 BBY
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 18 BBY :: Rampart is exposed for the ruin of Kamino in the Imperial Senate and is arrested, but senators nevertheless approve his Imperial Defense Recruitment Bill at the Emperor's persuasion, formalizing the transition from a clone military into one made up of stormtrooper recruits.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) :: During or after 18 BBY
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY :: Echo leaves the Bad Batch to join Rex in secretly supporting fellow clone troopers facing decommissioning.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY :: The Bad Batch’s ship gets stolen on Ipsidon whilst they are on a mining mission for Cid, but they steal it back and commence the liberation of Mokkotown .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY :: Cid sends the Bad Batch on a job to get valuable cargo from a crashed transport. It turns out to be an Imperial science vessel , and the Batch encounters a cloned Zillo Beast that terrorizes a village for energy to grow before being abducted by imperial forces and taken to Mount Tantiss .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY :: Imperial chief scientist Dr. Royce Hemlock and his assistant Dr. Emerie Karr arrive to take over the secret work on Mt. Tantiss, where Hemlock visits with the imprisoned Kaminoan scientist Nala Se, and requests Lama Su to come to the base. He tells Hemlock that Se’s weakness is her attachment to Omega.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY :: Crosshair is assigned to an outpost on Barton IV , where he meets clone Mayday . They thwart raiders’ attempts to steal TK trooper armor, but get badly injured in an avalanche and snowstorm. Crosshair kills Lieutenant Nolan , his commanding officer, when he refuses to help Mayday, then he is taken to Mt Tantiss.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY :: Clone force 99 helps as Phee Genoa muscle during with an artifact trade. She then takes them to Pabu, a peaceful ocean world where the Batch helps town get to high ground during a Tsunami, and is tempted to settle down on the planet to give Omega a normal life.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY :: Auction on Hosnian Prime .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY :: Skirmish on Dallow .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY :: Mission to Tryth .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY :: Echo and members of the Clone Underground attack an Imperial prisoner transport ship en route through the Balmorra system to the planet Wayland, in order to liberate Howzer and two surviving members of Howzer's squad , who had defied the Empire at Ryloth. Echo extracts portions of Advanced Science Division data before they destroy the ship and flee, with Imperial Captain Pearce dying in the process, along with most of his crew.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY :: The Galactic Empire's top strategists and scientists gather on the planet Eriadu to discuss their plans for the future of the Empire. The summit take place in Governor Wilhuff Tarkin 's fortified compound, Raven's Peak , and attendees also include Admiral Barton Coburn , Chief Scientist Royce Hemlock , Commander Orson Callan Krennic , and General Hurst Romodi .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY :: Clone Force 99 and Saw Gerrera's Partisans independently infiltrate Raven's Peak, the bad batch seeking to locate Doctor Hemlock in order to find and rescue Crosshair, and the partisans seeking to assassinate Governor Tarkin.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY :: Capture of Omega .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY :: Search for Omega .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY :: Escape from Tantiss .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY :: Cargo Dock Clash .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY :: Two senators, Riyo Chuchi of the moon Pantora , and Avi Singh of the planet Raxus , meet at a tea parlor on Pantora, where they discuss forming an alliance to oppose the Galactic Empire despite having been on opposite sides of the Clone Wars. The meeting is infiltrated by the Clone X trooper CX-1 , who is attempting to assassinate the senators. However, Rex and his Clone Underground foil the plot and capture CX-1.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY :: A operative of the Clone X trooper division , tracking a fellow operative captured by the Clone Underground, discovers the Underground headquarters in the B'omarr Order Monastery on the planet Teth .He infiltrates the monastery and calls for a Recovery Strike Team to reinforce him after identifying the presence of Omega in the compound. In the ensuing firefight the assassin kills many insurgents and forces the remaining clones to flee into the jungle, where they are cornered by the Strike Team. Despite Commander Wolffe allowing the clones to escape, the battle marks the fragmentation of the Clone Underground movement and the destruction of their primary headquarters.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY :: Hunt for Sylar Saris

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY :: The Empire raids the island Pabu to retrieve Omega. After Clone X trooper CX-2 tracks her down by interrogating her former associate Ciddarin Scaleback and stealing the island's coordinates from Phee Genoa's starship , CX-2 finds Omega, destroys Clone Force 99's shuttle the Marauder , and calls for reinforcements. The forces that arrive lock down the island by destroying any starships and sailing ships in the docks and begin searching the city's houses one by one. After Hunter fail to commandeer an Imperial Low Altitude Assault Transport to escape the blockade, Omega is forced to surrender to the Imperials in hopes that her allies can use her to track down Tantiss Base 's coordinates and rescue her. Following Omega's capture, all Imperial troops withdraw from the planet.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY :: Rescue of Edmon Rampart .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY :: Mission to Imperial Station 003 .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY :: Skirmish on Wayland .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY :: Rex would eventually join fellow clones Wolffe and Gregor on Seelos .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY :: Queen Apailana's reign ends, after she refuses to become a puppet of Emperor Palpatine, placing her duty to her people first.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY :: Following the defeat of the Nikardun Destiny and General Yiv , the Chiss Ascendancy undertakes a military effort to seek out and destroy the scattered Nikardun forces. Though the Ascendancy typically does not permit military action without having been attacked first, the threat that the Nikardun has posed leads the Chiss Syndicure to allow the defense forces to bend the protocols regarding preemptive strikes .

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY > Following the defeat of the Nikardun Destiny and General Yiv , the Chiss Ascendancy undertakes a military effort to seek out and destroy the scattered Nikardun forces.
