Though the Ascendancy typically does not permit military action without having been attacked first, the threat that the Nikardun has posed leads the Chiss Syndicure to allow the defense forces to bend the protocols regarding preemptive strikes . :: A Commander of the Nikardun Destiny military forces, who had blockaded and occupied the planet Rapacc under orders of General Yiv , is defeated by Commander Uingali foar Marocsaa of the Paccian Governance , and the blockade is lifted .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY > Following the defeat of the Nikardun Destiny and General Yiv , the Chiss Ascendancy undertakes a military effort to seek out and destroy the scattered Nikardun forces. Though the Ascendancy typically does not permit military action without having been attacked first, the threat that the Nikardun has posed leads the Chiss Syndicure to allow the defense forces to bend the protocols regarding preemptive strikes . :: Picket Force Six of the Chiss Expansionary Defense Fleet undertakes a mission to eliminate any remaining Nikardun military presence at the Vak Combine territory. Admiral Ar'alani and Senior Captains Thrawn and Lakinda believe the Ascendancy has nearly eliminated the Nikardun from the region, as they have cleared out most of the Nikardun bases listed on General Yiv's questis . After defeating the Nikardun forces in an unidentified system, Ar'alani gives Thrawn and his Springhawk permission to undertake his next mission . While the Springhawk travels to Rapacc, Ar'alani's Vigilant and Lakinda's Grayshrike investigate listening post KR20 acquired from interrogating Yiv. To their surprise, they find all Nikardun forces already destroyed by weaponized asteroids that double as missile launchers. They visit the last Nikardun base, Listening post KR21 , and investigate it but find that it as well is destroyed.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY > Following the defeat of the Nikardun Destiny and General Yiv , the Chiss Ascendancy undertakes a military effort to seek out and destroy the scattered Nikardun forces. Though the Ascendancy typically does not permit military action without having been attacked first, the threat that the Nikardun has posed leads the Chiss Syndicure to allow the defense forces to bend the protocols regarding preemptive strikes . :: Thrawn travels to the planet Rapacc , homeworld of the Paccosh species, to investigate the remnants of the Nikardun. He and Caregiver Thalias encounter their old friend Uingali foar Marocsaa , who tells them of a group of 200 refugees who have settled on Rapacc. Thrawn and Thalias meett the refugees and their leader, known only as the Magys , who seeks suicide for her people so they can join " The Great Beyond ." During the mission, Thrawn returns the Marocsaa subclan ring bearing a chimaera that Uingali had entrusted him with. Thrawn and Thalias also see a chimaera painted on the hull of a Nikardun blockade frigate that Uingali has captured.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY > Following the defeat of the Nikardun Destiny and General Yiv , the Chiss Ascendancy undertakes a military effort to seek out and destroy the scattered Nikardun forces. Though the Ascendancy typically does not permit military action without having been attacked first, the threat that the Nikardun has posed leads the Chiss Syndicure to allow the defense forces to bend the protocols regarding preemptive strikes . :: The Springhawk is attacked above the planet " Sunrise " by three formations of five missile boats. Soon thereafter, the Grayshrike , commanded by Lakinda arrives in the system to deliver a message from Admiral Ar'alani to Thrawn, and Lakinda orders her Grayshrike to join the battle to protect the Springhawk . Both Chiss warships are attacked by the HopeBreaker apparently accompanied by a repair ship which turns out to be a freighter. The Battle Dreadnought keeps the Chiss forces occupied while the freighter escapes to hyperspace. Thrawn disables or destroyed all of the missile boats as well as the armaments and sensors on the Battle Dreadnought's port side. After the freighter escapes into hyperspace, the Battle Dreadnought also flees, but first destroys all the intact missile boats so the Chiss can not learn anything about their design or schematics.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY > Following the defeat of the Nikardun Destiny and General Yiv , the Chiss Ascendancy undertakes a military effort to seek out and destroy the scattered Nikardun forces. Though the Ascendancy typically does not permit military action without having been attacked first, the threat that the Nikardun has posed leads the Chiss Syndicure to allow the defense forces to bend the protocols regarding preemptive strikes . :: A skirmish is fought above the planet Nettehi , homeworld of the Paataatus , between the Chiss Ascendancy and the Nikardun Destiny. It initially appears that the native Paataatus will attack the inbound forces of the Chiss Expansionary Defense Fleet , but the Paataatus military ally with the Chiss Ascendancy against the remnants of the forces of General Yiv. The Paataatus were subjugated by the Nikardun , and the skirmish above Nettehi frees them from Nikardun occupation.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY :: " Jixtus ", a Grysk agent of the Grysk Hegemony and coordinator of Grysk military , begins his plan to destroy the Chiss Ascendancy.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY > " Jixtus ", a Grysk agent of the Grysk Hegemony and coordinator of Grysk military , begins his plan to destroy the Chiss Ascendancy. :: Members of the telepathic Agbui species undertake an attempt to precipitate a civil war in the Chiss Ascendancy, on behalf of the Grysk Hegemony.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY > " Jixtus ", a Grysk agent of the Grysk Hegemony and coordinator of Grysk military , begins his plan to destroy the Chiss Ascendancy. > Members of the telepathic Agbui species undertake an attempt to precipitate a civil war in the Chiss Ascendancy, on behalf of the Grysk Hegemony. :: The Vigilant and the Grayshrike come across an asteroid missile platform in a nearly-circular orbit above Sunrise which was not there two weeks prior., and encounter the same Unidentified Battle Dreadnought from the first skirmish over Sunrise , bearing the damage inflicted to its port side weapons and sensor installments. The Battle Dreadnought's commander demands the Chiss leave the area, and a skirmish ensues using breachers , dibbers , plasma spheres , spectrum lasers , and the asteroid missile platform. The asteroid missile platform's outer shell detonates when it believes it comes under attack. Ar'alani keeps the Battle Dreadnought occupied with and leads the enemy commander to believe the Grayshrike destroyed by the asteroid missile launcher. Ar'alani damages the Battle Dreadnought from the Starboard flank, and Lakinda eventually attacks the already-damaged port flank. Ar'alani attempts to gain information from the damaged enemy, at which point he launches a missile that destroys the asteroid missile platform and initiates self-destruct , destroying his Battle Dreadnought.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY > " Jixtus ", a Grysk agent of the Grysk Hegemony and coordinator of Grysk military , begins his plan to destroy the Chiss Ascendancy. > Members of the telepathic Agbui species undertake an attempt to precipitate a civil war in the Chiss Ascendancy, on behalf of the Grysk Hegemony. :: A skirmish is fought between Chiss forces and the Watith crew of a freighter, whose Captain Fsir has been hired by Jixtus to destroy or delay Thrawn so he can not prevent a battle between Chiss Great Families over a supposedly valuable mining world . While exploring leads related to the Nikardun campaigns, Thrawn encounters a distress call from a freighter identifying itself as the Saltbarrel , whose captain claims to be under attack by Vagaari pirates, being attacked by three gunboats. Thrawn notes, however, that little damage is actually being done to the Saltbarrel and suspects that the attack is a trap, so he boards the Watith freighter and find remote-piloting systems aboard, as the Watith crew has been piloting the Watith gunboats . Upon returning to the Springhawk , Thrawn orders his crew to fire to disable the Saltbarrel , and they capture the ship, fourteen Watith gunboats, and the Watith crew.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY > " Jixtus ", a Grysk agent of the Grysk Hegemony and coordinator of Grysk military , begins his plan to destroy the Chiss Ascendancy. > Members of the telepathic Agbui species undertake an attempt to precipitate a civil war in the Chiss Ascendancy, on behalf of the Grysk Hegemony. :: A battle is fought above the planet Hoxim between Chiss forces as a result of the machinations by Jixtus. The culmination of Jixtus' plot to destroy the Chiss Ascendancy was to draw rival Chiss Great Families , each allied with a different Ruling Family , into open conflict; which would spread to the Ruling Families and then the entire Ascendancy. The skirmish nearly accomplishes this, but the actions of Thrawn and Lakinda of Picket Force Six prevent such an outbreak. Agents of Jixtus manipulate ambitious officials from the three rival Great Families of Xodlak , Pommrio , and Erighal into gathering their family fleets in an open confrontation to lay claim to the supposedly nyix -rich mining world of Hoxim . Hostilities nearly break out between the forces of the Great Families above the Agbui mining world, until Lakinda and Thrawn are able to convince the Great Families' fleets to work together against a supposed threat, which is in reality a force of remote-controlled gunboats controlled by Xodlak, Pommrio, and Erighal family members aboard Thrawn's Springhawk . Thrawn has his warriors and officers put on a light show , targeting their families' vessels with "maximum ferocity but minimal actual damage". At the end of the skirmish, Thrawn has his sky-walker navigator Che'ri use her powers of Third Sight to guide the hands of weapons officer Afpriuh . Che'ri and Afpriuh aim at the commandeered Watith freighter at the exact angle needed to destroy the false Agbui nyix mine, and make it look accidental. As a result, the Great Families can not fight over the fake nyix deposit, believing it destroyed.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY > " Jixtus ", a Grysk agent of the Grysk Hegemony and coordinator of Grysk military , begins his plan to destroy the Chiss Ascendancy. :: Grysk plot to engineer a Chiss civil war .

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY > " Jixtus ", a Grysk agent of the Grysk Hegemony and coordinator of Grysk military , begins his plan to destroy the Chiss Ascendancy. > Grysk plot to engineer a Chiss civil war . :: The armed freighters of the Chiss Tahmie and Droc families exchange fire at Csaus inside the Ascendancy, before Chiss Defense Force Admiral Ers'ikaro 's Firewolf man-of-war Bellicose stops the battle in order to prevent further escalation of the crisis.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY > " Jixtus ", a Grysk agent of the Grysk Hegemony and coordinator of Grysk military , begins his plan to destroy the Chiss Ascendancy. > Grysk plot to engineer a Chiss civil war . :: A skirmish takes place over the Ascendancy planet Jamiron between the Tahmie and Droc families, soon after the two families' previous engagement over Csaus. The Chiss Syndicure dispatches the Nightdragon man-of-war Vigilant , under the command of Admiral Ar'alani , to resolve the situation. When the warship reaches Jamiron, eighteen starships belonging to both families are engaged in battle . After the families ignore the admiral's orders to cease hostilities, Ar'alani's vessel fird plasma spheres , weapons that disable vessels, at all the combatants. After all eighteen Tahmie and Droc ships are neutralized, the Vigilant uses its tractor beams to haul the disabled ships away from each other. After the skirmish, Ar'alani meet with Supreme Admiral Ja'fosk of the Ascendancy's Defense Hierarchy Council , who informs her the Syndicure is infuriated by her intervention as it has humiliated both families.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY > " Jixtus ", a Grysk agent of the Grysk Hegemony and coordinator of Grysk military , begins his plan to destroy the Chiss Ascendancy. > Grysk plot to engineer a Chiss civil war . :: A standoff is fought between freighters of the Chiss Chaf and Ufsa families at the Ascendancy planet Sarvchi .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY > " Jixtus ", a Grysk agent of the Grysk Hegemony and coordinator of Grysk military , begins his plan to destroy the Chiss Ascendancy. > Grysk plot to engineer a Chiss civil war . :: A transport of the Erighal family and a patrol ship of the Boadil family fight above the planet Shihon .

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY > " Jixtus ", a Grysk agent of the Grysk Hegemony and coordinator of Grysk military , begins his plan to destroy the Chiss Ascendancy. > Grysk plot to engineer a Chiss civil war . :: At the Ascendancy planet Ornfra , the Orisson , a light cruiser of the Clarr family commanded by Captain Clarr'os'culry , engages at least two destroyers of the Dasklo family , before the heavy cruiser Grayshrike of the Chiss Expansionary Defense Fleet intervenes.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY > " Jixtus ", a Grysk agent of the Grysk Hegemony and coordinator of Grysk military , begins his plan to destroy the Chiss Ascendancy. > Grysk plot to engineer a Chiss civil war . :: Jixtus, who has allied himself with the Kilji Illumine nation, sends General Crofyp 's task force to the Rapacc system to capture a group of refugees hiding on the planet Rapacc . The general's task force, consisting of Crofyp's Kilji war cruiser Anvil and Colonel Tildnis 's Kilji picket cruiser Hammer , carry out an attack , confronting the Paccosh -commandeered Nikardun blockade frigate Aelos . Crofyp instructs the blockade frigate's captain, Uingali foar Marocsaa , to move aside and allow the Kiljis to capture the refugees they have come for; however, Marocsaa refuses, prompting the general to prepare a cross fire attack on the Aelos . The Anvil and the Hammer move along the frigate's starboard and portside respectively, and once they are in position, the Springhawk arrives at the outer system, firing two plasma sphere weapons at Crofyp's flagship before joining the confrontation in the inner system. Thrawn instructs Crofyp to move clear of the Aelos , angering the general, who fires spectrum laser salvos at the Springhawk . Shortly after, the plasma spheres slamm into the Anvil ' s starboard while the war cruiser shields the Hammer so that the picket cruiser can escape. Thrawn allowes the Hammer to escape and destroys Crofyp's flagship with breacher missiles.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY > " Jixtus ", a Grysk agent of the Grysk Hegemony and coordinator of Grysk military , begins his plan to destroy the Chiss Ascendancy. > Grysk plot to engineer a Chiss civil war . :: Jixtus has several falsified patrol ships, supposedly from the Erighal and Xodlak families , attack a Clarr family convoy carrying valuable technology at the Ascendancy planet of Jamiron . Though the Clarr freighters are highly damaged, their escort cruiser, the Orisson , commanded by Captain Roscu , as well as local Clarr patrol ships, fight back. The heavy cruiser Grayshrike , under the command of Senior Captain Ziinda which has been posted to defend at Jamiron, also step in to assist in the battle. After two of the false patrol ships are destroyed, the others self-destruct, leaving nothing intact to be captured. However, from analyzing the wreckage, both Ziinda and Roscu determine the patrol ships to be fake, though the latter assumes that the rival Dasklo family is responsible.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY > " Jixtus ", a Grysk agent of the Grysk Hegemony and coordinator of Grysk military , begins his plan to destroy the Chiss Ascendancy. > Grysk plot to engineer a Chiss civil war . :: After the attack on Rapacc , the Kilji picket cruiser Hammer flees, and travels to the Sunrise system according to General Crofyp and Jixtus's instructions, where keeps station with one of Jixtus's warships. The Paccosh-commandeered Aelos and its complement of Paccian commandos arrive shortly, and request permission from the Grysk vessel to dock with the Hammer , which is granted through comm. Docking with the picket cruiser, the blockade frigate is able to board the Kilji warship and kill all aboard , including the ship's commander, Colonel Tildnis . As the Chiss heavy cruiser Springhawk arrives at the system seeking the capture of Thrawn, who is aboard the Aelos , the Grysk warship fires at it. However, after Thrawn retrieves data on the Illumine Kilhorde , the Kiljis' battle fleet, the Grysk are forced to concentrate their laser fire on the rogue Hammer . Shortly after the remaining individuals—the Hammer ' s navigator Sarsh , Thrawn, Paccosh Commander Uingali foar Marocsaa and his Paccian commandos —aboard the Hammer flee aboard the Aelos and Bogey One, as the Springhawk has codenamed the Grysk vessel, destroy the Hammer and jump to hyperspace.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY > " Jixtus ", a Grysk agent of the Grysk Hegemony and coordinator of Grysk military , begins his plan to destroy the Chiss Ascendancy. > Grysk plot to engineer a Chiss civil war . :: The Paataatus Hiveborn nation carries out an attack on the Illumine Kilhorde, after the fleet conquered several small nations to the north and northwest of the Hiveborn's territory as part of the Illumine's conquests of enlightenment . with data on the Kilhorde's design and battle doctrine retrieved from Tildnis' warship. The Hiveborn obliterate the Kilji battle fleet and free the nations the Illumine's forces had conquered. The destruction of the Illumine's battle fleet prompts Jixtus, who had allied himself with the Illumine's ruler, Generalirius Nakirre , to murder him, as the Kilhorde's destruction renders Nakirre useless to his ally.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY > " Jixtus ", a Grysk agent of the Grysk Hegemony and coordinator of Grysk military , begins his plan to destroy the Chiss Ascendancy. > Grysk plot to engineer a Chiss civil war . :: A battle is fought between a Chiss Ascendancy fleet led by Thrawn and Jixtus's fleet . As Jixtus attempts to instigate a civil war in the Ascendancy, Thrawn realizes that if the Grysk's fleet is not destroyed, its commander will be able to use it to destroy the Ascendancy. Thrawn and his colleagues form a large fleet and bring it to the Sunrise system, where they deactivate it, making it seem as if it is destroyed. Once Jixtus's fleet and the Kilji war cruiser Whetstone arrive at Sunrise, Jixtus's Grysk warships move toward the Chiss; however, that separates the vessels and surrounds them with enemy ships that "revive" and attack the Grysks shortly afterward. As the Grysk StoneCrusher -class WarMasters and Prism -class BattleChiefs are destroyed by Thrawn's forces, fifteen starships of the Garwian Unity and Vak Combine nations arrive and attack the Grysk forces, destroying many of Jixtus's warships. His flagship, the Shatter -class WarMaster , then tries to flee. As the FateSpinner can destroy the Whetstone before fleeing so that there are no non-Chiss witnesses to the Sunrise confrontation, the war cruiser's navigator, Pathfinder Qilori of Uandualon , instructs the cruiser's crew—the Kilji vassals of the Kilji Illumine nation—to destroy the WarMaster before it can kill him. The vassals then fire all of the vessel's weaponry at the Fatespinner , destroying the Grysk warship's starboard aft flank. The Kilji attack prompts Jixtus to activate the self-destruct mechanisms aboard his seven remaining vessels, destroying all of them but his flagship. After telling Thrawn that the Grysks will return, the Grysk destroys the FateSpinner , killing himself.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY > " Jixtus ", a Grysk agent of the Grysk Hegemony and coordinator of Grysk military , begins his plan to destroy the Chiss Ascendancy. > Grysk plot to engineer a Chiss civil war . :: As Thrawn violated Chiss law that prohibits fighting if an enemy has not attacked the Ascendancy, the Syndicure remove him from the Expansionary Defense Fleet and turns him into a member of his family, the Mitth family , without rank. Although the Syndicure seeks to punish everyone who fought at Sunrise, they only press charges against Thrawn, who is exiled to a world in Wild Space . Supreme General of the Chiss Expansionary Defense Fleet Ba'kif and Thrawn view the exile as a way to send the senior captain to the region beyond the Unknown Regions known to the Chiss as " Lesser Space ." Thrawn is to contact the Galactic Empire and determine if the government can help the Ascendancy fight the Grysks.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY :: The Graf family is pursued by the Empire. Auric Graf and his wife Rhyssa are arrested by Captain Visler Aqorian Korda III of the Empire, forcing their two children, Lina and Milo , to escape on their own.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY :: Caleb Dume is rescued from capture by clone troopers on the planet Lahn by Kalleran smuggler Janus Kasmir and former Separatist general Kleeve . Caleb changes his name to Kanan Jarrus and begins a new life.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY :: Darth Vader eliminates a Geonosian queen and her group of Geonosians who were attempting to sabotage the Empire's Death Star project . Vader then inspects the Death Star plans on Scarif without neither Tarkin nor the Emperor's permission.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY :: Imperial forces, led by Wilhuff Tarkin , carry out arrests, executions, and massacres on Antar 4 , as punishment for Antar 4's allegiance to the Confederacy during the Clone Wars, in what the media would call the Antar Atrocity .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY :: A multi-week campaign is fought at the Salient system . Moff Tarkin leads an Imperial invasion force, and are opposed by Saw Gerrera 's Partisans and the rebeling factions on Salient's worlds, Salient I and Salient II .

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY :: Former Padawan Ferren Barr begins influencing the King of the planet Mon Cala , Lee-Char , in negotiations with the Galactic Empire. When the Emperor recognizes these negotiation techniques, Vader is dispatched alongside three Inquisitors and a death squad of Purge Troopers to investigate the possible Jedi. Shortly after Lord Vader's arrival, Imperial Ambassador Telvar is assassinated by Barr leading to Governor Wilhuff Tarkin , aboard the Imperial I -class Star Destroyer Sovereign , sending forces down to invade Mon Cala.

However, they are interrupted by a tsunami created by a school of large fish-like creatures which wipes out the Imperial forces on the surface of Mon Cala.

The Mon Calamari Gial Ackbar leads an assault on the platform and destroys it with Bergon aboard.

Barr, though, uses a mind trick to turn the Purge troopers against the Inquisitors, allowing the Padawan to escape..

Barr soon arrives and fights Vader. When the Jedi reveals he deliberately kick-started the war, the King panicks and orders a cease-fire of his forces, although the governor continues it for a while. Vader then kills Barr and arrests Lee-Char. At the same time, Raddus escapes with a handful of his merchant ships.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY > Former Padawan Ferren Barr begins influencing the King of the planet Mon Cala , Lee-Char , in negotiations with the Galactic Empire. The Empire proceeds to subjugate Mon Cala. The Mon Calamari, however, launch a mass exodus aboard spaceworthy structures disguised as city buildings.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY :: By this point, the first Empire Day had occurred, marking exactly one year after the initiation of the Jedi Purge, the end of the Clone Wars, and the founding of the Galactic Empire.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY :: The Empire begins a large terraforming project on Ilum after finally claiming it; the planet's core is exposed and exploited by Star Destroyers for kyber crystals.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY :: An uprising takes place against the Empire on the agricultural moon of Raada . In response to Imperial restrictions and the introduction of soil-destroying crops, the local farmers organize a resistance movement under the leadership and tutelage of Ahsoka Tano . Tano's plans go awry when the farmers Kaeden Larte and Hoban launch an unsanctioned attack on the Imperial administrative building, where several fighters including Hoban are killed by the Imperial garrison. Tano uses her Force powers to save the rebels but exposes herself to both the Empire and the Jedi sympathizer Senator Bail Organa , who is organizing a rebel movement . Tano and the surviving rebels flee to a network of cave hideouts near the moon's main settlement. After rescuing Kaeden from Imperial captivity, Tano goes into hiding offworld at Kaeden's advice. However, the Empire send the Inquisitor known as the Sixth Brother to hunt Tano. The Sixth Brother discovers the rebel hideout, captures Kaeden, and kills most of the rebels except Kaeden's younger sister Miara Larte . At the request of Senator Organa, Tano returns to Raada to investigate the Sixth Brother. After killing the Sixth Brother, Tano manages to rescue Kaeden again and takes part in helping to evacuate the moon's population with the help of Senator Organa's rebel forces.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY :: At this point, the Empire continues to deploy Clone Wars-era prefab buildings and walkers of the Republic's military.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY :: Bail Organa and Ahsoka Tano organize a rebel network .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY :: The Western Reaches pacification operations begin in response to rebellion from remaining Separatist holdouts .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY :: Darth Vader and Governor Ahr lead Imperial attacks on insurgents with a Venator -class Star Destroyer and its complement of V-wings . Vader is ordered to follow Ahr's orders to the letter by Emperor Palpatine to teach him a lesson.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY > Darth Vader and Governor Ahr lead Imperial attacks on insurgents with a Venator -class Star Destroyer and its complement of V-wings . Vader is ordered to follow Ahr's orders to the letter by Emperor Palpatine to teach him a lesson. :: The Imperials attack the Mid Rim world of Namzor , with the aim to secure coaxium supplies and to destroy the insurgents near the area. Ahr sends out V-wing starfighters to annihilate them, resulting in most of the insurgents dying. As Ahr sees the coaxium supplies he recalls all of his fighters, though Vader in his starship attacks, causing the destruction of the coaxium that the Moff wanted.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY > Darth Vader and Governor Ahr lead Imperial attacks on insurgents with a Venator -class Star Destroyer and its complement of V-wings . Vader is ordered to follow Ahr's orders to the letter by Emperor Palpatine to teach him a lesson. :: Governor Ahr leads the Imperial forces to attack the Mid Rim planet of Ahn Krantarium , in order to destroy deadly droids that had overrun the world long before the Imperial Era, and that had rebuilt themselves to be more dangerous than regular security droids. Ahr sends out a stormtrooper patrol, but all are killed, so instead of sending out more to die for nothing, the Governor sends Vader alone. He survives and destroys all of the droids in the vicinity.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY > Darth Vader and Governor Ahr lead Imperial attacks on insurgents with a Venator -class Star Destroyer and its complement of V-wings . Vader is ordered to follow Ahr's orders to the letter by Emperor Palpatine to teach him a lesson. :: The Imperial forces launch an attack against insurgents based on the moon Phelzepham . As Vader and the stormtrooper detachment he leads push their numerical advantage and are on the verge of victory, Ahr decides to test his hold over Vader by ordering the stormtroopers to withdraw. Although he is suddenly left alone, Vader completes the mission and defeats the insurgents.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY > Darth Vader and Governor Ahr lead Imperial attacks on insurgents with a Venator -class Star Destroyer and its complement of V-wings . Vader is ordered to follow Ahr's orders to the letter by Emperor Palpatine to teach him a lesson. :: The governor decides to head to the Kankalo Belt Containment Zone despite the protests of a subordinate of his. He sends out Vader in his starship to destroy the greatest threat there, the Kankalo Belt creature . Since Vader sees Ahr as his biggest threat, when he sees the creature, he turns back toward the Star Destroyer, with the creature following him, to eliminate the Moff. When Vader returns, Ahr is angry with him and orders that he kneel before him as the Emperor commanded. When Vader kneels, the creature claws into the Star Destroyer's bridge to grab Ahr away, killing him and multiple other personnel in the vacuum of space. The Emperor then senses the governor's death and sees that Vader has learned his lesson.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > During or after 18 BBY :: Mon Mothma begins to organize military and intelligence movements for an anti-Empire coalition known then as the "Alliance" .

Declaring himself Grand Protector of the planet, he switches the planet's capital to his home city, which is also the seat of his clan.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 14 BBY :: By this point, the mother of the individual who came to be known as TK-462 had passed away. (Approximate date)
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 14 BBY :: Several members of the Free Ryloth Movement led by Pok undertake a mission to hijack an Imperial weapons shipment at the Yaga Minor shipyards . They are all killed by Vader, and Cham Syndulla and Isval vow to accelerate their rebellion against the Empire.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 14 BBY :: Darth Sidious and Darth Vader undertake a mission to quell the growing rebellion on Ryloth, and to root out a traitor within the Imperial ranks.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 14 BBY > Darth Sidious and Darth Vader undertake a mission to quell the growing rebellion on Ryloth, and to root out a traitor within the Imperial ranks. :: The Free Ryloth Movement led by Cham Syndulla attempts to assassinate the two Sith Lords.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 14 BBY > Darth Sidious and Darth Vader undertake a mission to quell the growing rebellion on Ryloth, and to root out a traitor within the Imperial ranks. :: Deaths of Belkor Dray and Isval .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 14 BBY :: Former Galactic Republic Intelligence officer Berch Teller leads a rebel campaign against the Galactic Empire.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 14 BBY > Former Galactic Republic Intelligence officer Berch Teller leads a rebel campaign against the Galactic Empire. :: Berch Teller's rebel cell launches an attack against the Empire's Sentinel Base . Using communications equipment supplied by Vice Admiral Dodd Rancit , the rebels send a fake holotransmission claiming that Rampart Station is under attack. Tarkin detect the message as a fraud and sends his Venator -class Star Destroyer , Electrum on a microjump to the edge of the system. Shortly after, a modified Providence -class Dreadnought enters the system . Tarkin then deploys the Venator and orders it to ambush the intruders. The Providence cruiser retreats to hyperspace shortly afterward.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 14 BBY > Former Galactic Republic Intelligence officer Berch Teller leads a rebel campaign against the Galactic Empire. :: Vader and Tarkin travel to the planet Murkhana , where a cache of communications equipment has been discovered, which is in fact bait meant to lure the pair to the world so that a Berch Teller's cell can steal Tarkin's personal starship, the Carrion Spike . Upon reaching Murkhana, and accompanied by a dozen stormtroopers, they retrieve the equipment, which is then left to the troopers to document and transport to the Carrion Spike . Vader and Tarkin visit the Murkhana City Imperial compound , where they meet with the planet's Imperial ambassador and the Koorivar Intelligence asset who has discovered the cache. While the meeting is taking place, the rebel cell creates a false communication to the troopers in which they impersonate Tarkin and tell the Imperials to return to the cache. The cell then boards the Carrion Spike and kills its crew before stealing it. Tarkin and Vader are alerted to the theft, and visit a local Sugi crime lord named Faazah , who they blackmail into giving them a ship, the Parsec Predator . However, the cell manages to escape the Murkhana system and the Imperials.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 14 BBY > Former Galactic Republic Intelligence officer Berch Teller leads a rebel campaign against the Galactic Empire. :: After stealing the Carrion Spike from Tarkin, Teller's cell attacks an Imperial space station orbiting the planet Galidraan III in the Galidraan system . The starship manages to cause large amounts of damage to the station, and the stations defenses have trouble returning fire. Tarkin and Vader arrive in the system on board the Parsec Predator and proceeded to try and attack the Carrion Spike alongside starfighters from the station. The corvette is damaged by the Predator , but manages to disable the Imperial's vessel before escaping the system.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 14 BBY > Former Galactic Republic Intelligence officer Berch Teller leads a rebel campaign against the Galactic Empire. :: Berch Teller and his cell attack TaggeCo's ore mining facility on Lucazec , while Tarkin is chasing the Carrion Spike through hyperspace. Extensive damage is made to the mining facility and several V-wings and ore haulers are destroyed. The dissidents then broadcast their attack on a holovid that is circulated throughout the Imperial HoloNet.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 14 BBY > Former Galactic Republic Intelligence officer Berch Teller leads a rebel campaign against the Galactic Empire. :: A battle takes place in the Phindar system between Berch Teller's cell and Imperial forces under Tarkin and Vader. The Imperials intercept the stolen Carrion Spike as is docking with a tanker to refuel above Phindar . Vader and Tarkin's Yellow Squadron of V-wing starfighters attack the Carrion Spike , and the tanker dispatches Aggressive ReConnaissance-170 starfighters to intercept Tarkin's starfighters. Before the Imperials can fire at the Carrion Spike , the ship flees into hyperspace. The tanker is then destroyed by a rogue fuel cell , which destroys several V-wings. .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 14 BBY > Former Galactic Republic Intelligence officer Berch Teller leads a rebel campaign against the Galactic Empire. :: Berch Teller transmits disinformation that the Carrion Spike is traveling to the Obroa-skai system . Knotts , one of Teller's dissidents, hires the Koorivar captain of the YT-1000 freighter Reticent to travel to the Obroa-skai system as a decoy. Acting on this information, Tarkin and Vader deploy three Imperial interdictor vessels into the Obroa-skai system, hoping to recover the stolen Carrion Spike . However, the Immobilizer 418's gravity well projector malfunctions and yanks several civilian ships out of hyperspace, including the luxury MC80 Star Cruiser Stellar Vista , the smuggling vessel Truant , and the Reticent . The Stellar Vista collides with a Detainer CC-2200 , damaging both ships and causing the deaths of 1,100 civilians. Despite failing to capture Teller's dissidents, the Imperials manage to destroy the Truant and capture the Reticent . After interrogating the captain and his crew, Vader and Tarkin learn about the identity of Teller and his dissidents.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 14 BBY > Former Galactic Republic Intelligence officer Berch Teller leads a rebel campaign against the Galactic Empire. :: Berch Teller and his rebels attack the Imperial facility in the Nouane system . In response to the insurgents, Vice Admiral Dodd Rancit fortifies the facility. Despite the stolen Carrion Spike ' s stealth technology, the insurgents are forced to retreat by a continuous onslaught of long-range lasers. After barely escaping with their lives, Teller and his dissidents deduce that their contact Rancit has betrayed them. Suspecting a trap at Carida , the dissidents instead send the Carrion Spike on autopilot to the Carida system , and transfer to Berch Teller's warship .

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 14 BBY > Former Galactic Republic Intelligence officer Berch Teller leads a rebel campaign against the Galactic Empire. :: A battle takes place over an unidentified moon near the Gulf of Tatooine . Berch Teller chooses this location for his rebel cell to ambush an Imperial convoy, transporting the hyperdrive for the secret Death Star project from Desolation Station to Sentinel Base . However, Tarkin learns about their plan and leads Imperial Navy reinforcements which defeat the dissidents. However, Teller manages to escape into hiding.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 14 BBY :: Moff Wilhuff Tarkin enunciates the Tarkin Doctrine .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 14 BBY :: A major shift in the Imperial bureaucracy occurs as a result of the Berch Teller campaign; the Deputy Director position of Imperial Intelligence 's Naval Intelligence Agency is filled by Colonel Wullf Yularen of the Imperial Security Bureau whilst ISB Deputy Director Harus Ison is reassigned to the Ubiqtorate . Additionally, Admiral Nils Tenant is promoted to the Joint Chiefs , Wilhuff Tarkin is appointed to the position of Grand Moff and Governor of the Outer Rim, and the Imperial Ruling Council members Conan Antonio Motti and Cassio Tagge are also promoted.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 14 BBY :: With its newly installed hyperdrive recovered from Berch Teller 's insurgency, the Death Star leaves Geonosis' orbit and moves into deep space.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 14 BBY :: Secret mission to rebuild the Jedi Order
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 14 BBY > Secret mission to rebuild the Jedi Order :: The Inquisitorius senses an echo in the Force from the former Padawan Cal Kestis in hiding on Bracca , and the Second Sister and Ninth Sister move to apprehend him. Following a tense confrontation, Kestis escapes the planet aboard the Stinger Mantis .

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 14 BBY > Secret mission to rebuild the Jedi Order :: Cal Kestis sets out with former Jedi Cere Junda, pilot Greez Dritus , and the droid BD-1 to recover a holocron containing a list of Force-sensitive children sealed within a vault on Bogano by the Jedi Master Eno Cordova . Following Cordova's instructions to open the Vault, Kestis travels to Zeffo , where a Project Auger outpost, by order of the Emperor himself, excavates the tombs of Zeffo Sages Eilram and Miktrull . Kestis is then tasked to find Chieftain Tarfful, a friend of Cordova's.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 14 BBY > Secret mission to rebuild the Jedi Order :: Saw Gerrera and the Partisans attack an Imperial Refinery on Kashyyyk. With the help of Cal Kestis, they successfully free numerous Wookiees, though Gerrera retreated from the planet in thinking that Kashyyyk was already lost. Several Partisan fighters choose to stay; led by Mari Kosan , they flee into the Shadowlands to join Tarfful's resistance.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 14 BBY > Secret mission to rebuild the Jedi Order :: Kestis, in addition to having a large bounty placed on him by the Haxion Brood , is wanted by the Imperial Inquisition. Nevertheless, the Jedi learns to let go of his fear as well as to keep rising up from the Origin Tree .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 14 BBY > Secret mission to rebuild the Jedi Order :: Kestis finds that the Bogano Vault could be unlocked by an astrium from the Tomb of Kujet on Dathomir. However, the Jedi is accosted by the nightsister Merrin and the mad Jedi Taron Malicos on Dathomir. Eventually arriving at Kujet's tomb, Kestis is overwhelmed by regret and anger over his past and loses control, destroying his master's lightsaber.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 14 BBY > Secret mission to rebuild the Jedi Order :: After building a new lightsaber on Imperial Ilum, which now houses a world-wide artificial trench, Cal Kestis returns to Dathomir, where he and Merrin realize that they were both survivors who shared a similar past of loss and aim to restore their kind. They thus join forces, allowing the intricate Force of Dathomir to consume the fallen Taron Malicos.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 14 BBY > Secret mission to rebuild the Jedi Order :: While Merrin expresses her concern that recruiting the children of the Force would instead endanger the potential Jedi, Kestis presses on his quest for the holocron at Bogano as the Empire approaches. While Kestis overpowers the Second Sister, the echo of her past and suffering overwhelms Kestis, allowing the Inquisitor to steal Eno Cordova's holocron for the Empire.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 14 BBY > Secret mission to rebuild the Jedi Order :: Cal Kestis and Cere Junda discuss the Second Sister's past, with Junda accepting the pain of the past, letting go and willing to move on. Kestis is knighted, and together, the two Jedi storm the Fortress Inquisitorius on Nur to recover Eno Cordova's holocron. The Second Sister dies, though not before she sees pass her hate for Junda's failure regarding Padawans, instead desiring her kind to be avenged. Kestis and Junda barely escape the facility with their lives and the holocron, and back aboard the Stinger Mantis , Kestis destroys the holocron; the Jedi chooses to let go of forging the destinies of children, instead letting their destinies lie before the will of the Force.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 14 BBY :: Berch Teller fails in attempting to assassinate Wilhuff Tarkin on Eriadu's Tarkin family –owned Carrion Plateau .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 14 BBY :: Tarkin's tightening of the Empire's grip on the Outer Rim, including his homeworld Eriadu, ensures a safer environment for Imperial citizens on Eriadu. One such citizen was the brother of Xea, who leaves home to enlist as a stormtrooper after Governor Tarkin's visit to his farm despite his father's warnings. The brother of Xea joins the Junior Academy in Phelar , Eriadu. (Approximate date)
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) :: 13 BBY
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 13 BBY :: While hiding on the planet Lah'mu with his wife, Lyra, and his daughter, Jyn Erso, Galen Erso is taken away by Orson Krennic to continue his work on the Death Star. Jyn is taken in by Saw Gerrera .

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 13 BBY :: Goll , a former member of the Free Ryloth movement, along with Hera Syndulla , Karthakk Group member Trae Baratha and the crew of the freighter Eclipse go on relief mission to deliver gattis-root extract to Twi'lek elders on Ryloth stricken with bybbec fever .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 13 BBY :: Han and Qi'ra partake in an auction on Corellia for a data cube with plans for a shield generator ; originally won by the Droid Gotra , the Kaldana Syndicate ultimately wins the bid.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 13 BBY :: Han and Qi'ra attempts to escape from the Imperial– and White Worm Gang –controlled Corellia; only Han is successful, with Qi'ra being later rescued by Crimson Dawn's Dryden Vos and enlisted as his lieutenant .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 13 BBY :: Han enrolls in the Imperial Starfleet with the hopes of being a pilot. As Han lacks a family name, the enroller gives him the surname "Solo."
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 13 BBY :: Han Solo trains alongside the brothers Lyttan and Tamu Dree , Kanina Nico , and Beilert Valance at the Imperial Carida Academy as TIE pilot cadets .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 13 BBY :: During the final stages of the mission on Qhulosk , Han Solo disobeys Instructor Yurib Nakan 's orders to rescue Valance, saving his life. Solo's crew becomes disillusioned with the Empire and lets Ghular miners to escape from both their Qhuloskian slavers and the Empire. Valance is fitted with cybernetics to compensate for his injuries whilst Kanina Nico rejoins her love in the nearby Huru system to escape from the oppression of the Empire.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 13 BBY :: The brother of Xea graduates from the Junior Academy to the Imperial Academy of Eriadu . He eventually becomes the stormtrooper TK-462 and requests to be reassigned to combat rebels on Lothal. (Approximate date)

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 13 BBY :: A crisis begins on Moltok when a Makurth boss shoots at a Ho'Din settlement . This leads the Ho'Din to request Imperial support to prevent the crisis from becoming a full-fledged war. At the time of the crisis the Gozanti -class cruiser Blood Crow is assisting the freighter Dromedar when it is requested to help settle the matter. While members of the Blood Crow ' s crew stay behind to fix the Dromedar , the Blood Crow jumps into hyperspace.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 13 BBY :: The Dromedar is raided and captured by the pirate band Culoss , led by the insurgent leader Nevil Cygni . The pirates take the crew prisoner and capture the ship's tibanna cargo. When an Imperial boarding party led by Lieutenant Thrawn answers the Dromedar ' s distress call, they are greeted by Cygni, who trapps the Imperials. However, Thrawn turns the tables on his captors by using a buzz droid to cut a hole in the cage and expose parts of the ship to the vacuum of space. Despite his efforts, Cygni and some of his followers manage to escape with the smuggled tibanna canisters.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) :: 12 BBY
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 12 BBY :: Lord Momin constructs Fortress Vader on Mustafar's Gahenn Plains for Darth Vader, who then kills the dark side servant and artist after a battle against Mustafarians disturbed by the castle's construction. Vader then enters the portal within the Sith cave beneath his fortress, encountering apparitions of his past and losing his lover, Padmé Amidala, once again in his inability to let go. After witnessing the future coming of a figure who was, unbeknownst to the Dark Lord of the Sith, his son Luke Skywalker, Darth Vader destroys the portal in his anger, accepting that it gave him what he needed.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 12 BBY :: Ephraim and Mira Bridger are captured by the Galactic Empire.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 12 BBY :: Hera Syndulla leaves Ryloth to fight against the Empire.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 12 BBY :: K-2SO is constructed on Vulpter .

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 12 BBY :: Darth Sidious creates a being later known as Dathan on his secret Sith world of Exegol .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) :: Between 12 BBY and 11 BBY
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > Between 12 BBY and 11 BBY :: Cal Kestis and Merrin duel the Fifth Brother on Murkhana . Within years, the crew of the Stinger Mantis break up.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) :: 11 BBY
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 11 BBY :: A conflict takes place between a small group of rebels on the planet Gorse and its moon, Cynda , and the Empire, represented by the ambitious industrialist Count Denetrius Vidian . After learning that Vidian is visiting the Gorse system , the rebel operative Hera Syndulla travels there to tail the Count. In an attempt to meet the Emperor's new thorilide quota and to discredit his rival Baron Lero Danthe , Vidian decides to detonate Cynda under the pretext of accessing the moon's substantial thorilide resources. In response, Hera forges an informal rebel cell consisting of the dissident miner Skelly , the former Jedi Padawan Kanan Jarrus , and the former surveillance operator Zaluna Myder to stop the Count. After learning that Vidian has tampered with his research on Cynda's detonation, the rebels are able to convince the Imperial Captain Rae Sloane that Vidian's destruction of Cynda will cause the moon's thorilide crystals to disintegrate. Sharing a common enemy, the rebels and Sloane takw action to stop Vidian's plan. The Count is killed during a showdown above Cynda. Due to the rebels' actions and Sloane's help, Cynda is saved from destruction.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 11 BBY :: Hera Syndulla and Kanan Jarrus join forces.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 11 BBY :: Temmin Wexley is born on the planet Akiva to Norra and Brentin Lore Wexley .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 11 BBY :: Death of Enfys Nest's mother , leader of the Cloud-Riders , the mantle of which passes down to the teenage Enfys Nest.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 11 BBY :: A battle erupts above the planet Umbara between naval forces of the Empire and forces controlled by the mysterious individual known as Nightswan . The battle ends in the Empire's favor when Captain Thrawn relays orders to the other vessels of Admiral Carlou Gendling 's task force to target Nightswan's Vulture droids while they are within the transmission shadow of the individual ships.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) :: Between 11 BBY and 9 BBY
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > Between 11 BBY and 9 BBY :: A land dispute takes place on the planet Cyphar between the local Cyphari Afe clan and human colonists based on the Hollenside Enclave , rooted in the colonists' desire to harvest a pre-spice material used in the manufacture of scarn that is running in a vein beneath the Afe territory. After a brief battle, Commander Thrawn and his aide Eli Vanto manage to arrest Clay Tanoo and a group of other colonists that entered Afe lands in the dark of night to collect the pre-spice, but they can not be brought on charges because possession of pre-spice is not considered to be illegal. To destroy the colonists' source of profit, Thrawn uses turbolaser fire to burn out the entire pre-spice vein, which angers the Afes, whose farmland suffers a considerable amount of damage in the process. In response to angry report of Afe Chief Joko , Thrawn has to explain his actions for a court-martial panel, but is cleared of all misconduct over the incident, and soon after Thrawn and Vanto both receive promotions to higher ranks in the Imperial Navy.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > Between 11 BBY and 9 BBY :: A large espionage operation is conducted by members of the Higher Skies Advocacy Group and Yinchom Dojo on Coruscant, involving collecting information on Imperial projects and targets on behalf of the insurgent leader Nevil Cygni . This spy network is exposed by the Lothalite expatriate Arihnda Pryce , who becomes a double agent under the guidance of Commander Thrawn and the Head of the Imperial Security Bureau Colonel Wullf Yularen . As a reward for her services, Pryce is made Governor of the Lothal sector by Grand Moff Tarkin.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > Between 11 BBY and 9 BBY :: The troop carrier Sempre is attacked by two squadrons of stolen V-19 fighters and the frigate Castilus while transporting Wookiee slaves. The attackers damage and board the transport, and the Sempre ' s captain transmits a distress call that is received by the Imperial Star Destroyer Chimaera , Commodore Thrawn's flagship. Imperial authorities ordered the Chimaera ' s stormtrooper commander Major Ayer not to tell the Chimaera ' s crew that the Sempre is transporting slaves. However, Thrawn and Lieutenant Commander Eli Vanto insist on inspecting the Sempre and discover its true cargo.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > Between 11 BBY and 9 BBY :: After the attack on the Sempre , insurgents and freed Wookiee slaves attack the space station Lansend Twenty-Six in an attempt to liberate their companions. With their communications jammed, Lansend Twenty-Six can not call on the nearby Baklek Base for support, but are aided by the Chimaera under the command of Thrawn, who had anticipated the attack. The rebels are defeated soon after.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) :: 10 BBY
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 10 BBY :: The Gorothites of Goroth Prime are enslaved by the Empire and mine hyperbarides . This releases a dangerous amount of radiation onto the planet and its inhabitants.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 10 BBY :: The Galactic Empire establishes the Imperial security complex on Scarif's surface.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 10 BBY :: " Vader's Many Prosthetic Parts ," a song written by Nakari Kelen 's mother and performed by the group Hakko Drazlip and the Tootle Froots , that mocked Darth Vader, is published. Just days after its release, the Empire sent the entire membership of the group to the spice mines of Kessel .

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 10 BBY :: Lando Calrissian and L3-37 take up a weapons smuggling job to Kullgroon . They supply the local Petrusian resistance, assisting Kristiss to liberate her people from the Empire. Later, Calrissian denies Kristiss' offer to pioneer a new future for Petrusia , and instead tracks down the Millennium Falcon , which had been captured by the Empire and impounded on Vandor . Calrissian and L3-37 arrive at Vandor's Fort Ypso one week after leaving Kristiss' people.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 10 BBY :: Han Solo is expelled from the Imperial Flight Academy for insubordination. He is reassigned to Mimban as an Imperial Army trooper .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 10 BBY :: The Empire wages a planetary war against the Mimbanese Liberation Army on the swamp world of Mimban , in an effort to secure the planet's vital resources while establishing a regime loyal to the Empire. The 224th Imperial Armored Division engages the Mimbanese, with the latter eager to drive the Imperials from their homeworld at all costs. The campaign sees the deployment of numerous swamp troopers and wet-weather gear stormtroopers , with the Imperial ground forces making use of trench warfare against the Mimbanese.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 10 BBY > The Empire wages a planetary war against the Mimbanese Liberation Army on the swamp world of Mimban , in an effort to secure the planet's vital resources while establishing a regime loyal to the Empire. The 224th Imperial Armored Division engages the Mimbanese, with the latter eager to drive the Imperials from their homeworld at all costs. The campaign sees the deployment of numerous swamp troopers and wet-weather gear stormtroopers , with the Imperial ground forces making use of trench warfare against the Mimbanese. :: In one of the bloodiest battles of the campaign, Major Staz leads a group of Swamp troopers including Corporal Han Solo in a ground assault to capture a ridge from the enemy. The Mimbanese fire explosives which kill Major Staz. Han and several soldiers regroup in a trench under the command of "Captain" Tobias Beckett , the last senior officer in the group. Beckett leads the Swamp troopers in a successful assault on the ridge.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 10 BBY > The Empire wages a planetary war against the Mimbanese Liberation Army on the swamp world of Mimban , in an effort to secure the planet's vital resources while establishing a regime loyal to the Empire. The 224th Imperial Armored Division engages the Mimbanese, with the latter eager to drive the Imperials from their homeworld at all costs. The campaign sees the deployment of numerous swamp troopers and wet-weather gear stormtroopers , with the Imperial ground forces making use of trench warfare against the Mimbanese. :: During a break in the fighting, Solo encounters "Captain" Beckett and his gang associates Val and Rio Durant , who are planning to commandeer a Y-45 armored transport hauler . Recognizing an opportunity to leave behind the Empire, Solo offers them his services as a pilot. When Beckett rebuffes Solo, he threatens to expose them as infiltrators, so Beckett convinces Lieutenant Bolandin they caught Solo trying to desert the Imperial Army. Bolandin throws Solo into a pit to be devoured by "the beast," a Wookiee called Chewbacca . Solo use his knowledge of Shyriiwook to convince the Wookiee to help him escape. After breaking out they go to a nearby airfield where Beckett and his gang are preparing to depart on their stolen transport hauler. Impressed by his determination, Beckett allows Solo and Chewbacca aboard his stolen hauler.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 10 BBY :: Seeking to repay his debts to the Crimson Dawn crime lord Dryden Vos , Beckett leads his gang in a heist on the planet Vandor to recover coaxium from an Imperial conveyex . The criminals face a group of range troopers , led by Captain Denwade Banevans , guarding the transport, forcing them to detach several of the conveyex's carriages to lose them. The group is later ambushed by Enfys Nest and her Cloud-Riders , who intend to seize the cargo for themselves. During the gun battle, Rio is fatally wounded while repelling boarders from his AT-hauler. Val, who had set up position on a nearby bridge overlooking the tracks, is attacked by several 11-3K viper probe droids . Realizing there is not enough time for her to abandon her position, she blows the bridge up and is killed in the ensuing explosion. The coaxium wagon falls into a deep gorge and explodes the Luftgriff Peak in half.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 10 BBY :: Beckett and his crew raid the spice mines of the planet Kessel controlled by the Pyke Syndicate, in order to retrieve raw coaxium for Crimson Dawn.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 10 BBY > Beckett and his crew raid the spice mines of the planet Kessel controlled by the Pyke Syndicate, in order to retrieve raw coaxium for Crimson Dawn. :: Enslaved workers, organics and droids alike, are liberated from the Spice Mines of Kessel, including the Wookiee Sagwa . L3-37 is destroyed while leading the rebellion, and Han Solo makes the Kessel Run in just under thirteen parsecs, which Solo rounds down to twelve.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 10 BBY :: A showdown breaks out on the planet Savareen between Crimson Dawn, the gang of Tobias Beckett and the Cloud-Riders to determine who would take control of the coaxium stolen from Kessel. Dryden Vos is killed by Qi'ra and Tobias Beckett is killed by Han Solo.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 10 BBY :: Qi'ra joins Maul, leader of Crimson Dawn, on Dathomir. The Dawn disappears a few years later.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 10 BBY :: Han Solo wins the Millennium Falcon from Lando Calrissian in a game of sabacc on Numidian Prime .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 10 BBY :: Sebulba and his crew ; consisting of podracer Volo Bolus , Luuda Fluxion and Trizz Vezcro , escape from a facility belonging to Sorbee Manufacturing after stealing a binder coil .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 10 BBY :: Sebulba's crew attempts a heist to steal credits at the Hutt colosseum on Nar Shaddaa . They fail, resulting in Sebulba being captured by Grakkus Jahibakti Tingi .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) :: 10/9 BBY
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 10/9 BBY :: The Empire sterilizes Geonosis, eventually leaving Klik-Klak and Karina as the only remaining Geonosians on the planet.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 10/9 BBY :: Construction of the Death Star moves from Geonosis to Scarif .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) :: 9 BBY

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 9 BBY :: Cal Kestis' adventures
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 9 BBY > Cal Kestis' adventures :: Kestis faces Senator Daho Sejan on Coruscant, retrieving data for Saw Gerrera and killing the Ninth Sister.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 9 BBY > Cal Kestis' adventures :: Kestis finds the High Republic Jedi Dagan Gera on Koboh , but Gera had fallen to the dark side and the Bedlam Raiders rallies to Gera. While the Empire battle the raiders, Kestis kills Gera with the help of Bode Akuna and acquires the Abyss compass .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 9 BBY > Cal Kestis' adventures :: Akuna, an Imperial agent and fallen Jedi, betrays Kestis; the Empire raids Cere Junda and Eno Cordova's Jedi Archives on Jedha, battling Hidden Path insurgents while Vader kill the Jedi.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 9 BBY > Cal Kestis' adventures :: Kestis and Merrin duel Akuna on Tanalorr . The pair then begin preparing the world to host the Hidden Path.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 9 BBY :: Miara Larte is promoted to the Rebel Alliance rank of captain at a ceremony on Alderaan.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 9 BBY :: Admiral Jyrom Ottdell of the Imperial Navy is murdered with poisonous zolall extract .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 9 BBY :: Imperial Security Bureau's Agent Andressa Divo investigates the Malkite poisoners as a result of Jyrom Ottdell's murder, though Divo receives " Malkite poisoner kits " with an inscription warning against continuing the investigation.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 9 BBY :: Jak is born on Coruscant.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 9 BBY :: The Jedi Nari is hunted down on Tatooine by the Grand Inquisitor , the Fifth Brother, and the Third Sister . The Inquisitors kill him and hang his deceased body in a village for the residents to look upon.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 9 BBY :: Leia Organa is kidnapped on Alderaan by Vect Nokru and his crew at the behest of the Third Sister; Bail Organa travels to Tatooine and begs Obi-Wan Kenobi for help to find his beloved daughter.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 9 BBY :: Obi-Wan Kenobi searches for Leia at the planet Daiyu while being hunted down by the Inquisitorius. They successfully escape the planet, and Kenobi learns that Anakin Skywalker is alive at the process.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 9 BBY :: Obi-Wan and Leia arrive at the planet Mapuzo on instructions from the criminal Haja Estree , who contacts them with Imperial Captain Tala Durith , an undercover agent of the Hidden Path . Vader arrives on the planet and confronts Kenobi, injuring and burning him.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 9 BBY :: Kenobi, alongside Tala Durith, infiltrate the Fortress Inquisitorius on the moon Nur to rescue Leia after she was captured by the Third Sister . They escape with the help of members of the Hidden Path.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 9 BBY :: Vader leads an attack on Jabiim and its Hidden Path fighters. Tala Durith sacrifices herself while the Third Sister reclaims her identity as Reva Sevander and betrays Vader.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 9 BBY :: Vader and Kenobi duel at a labyrinth of rock pinnacles on a barren moon ; the latter is victorious and leaves. The hunt for Obi-Wan Kenobi ends after Palpatine questions Vader.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 9 BBY :: Reva Sevander makes an assault on the Lars Homestead in order to kill Luke Skywalker, but she spares him and redeems herself to the light side of the Force with the help of Obi-Wan Kenobi.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 9 BBY :: Obi-Wan Kenobi, having found his inner peace, is finally able to see and converse with the Force ghost of Qui-Gon Jinn.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 9 BBY :: Governor Quesl of the planet Botajef attempts a secession from the Empire, as a cover to steal the planet's valuable art collections for himself. Commodore Thrawn leads Imperial forces in a battle against the Botajef Defense Force that ends in an Imperial victory, Quesl is arrested and Botajef returns to the Empire.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) :: 9/8 BBY
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 9/8 BBY :: Obi-Wan Kenobi saves Luke Skywalker when the boy confronts Jabba the Hutt's henchmen about their master's " water tax ," under which the local farmers are suffering during the Great Drought . Kenobi carries the unconscious Skywalker to the Lars family homestead and leaves before he wakes up, but the child's courage reassures Kenobi that the Jedi have not yet met their end; his waning hope is renewed.
