Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) :: 8 BBY
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 8 BBY :: Keln , a loved one of Boushh 's, is born.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 8 BBY :: Un'hee is born. (Approximate date)
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) :: 8/ 7 BBY

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 8/ 7 BBY :: A year after the Great Drought, Pagetti Rook and a Klatooinian guard take the Wookiee bounty hunter Black Krrsantan to the Dune Sea where Obi-Wan Kenobi stopped Jabba's water tax. Rook tells Krrsantan about how five of Jabba's saltiest warriors were taken down while not one of them saw the man who did it. Krrsantan roars and starts off towards the moisture farm in front of him. At the Lars Homestead, Owen Lars just recently returned from Tosche Station with some T-16 skyhopper parts. He looks for his nephew, Luke Skywalker in hopes of repairing their busted speeder with him and mend their already strained relationship. All of a sudden, as Owen calls out to his wife Beru , Krrsantan kidnaps him. Meanwhile, unbeknownst to Owen, Luke has run away from home with the intention of going to Mos Eisley and getting off of Tatooine. He does not make it that far as he senses something is wrong. He runs back to the homestead to find the place a mess and his aunt Beru wounded on the floor with a rifle. Luke asks her what happened and where uncle Owen is. She tells him that it took him and orders him to hide in the maintenance bay until he hears her voice.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) :: Between 8 BBY and 2 BBY
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > Between 8 BBY and 2 BBY :: A crew led by Khel Tanna , Han Solo and Chewbacca carries out a heist on the planet Galator III on behalf of Jabba Desilijic Tiure. According to Tanna, Solo and Chewbacca blew the crew's rendezvous, which almost gets the crew killed. Despite this, Chewbacca and Solo prevent Tanna's crew from being placed in shackles, and the heist is pulled off. Afterwards, Tanna, Solo, and Chewbacca visit Jabba on Tatooine, who praises Solo for his actions, to the displeasure of Tanna, who questions why Solo is credited for the success of the heist as it had been her crew that had pulled it off. After the discussion with Jabba, Tanna informs Solo it is the last job she will pull with him as she leaves Jabba's Palace .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > Between 8 BBY and 2 BBY :: Jabba Desilijic Tiure sends Han Solo, Chewbacca, and the bounty hunter Greedo to travel to Coronet City on Corellia, to steal an urn of pure verillix containing the ashes of Krestrel D'Naran , the archrival of Jabba. They fail to recover the urn and learn that is has been sold by Augustus Graves , the CEO of the Corellian Engineering Corporation , to Madelin Sun on the planet Antillion .

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > Between 8 BBY and 2 BBY :: Han Solo meets Corbus Tyra , who claims to be his father, Ovan .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > Between 8 BBY and 2 BBY :: Solo, Chewbacca, and Corbus Tyra travel to Antillion where Solo and Tyra are able to locate the urn, but before they can leave with it, they are intercepted by the Wookiee bounty hunter Krrsantan , who is working for Augustus Graves. He stunnes Tyra, and takes Solo to his ship . Chewbacca engages in a firefight with Krrsantan but the bounty hunter is able to escape, though not before Chewbacca places a homing beacon on his ship.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > Between 8 BBY and 2 BBY :: Chewbacca and Tyra follow Krrsantan to the planetoid Mollo Tanka , though before Tyra could accompany Chewbacca on the rescue , he pretends to be dazed by the stun blast, and Chewbacca leaves him aboard the Falcon. Chewbacca makes his way to Graves' building, and after scaring off a pair of security guards, makes his way to Graves' office, where he discovers Solo being interrogated for the location of the urn. Chewbacca manages to avoid a blast by Krrsantan, and engages him in a Wookiee death-duel . As the two fight, Chewbacca pulls out a thermal detonator and sets it off at the feet of the bounty hunter, which allows Chewbacca and Solo to fire at security guards before they make their escape from Graves' office. The two, being chased by Krrsantan and security guards, arrive to where the Millennium Falcon had been parked, only to discover that it had been stolen by Tyra.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > Between 8 BBY and 2 BBY :: Tyra, in possession of the urn, makes his way to the planet Iakar, and while waiting to speak with an Imperial supervisor, is arrested by Marshal Buck Vancto for various prior crimes. The marshal takes Tyra to the Benelex Marshal Service Headquarters on the planet Paqualis III . The Falcon ends up in an Imperial Impound Facility on Iakar.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > Between 8 BBY and 2 BBY :: Khel Tanna's crew, Han Solo, Chewbacca, and Greedo extract Corbus Tyra from the Benelex Marshal Service Headquarters. However, Chewbacca is captured and imprisoned on Gulhadar , and Solo is shot by Greedo and dumped onto the planet Escalan .

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > Between 8 BBY and 2 BBY :: Tanna's crew make plans with Greedo and Tyra to recover the Falcon and the urn. Tyra and the bounty hunter T'onga go undercover as Imperial officers in the facility on Iakar, while Tanna, Akko , and Ooris Bynar hide in a smuggling compartment as Tanna's ship is captured by the Empire and taken to the impound facility. Greedo arrives at the facility in an attempt to reclam the Falcon and causes enough of a ruckus to be escorted out of the facility by a stormtrooper. At that moment, Greedo caused an explosion and T'onga lockdowns the facility and discovers where the Falcon is being held, and passes on the location, Impound Paddock XM139 , to Tanna and Bynar. The pair go to the paddock and find the Millennium Falcon in a docking clamp . Bynar sets to remove the clamp, but they are attacked by stormtroopers. The Thisspiasian is able to remove the clamp, and Tanna calls in Akko to use her ship to attack the stormtroopers, allowing Tanna and Bynar to escape the facility with the Falcon . They make their way to a rendezvous point, and Tyra learns that the urn had been taken from the Millennium Falcon .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > Between 8 BBY and 2 BBY :: Solo washes up on the shore on Escalan and is found by Doctor Aber , who heards him mutter his own name as she brings him to a spare bed. After healing, Solo goes to a local bar, but is kicked out due to not speaking the native language. He then sees a broadcast reporting on the impending executions of three prisoners in the Benelex Marshal Service prison on Gulhadar, including Chewbacca. He finds a local who speaks Basic and gets them to fly him to Gulhadar. Upon reaching the prison, they pick up Chewbacca, the criminal Phaedra , and the pirate Maz Kanata, who were in the midst of escaping.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > Between 8 BBY and 2 BBY :: After dropping off Kanata at her castle on Takodana , Solo and Chewbacca fly to Tatooine along with Phaedra. At Chalmun's Cantina they find Greedo and interrogated him for the Falcon 's location. Greedo confesses that the ship is in a docking bay in Mos Entha . Before split from Tanna's crew, Greedo had cheated in a game of Sabacc to gain ownership of the Falcon . He also admitts that the urn had been confiscated by Grand Moff Wilhuff Tarkin and stored within his office on Coruscant. Once punching Greedo and leaving him unconscious in the street, Solo reclaims the Falcon .

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > Between 8 BBY and 2 BBY :: Solo, Chewbacca, and Phaedra travel to Coruscant, and after Phaedra calls in a couple of favors, the trio acquires uniforms of waste disposal personnel, as the Empire does not do background checks on those who work in the sewers. Through sewer network they arrive to Tarkin's building , and Phaedra disguises herself as the RA-7 protocol droid 6D6-RA-8 and persuades Tarkin to leave the office to speak with the Emperor. Solo and Chewbacca arrive and kill the Grand Moff's guard of death troopers . Solo finds the urn, but soon stormtroopers arrive, and the trio jumps from the window and land on a passing train, as Phaedra had planned. A trooper shoots Chewbacca, and the Wookiee falls off of the train and into the city .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > Between 8 BBY and 2 BBY :: As he falls Chewbacca grabbs hold of a speeder, turning it to the side and causing an Imperial officer to fall out of it, before the Wookiee gets into the speeder. Meanwhile, Solo and Phaedra are still aboard the train, though Solo dropps the urn on the train, which splits it open. Phaedra grabbs the bottom half whilst Solo collects the top, and she learns that what is inside the urn is not ashes as they been informed and asked him what it is. The three returned to the Falcon and at the suggestion of Solo, they visit former Sava Korin Pers on Nar Shaddaa , who informs them that they have the neural core of the droid insurgent Ajax Sigma , destroyed during the High Republic by the Jedi Order. They decide to bury the neural core.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) :: 7 BBY
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 7 BBY :: The mechanic Salman Paak from Ferrix attends a Separatist meeting on the planet Jondora where he meets a woman who tells him that, if he is serious about politics, he can act as a liaison for Ferrix to, unknowingly, the Axis network via the use of a fractal radio unit, which he will be paid to keep alive. He eventually meets the network's leader, Luthen Rael , and introduces him to local mechanic Bix Caleen .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 7 BBY :: Bodhi Rook enters the Terrabe Sector Service Academy to train to be a pilot.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 7 BBY :: Jyn Erso goes on a solo mission to the planet Horuz , tasked by Saw Gerrera, with the goal of assassinating Imperial scientist Dorin Bell . Jyn suspects that Gerrera sent her to the mission as a way to celebrate her fifteenth birthday, and that the Partisan Staven helped convince Gerrera to trust her with a mission following her failure when Gerrera tested her with a detonator kit. En route to Horuz, Staven briefs Jyn on the terrain, the drop point, and the rendezvous point. Jyn arrives hours early to Bell's small transport unit. When he arrives, he is killed by land mines, an insurance that Gerrera had planned for. Jyn feels some shame and some relief that she has not been the one to kill Bell.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 7 BBY :: The Imperial Military brutally suppresses an uprising on the Lasat homeworld of Lasan . Alexsandr Kallus of the ISB orchestrates an invasion that sees the near-extinction of the Lasat species. After Kallus defeats a member of the Lasan High Honor Guard , the guard gives him his J-19 bo-rifle in accordance with the Boosahn Keeraw , the Lasat warrior way. A number of Wookiee soldiers armed with weapons whittled from the wroshyr trees of their home planet also take part in the battle, sacrificing their own lives to try to prevent the massacres on Lasan. Thanks to the Wookiees' intervention, some Lasat are saved. The conflict causes the Imperial Senate to ban T-7 ion disruptor rifles , after Taris 's senator Tynnra Pamlo sees evidence of the atrocity.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 7 BBY :: Obi-Wan Kenobi rescues 12-year old Luke Skywalker and his friend Windy after they are left unconscious during a sandstorm .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) :: 6 BBY
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 6 BBY :: The Partisans , using invitations forged by Jyn Erso, infiltrate the sakoola blossom festival on the planet Inusagi alongside Arane Oreida and her partisans . They massacre all the guests at the festival using flechette launchers .

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 6 BBY :: The warlord Malkhan of the planet Crucival goes into conflict against other factions that seek to control the planet on the Malkhani Insurrections . Eventually, Malkhan dies, and his lieutenants each stak a claim to his cache of offworld weapons. An unprecedented bloodletting ensues, and all the former warriors of Malkhan, who now lack an army to serve, fall into misery and deprivation.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 6 BBY :: The Mandalorian Gar Saxon , a former member of Death Watch who fought under Maul in the Clone Wars, becomes governor of Mandalore as an Emperor's Hand and leader of the Imperial Super Commandos . (Approximate date)
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 6 BBY :: As a cadet in the Imperial Academy , Sabine Wren builds an Arc Pulse Generator which reacts with the beskar alloy inside the Mandalorian armor, incinerating the armor and disintegrating their wearers. As Wren witnesses the Empire subjugating Mandalore, she is wracked with guilt and escapes the Imperial Academy with the help of her friend, Ketsu Onyo , and deserts the Empire. (Approximate date)
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 6 BBY :: The Leonis family migrates to Capital City on the planet Lothal, where Leo and Tepha work as agricultural scientists for Imperial Ministry of Agriculture .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 6 BBY :: Imperial forces on Lothal under the command of Lieutenant Piers Roddance massacre a gathering of peaceful demonstrators in the Westhills near Capital City.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 6 BBY :: Dhara Leonis , daughter of Tepha and Leo, becomes a cadet of the Academy for Young Imperials . Inside she is identified as a Force-sensitive and kidnapped by the Grand Inquisitor , in order to be forcibly inducted into Project Harvester on the planet Arkanis and be trained as an Inquisitor.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 6 BBY :: After witnessing the Westhills massacre , Lothalite Beck Ollet joins a three-member rebel cell, and they infiltrate a mining camp in Lothal's Westhills and plant detonators on Imperial mining droids.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 6 BBY :: The Dizonites of Dizon Fray are massacred using Sabine Wren's weapon.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 6 BBY :: An Imperial masking unit is stolen from the Imperial naval yard on Lozash , one of many high-level thefts of Imperial technological items during the early rebellion against the Galactic Empire. The masking device finds its way into the hands of Anto Kreegyr 's rebel group .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 6 BBY :: The Empire's mining activities on Jelucan lead to the planet becoming polluted.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 6 BBY :: Jelucan people start using similar clothes and house decorations, reducing the division between the first and second-wavers .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 6 BBY :: Chio Fain and his partner Rone undertake a job which involved slicing into property of the Empire. The two slicers were caught, and Rone lets TIE fighters catch up to her, which allows Chio to escape into hyperspace. Rone is killed, which makes Chio hold a hatred towards the Empire, and wanting to kill Darth Vader.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 6 BBY :: Kuat Drive Yards designs the Raider -class corvette .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 6 BBY :: Miramir is born on the planet Hyperkarn .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 6 BBY :: Tiras Or'un is born.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) :: 5 BBY (exact chronology undefined)

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 5 BBY (exact chronology undefined) :: Cassian Jeron Andor is embarked on a search for his long lost sister Kerri , whom he had not seen since she was six years old on their homeworld of Kenari . Having discovered a lead on a Kenari girl working at the Leisure Zone brothel on the planet Morlana One , Cassian travels to the brothel but discovers from a hostess that the woman in question has departed months earlier. He enters in a conflict with Preox-Morlana sentry guards Kravas Drezzer and Verlo Skiff , who both end up dead at his hands.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 5 BBY (exact chronology undefined) :: Deputy Inspector Syril Karn begins to investigate the incident on Morlana One and is able to determine a male from Kenari as the perpetrator of the crime. After receiving a tip from a Ferrix resident identifying the suspect as Cassian Andor, Karn and three Pre-Mor squads led by Sergeant Linus Mosk arrive on Ferrix to detain him. Andor is however aided by rebel agent Luthen Rael and the pair are able to kill several Pre-Mor agents and escape , while other townsfolk destroy the East Team's Tac-Pod . Andor and Rael then rigged a speeder bike with a bomb and send it towards the remaining Pre-Mor agents while escaping in another, causing it to explode and kill at least two. In the chaos that ensued, they are able to reach Rael's ship and escape the planet, leaving the police agents scattered and the city in disarray.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 5 BBY (exact chronology undefined) :: The Galactic Empire annexes the Morlani system and overall Morlana sector from Preox-Morlana following the disastrous results of the Pre-Mor Security's attempt to capture Cassian Andor. The Empire occupies Ferrix, and an Imperial garrison is established on the planet, headquartered in Hotel Rix . The occupation is overseen by ISB Supervisor Blevin , who has Chief Inspector Hyne , Deputy Inspector Syril Karn and Sergeant Linus Mosk transferred away from Ferrix.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 5 BBY (exact chronology undefined) :: As Supervisor Blevin departs Coruscant to oversee the takeover of the Preox-Morlana Corporate Sector, he misses a conference on the ISB headquarters office, resulting in a speech he was meant to give being agreed to be moved up.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 5 BBY (exact chronology undefined) :: A rebel cell comprised of leader Vel Sartha , Taramyn Barcona , Arvel Skeen , Karis Nemik , Cinta Kaz , and Imperial double agent Lieutenant Gorn plan a mission to the planet Aldhani , with the goal of stealing a quarter's worth of the Imperial payroll for the entire sector. Cassian Andor, operating under the name "Clem," is forcibly installed by Luthen Rael on the job. On the day of the phenomenon known as the Eye of Aldhani , Barcona leads Skeen, Nemik, and Andor to the Aldhani dam , the main Imperial base, disguised as Imperial Army troopers. Meanwhile, Sartha and Kaz travel through the water, climbing up the dam before disabling communications. Gorn allows the squad of four men to pass by and take the Imperial commandant, Jayhold Beehaz , and his family, hostage. Rendezvousing with the other two members of their group, Gorn watches their backs as Sartha, Barcona, Nemik, Skeen, and Andor go down to the vault to locate the credits. As Kaz watches the hostages, the others force the Imperial soldiers there to load the credits onto a Max-7 Rono freighter . However, by the time Gorn arrives in the vault to escape as well, a group of Imperials has discovered them. After a shootout that leaves Gorn and Barcona dead, Andor is able to launch the ship, although Nemik is injured during takeoff. After escaping from three TIE fighters in the chaos of the Eye of Aldhani, a brilliant meteor shower, Sartha, Skeen, and Andor visit a doctor to help Nemik while Kaz leaves the Imperial base disguised as an Imperial officer. Nemik can not be saved, and when Skeen suggests that he and Andor take the payload and abandon Sartha, Andor shoots and kills him. He tells Sartha of this before taking his cut and departing.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 5 BBY (exact chronology undefined) :: The Empire ramps up its security measures in response to the events on Aldhani. The Public Order Resentencing Directive results in tribute tax of 400 million credits levied on any system harbouring partisan activity, re-evaluation or doubling of all sentences for crimes directly or indirectly affecting the Empire, suspension of fine and levy postponements, changes in the Imperial prison system including removal of oversight measures, and classification of all anti-Imperial offenses as a Class One Offense. The Imperial Emergency Act results in increase of Imperial Security Bureau authority with officers allowed to request personnel and material from the Imperial Army and Navy and increased focus of Supervisor responsibilities, imediate revocation of local festivals and customs suspected of harbouring rebel activity, and increase of Imperial Army and Navy authority.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 5 BBY (exact chronology undefined) :: Andor travels to Niamos , a tropical world that had once been a popular site for fugitives who needed to lie low but since had fallen under Imperial occupation . There, he goes under the alias Keef Girgo and stays in a hotel with the woman Windi . After a run-in with a shoretrooper , who accuses him of being involved in anti-Imperial activity, Andor is arrested and brought to the Niamos Courthouse . Waiting in a long line of citizens waiting to be heard by a judge and prosecutor . Under the Empire's recently enacted Public Order Resentencing Directive, Andor is sentenced to six years in prison on Narkina 5 and given no chance to protest.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 5 BBY (exact chronology undefined) :: The Imperial Security Bureau initiates a hunt for Cassian Andor for his role in the ambush on Ferrix and his links to the rebel operative Axis, unaware that Andor is already incarcerated under a false name on the moon Narkina 5. The operation is headed by Supervisor Dedra Meero , who questions former Preox-Morlana Deputy Inspector Syril Karn to find out any information about Andor. She sets up an active base in the Ferrix main city.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 5 BBY (exact chronology undefined) :: Luthen Rael evades capture above Segra Milo after his ship engages Imperial Captain Elk and his Cantwell -class Arrestor cruiser .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 5 BBY (exact chronology undefined) :: A prisoner uprising occurs in the Imperial Prison Complex on Narkina 5 . Cassian Jeron Andor and Ruescott Melshi are among the prisoners that escape.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 5 BBY (exact chronology undefined) :: Former Confederacy of Independent Systems member and Neo-Separatist rebel leader Anto Kreegyr , with thirty or fifty insurgents from his rebel cell , launch a raid against an Imperial power station on the planet Spellhaus . Luthen Rael attempts to recruit the Partisans into the effort and asks them to provide air support, but Saw Gerrera refuses to put his soldiers at risk for another group. By the time Gerrera has changed his mind, the Imperial Security Bureau has learned of the attack, forcing Rael and Gerrera to agree to let Kreegyr and his men fall into the trap. They reason such a sacrifice will give the rest of the rebellion an opening and protect Rael's ISB spy, Lonni Jung . The raid ends in a complete destruction of Kreegyr's forces. To the Empire, the victory is a way to recover from the rebel attack on Aldhani, much to the disapproval of Dedra Meero, who believes that capturing prisoners who might've had valuable information was ignored.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 5 BBY (exact chronology undefined) :: In order to hide her illicit rebel fundings from the Empire, Mon Mothma begrudgingly lets her daughter, Leida , meet Stekan Sculdun , the son of oligarch Davo Sculdun .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 5 BBY (exact chronology undefined) :: A spontaneous riot and citizen uprising against the Empire takes place on Rix Road in Ferrix City during former Daughters of Ferrix president Maarva Carassi Andor 's funeral procession . After the planet came under Imperial occupation, Ferrixians including Maarva Andor become increasingly weary and restless against their reign. Inspired by the recent attack on Aldhani, Andor decides to take action against the Empire, using her farewell speech to condemn the Empire and to urge her people towards action against them. As contempt and anger towards the Empire had been building for weeks, her speech is the spark that ignited a full on riot against the Imperial forces led by Dedra Meero and Prefect Vanis Tigo . When the latter tries to interrupt and cut off her recorded anti-Imperial speech, the denizens of Ferrix attack the Imperial Army trooper units guarding the Hotel Rix headquarters attempting to storm the hotel and lynch Tigo. During the uprising, the son of a recently executed Ferrix denizen threw a homemade bomb against the troopers, killing and injuring many. In response, Tigo orders the stormtrooper forces stationed in the facility to open fire against the protestors, resulting in heavy casualties.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 5 BBY (exact chronology undefined) :: Saw Gerrera, along with Partisans Jyn Erso , Reece Tallent and Codo go on a mission to the planet Tamsye Prime to scout out an old Clone Wars munitions factory that the Empire is using in hopes of a future, larger attack. Establishing a cover as tertiary units on a propaganda documentary filming on the site, Erso forges the scandocs for their cover. The crew enters the factory to capture information on film and send it to other rebel groups, but are betrayed by Tallent, who suspects Jyn's true identity as daughter of Imperial scientist Galen Erso . The remaining Partisans fight their way out the trap and the Empire's planned destruction of the factory, but with the Empire behind them, Gerrera gives Jyn a small blaster and orders her to hide in an old shell turret until daylight. Believing that he will come back for her, Jyn watches from the bunker as Gerrera deals with the traitorous Tallent, and then leaves her alone.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 5 BBY (exact chronology undefined) :: Assisted by street orphan Ezra Bridger, Bossk is hailed as an Imperial hero on Lothal.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 5 BBY (exact chronology undefined) :: The Spectres rebel cell raid Imperial shuttles on the occupied worlds of Ryloth , Christophsis , Kashyyyk , Toydaria , and Lothal , downing TIE fighters , Imperial Troop Transports , and All Terrain Defense Pods defending the supply ships. Hera Syndulla pilots the Spectres' ship , the Ghost ; Kanan Jarrus fires its laser cannon turrets; Sabine Wren drops her ion bombs; and Garazeb Orrelios transfers cargo from the shuttles to the Ghost , defended by its detachable fighter , the Phantom . The raids conclude with the Spectres' stay on Lothal, having struck a significant blow against the Empire and strengthening the growing rebel movement.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 5 BBY (exact chronology undefined) :: The rebel crew of the Ghost undertakes an operation to attack and destroy over two dozen Imperial interrogator droids near an Imperial center on Lothal. The rebels, including Sabine Wren and Garazeb Orrelios, attack an Imperial convoy and successfully destroy the shipment using a paint bomb. After the attack, the Empire releases a propaganda report via the Imperial Holovision claiming that a large group of insurgents destroyed Timely Tutor education droids bound for Lothal's neediest schools.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 5 BBY (exact chronology undefined) :: Kanan Jarrus comes up with a plan to attack what he and the Spectres believe is an Imperial cargo convoy, but it is a trap set by the Empire where at least four TIE/ln space superiority starfighters attack the Ghost . Outnumbered and sustaining damage, Jarrus and Hera Syndulla struggle to work together as a communication disruption prevents the two from coordinating their responses. Their astromech droid, C1-10P —or "Chopper"— uses Phantom , to destroy the remaining TIE fighters, allowing the Spectres to escape.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 5 BBY (exact chronology undefined) :: Sabine Wren attacks an Imperial airfield on Lothal, as a diversion to allow the Ghost , to lift off nearby without detection from Imperial forces. Wren paints her crest, the starbird , onto a TIE fighter and places a paint bomb in the center of it. The resulting explosion destroys the TIE fighter and draws the attention of the Empire, allowing the Ghost to go undetected. As a result of her attack, the Empire names a person matching Wren's description as one of Lothal's most-wanted criminals .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 5 BBY (exact chronology undefined) :: Ezra Bridger witnesses a battle between the Ghost and a TIE fighter piloted by Baron Valen Rudor , which is shot down and crashes to the ground. Bridger steals several components from the TIE fighter that he will later sell in the Black Market, including the pilot's flight helmet. Rudor opens fire on Bridger with the TIE fighter's laser cannons, but Bridger incapacitates Rudor with his energy slingshot.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 5 BBY (exact chronology undefined) :: The Spectres, contracted by crime lord Cikatro Vizago , undertake a heist to steal blasters from the Empire at the Capital City of Lothal. They are interrupted by Ezra Bridger, who steals one of their sets of crates, leading to a chase throughout the city, with the rebels attempting to outrun the Empire, and Bridger attempting to outrun both. They make it out of the city, fighing off stormtroopers and TIE fighters as they flee. Bridger eventually gets away from the rebels, but is saved by the Ghost , which shoots down an approaching TIE fighter. Kanan Jarrus offers Bridger a chance to escape as more fighters approached, and he leaps onto the ship. The Ghost is able to escape into hyperspace before returning to Lothal, where it scrambles its signal so it can not be recognized. The rebels bring many of the crates, also containing food and other supplies, to a refugee settlement called Tarkintown , where the rebels provide it for free to the refugees. Jarrus and Hera Syndulla deliver the blasters to Vizago, who pays them in money and in information, including the potential whereabouts of Wookiees who had been enslaved by the Empire.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 5 BBY (exact chronology undefined) :: The Spectres attempt to rescue the Wookiees aboard Transport 651 , an Imperial Gozanti -class cruiser , however they walk into a trap set by Agent Alexsandr Kallus of the Imperial Security Bureau . They soon realize the Wookiees are not aboard the vessel, and are forced to flee from Kallus and his forces. During the escape Ezra Bridger is captured, and the rest of the crew has to return and rescue him. While a prisoner, Bridger learns that the Wookiees are being taken to the spice mines of the planet Kessel . The rebels soon arrive at the mines, where the Ghost fires upon the stormtroopers guarding the slaves, freeing the prisoners. They are confronted by Kallus and his troops, who open fire on the rebels and the Wookiees. As the Ghost engages with TIE fighters, Kanan Jarrus ignites his lightsaber and reveals himself as a Jedi. While Jarrus and other members of his crew secure most of the Wookiees, Bridger goes in pursuit of the Wookiee child Kitwarr , who is being chased by a stormtrooper. Jarrus saves them both from Agent Kallus, and the Ghost flees from Kessel with the Wookiees.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 5 BBY (exact chronology undefined) :: As Cikatro Vizago is willing to pay for weapons, the rebel crew of the Ghost travels to the planet Garel with the objective of stealing T-7 ion disruptor rifle from the Empire, aboard a star commuter shuttle . Aboard the shuttle is Minister Maketh Tua , who is attempting to procure the weapons from their manufacturer, Amda Wabo , with the help of R2-D2 and C-3PO , but the droids are secretly working for Senator Bail Organa of Alderaan to destroy the weapons. The rebels learn the location of the weapons in the Garel City Spaceport , and steal them after a brief firefight over Garel. They escape back to Lothal, where they attempt to sell the weapons to Vizago, but are discovered by Agent Kallus. This leads to a brief battle against Imperial forces, in which the rebels destroy the disruptors so they can not fall into Imperial hands. The Spectres escape Agent Kallus' forces and return the droids to Senator Organa, who is able to learn about them from R2-D2.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 5 BBY (exact chronology undefined) :: Ezra Bridger and Garazeb Orrelios are send on a supply run by Hera Syndulla, where they attempt to steal meiloorun fruits purchased by the Empire, but are discovered, leading to a chase throughout the settlement of Kothal . Orrelios hijackes a TIE fighter operated by Baron Valen Rudor, and the two escape aboard it away from the settlement. Originally planning to return to the Ghost , they discover Imperial Troop Transports carrying three prisoners: Morad Sumar , an old friend of Bridger's parents, as well as Sumar's wife and an Aqualish . The rebels mount a rescue , freeing the prisoners and afterwards hidding the TIE fighter on Lothal.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 5 BBY (exact chronology undefined) :: The Spectres learn that Jedi Master Luminara Unduli survived the Great Jedi Purge and is being held in the Spire , a prison on the planet Stygeon Prime , and attempt to rescue her. However, Unduli in reality was executed by the Empire years earlier and the residual Force essence within her bones is used to lure surviving Jedi to their deaths. The Grand Inquisitor intends to trap the rebels in the Spire and kill Kanan Jarrus and Ezra Bridger. The rebels infiltrate the Spire and make their way through the facility, Jarrus and Bridger locate Unduli's cell, and find the Inquisitor waiting for them. Jarrus and the Inquisitor engage in a duel during which the Inquisitor attempts to turn Bridger to the dark side of the Force. When the boy refuses, the Inquisitor aimes to kill them both, but they are able to flee and, ultimately, escape the facility with the other rebels.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 5 BBY (exact chronology undefined) :: An Imperial Gozanti -class cruiser engages the Ghost near an ion storm . A pair of TIE fighters deployed by the transport follow the rebel ship while it tries to escape the pursuers in the ion storm, where Sabine Wren is able to shoot them. With only the cruiser remaining, lightning hits the Ghost but Ezra Bridger manages to recover energy and Sabine eliminates the cruiser. The Ghost leaves the ion storm and Hera Syndulla jumps to hyperspace to meet with Kanan Jarrus and Garazeb Orrelios, who simultaneously raid an Imperial transmission centre aboard the Phantom .

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 5 BBY (exact chronology undefined) :: The crew of the Ghost embarks on a mission to locate and destroy a powerful kyber crystal held by the Empire. To locate it, they need an Imperial decoder from the office of ISB Agent Kallus at the Imperial Headquarters on Lothal. To gain access to the complex, Ezra Bridger is tasked with infiltrating the Imperial Academy below to steal the decoder, where he spends several weeks training with the Imperial Army. He finally locates the decoder and steals it with the help of fellow cadet Zare Leonis , who is looking for his sister, cadet Dhara Leonis , disappeared from the academy months before. Bridger provides the decoder to the rest of the rebels, allowing Kanan Jarrus and Hera Syndulla to go to the coordinates of the kyber crystal. The Spectres then pass the decoder to Leonis' girlfriend Merei Spanjaf , who needs it to access several classified ISB files on Dhara at the Imperial Transportation Ministry of Lothal. Bridger remains behind to save fellow cadet Jai Kell , who is in danger from the Grand Inquisitor after showing signs of Force sensitivity. Leonis and Kell commandeer an All Terrain Defense Pod and the three are able to make it out of the academy before the Inquisitor arrives. Bridger and Kell flee, but Leonis remains to find out more about his sister, under the pretense of having tried to stop the other two. Jarrus and Syndulla track down the Imperial convoy carrying the kyber crystal and destroy it.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 5 BBY (exact chronology undefined) :: Imperial forces led by the Grand Inquisitor arrive at a village on the planet Vyndal searching for Kanan Jarrus, who had been taken in by the villagers after landing on Vyndal. The Imperials first visit the villager's protector Yeleb , who lies and claims that they had not seen Jarrus. Sensing the lie, the Inquisitor orders his stormtroopers to burn down the village, drawing out Jarrus and causing both the Jedi and Yeleb to attack . Yeleb receives a mortal wound during the battle, causing Jarrus to flee through the village, drawing the Imperials away from fighting villagers. Jarrus then boards one of the Imperials' Gozanti -class cruisers and sets it to leave the planet before exiting the vehicles. The Imperials chase the vehicle off world believing Jarrus is still aboard.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 5 BBY (exact chronology undefined) :: Capture of Jovan .

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 5 BBY (exact chronology undefined) :: The crew of the Ghost attack an Imperial convoy on Lothal using intelligence from Ahsoka Tano, who secretly coordinates with cells such as the Ghost crew under the codename " Fulcrum ". The information about the convoy's timing and route is accurate, but Tano's intelligence underestimates Imperial defenses. This leads to at least four TIE fighters chasing the Phantom , the Ghost attack shuttle, which is carrying Hera Syndulla, Sabine Wren, and Ezra Bridger. Syndulla is able to outfly and destroy the TIE fighters, allowing the Phantom to escape. Fulcrum's identity and the intelligence she provides become a point of contention for Wren, who wants to know why the rebels are risking their lives on operations like the convoy attack. Syndulla assures her that they are part of something bigger and that there is a plan for fighting the Empire.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 5 BBY (exact chronology undefined) :: Mission to Fekunda Outpost .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 5 BBY (exact chronology undefined) :: The Spectres steal Imperial research crates carrying biological weapons from the Kessel Bunker within the Kessel system .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 5 BBY (exact chronology undefined) :: An armed rebellion is fought by the Liberators in the Atrivis sector , who manage to seize the Imperial Mantooine garrison . Shortly afterward, the Imperial Navy counterattacks, swiftly defeating the Liberators on Mantooine . The Atrivis Resistance Group , after due deliberation, soon joins the Alliance to Restore the Republic .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 5 BBY (exact chronology undefined) :: Beaumont Kin is born.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early rebellion against the Empire (19–5 BBY) > 5 BBY (exact chronology undefined) :: Mapo is born.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) :: 4 BBY
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 4 BBY :: Mission to Kaller .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 4 BBY :: Rescue of Morfizo .

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 4 BBY :: The Spectres disrupt the Empire Day celebration in Lothal's Capital City by carrying out a bombing that destroys an experimental TIE/ad v1 , prompting an Imperial crackdown on Lothal.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 4 BBY :: TK-462 is killed in action on Lothal. (Approximate date)
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 4 BBY :: The Spectres rescue Tseebo , a Rodian defector from the Imperial Information Office , escaping Lothal while being pursued by agent Alexsandr Kallus of the ISB. They safely deliver him and his knowledge to Ahsoka Tano.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 4 BBY :: Ezra Bridger passes his trial to overcome his fears and receives a kyber crystal. He uses it in the construction of his lightsaber , which is completed several weeks later.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 4 BBY :: The Ghost crew accompany Lando Calrissian to his farm on Lothal, fighting their way past an Imperial blockade to get there, after aiding him in acquiring a puffer pig from the crime lord Azmorigan . There they are confronted by Azmorigan and repel him and his enforcers. Calrissian, with the puffer pig, can begin a mining operation.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 4 BBY :: The Empire carry out an assault on the headquarters of the Gray Syndicate , located in the former Ake's Tavern in Lothal's Capital City. Multiple Gray Syndicate members are killed.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 4 BBY :: ISB agent Kallus laids a trap in the Lothal City Capitol Building to the Lothal rebels, using the fake rebel Senator Gall Trayvis . The rebels manage to elude the trap thanks to warning from Zare Leonis.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 4 BBY :: The Specters raid an Imperial installation and steal supplies near the town of Jalath , pursued by Commandant Cumberlayne Aresko and Taskmaster Myles Grint and their forces but managing to escape. Due to their failures, Aresko and Grint are executed by the Grand Inquisitor on Tarkin's orders.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 4 BBY :: Wilhuff Tarkin launches a raid Lothal's Imperial Communications Center during a rebel mission to hack into the communications tower to send out a transmission of rebellion to the galaxy.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 4 BBY > Wilhuff Tarkin launches a raid Lothal's Imperial Communications Center during a rebel mission to hack into the communications tower to send out a transmission of rebellion to the galaxy. :: Kanan Jarrus lets himself to be captured by Grand Moff Tarkin to allow the rest of the Specters to escape.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 4 BBY > Wilhuff Tarkin launches a raid Lothal's Imperial Communications Center during a rebel mission to hack into the communications tower to send out a transmission of rebellion to the galaxy. :: Ezra sends out a message inciting rebellion which is heard on Lothal and in surrounding star systems.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 4 BBY :: The Spectres stage a mission to rescue Kanan Jarrus. With the help of the Devaronian crime lord Cikatro Vizago , the rebels send Chopper to infiltrate an Arquitens -class command cruiser in order to find Jarrus' location. They discover that he is being transported to the Mustafar system aboard Wilhuff Tarkin's flagship Sovereign . Using a stolen Gozanti -class cruiser , the rebels manage to infiltrate the Sovereign and use detonators to unleash a massive electro-magnetic pulse that disables the Star Destroyer. Ezra Bridger then frees his master. While escaping the ship, Jarrus defeats the Grand Inquisitor in a duel which damages the Star Destroyer's reactor core, setting of a chain-reaction and destroying the Star Destroyer. The Spectres manage to escape the stricken vessel before it explodes but are pursued by several TIE fighters. Several rebel ships from Phoenix Cell arrive and rescue them. Following Jarrus' rescue, the rebels meet the former Jedi Ahsoka Tano and learn that they are part of a greater rebellion. For the rebels, the skirmish marks one of their earliest victories in the Galactic Civil War , as well as an escalation of rebellious activity in the Outer Rim Territories.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 4 BBY :: A tribunal is held at the Arkanis Academy against Zare Leonis for crimes against the Empire while training as one of its cadets. He is charged with numerous offenses, plead guilty to them all, and sentenced to death. However, he escapes with help of The Spectres.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 4 BBY :: Dhara Leonis is rescued from Imperial custody, in Area Null of the Arkanis Academy , by her brother Zare Leonis, aided by Beck Ollet, Merei Spanjaf and the Spectres.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 4 BBY :: Hera Syndulla, Ahsoka Tano and Sabine Wren attempt to rescue Janard , an old contact of Sabine prisoner of the Empire in a freighter crashed in the oceans of the aquatic world of Quila . He is killed by stormtroopers during the escape.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 4 BBY :: Commander Jun Sato leads a rebel mission to steal shield generators from an Imperial convoy. Three of his blockade runners and two pilots from Phoenix Squadron distract the convoy's TIE escorts and the three freighters while the Spectres attack the main ship to knock the cargo into space. Hera Syndulla pilots the Ghost to capture the cargo, but two Arquitens -class command cruisers arrive to fire on the rebels, and Sato orders a retreat after the blockade runner's shields are disabled. Syndulla captures the cargo and the rebels flee into hyperspace. When they rendezvouse later, they discover that although the cargo holds vital supplies, it does not have shield generators as their intelligence suggested.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 4 BBY :: The Empire orders a lockdown of Lothal as a reaction to the ongoing insurgency and the unrest on the planet, following the success of the rebel operation in the Mustafar system and the destruction of the Sovereign . As Lothal is an industrial world vital to the Imperial conquest of the Outer Rim, the Emperor sends Darth Vader to put an end to rebel activities on the world. The Empire cracks down on Lothal, bringing numerous Star Destroyers over Capital City as an intimidation tactic, and the demands on the local authorities to produce results in finding the rebels and their sympathizers are intensified. Minister Maketh Tua , acting in place of the absent Governor Arihnda Pryce , is unable to produce the results that Darth Vader and Governor Tarkin expect. Worried that she will be punished, she contacts the rebels and requests assist in defecting from the Empire in exchange for a list of names of those on Lothal who sympathize with the rebellion. The crew of the Ghost agrees to rescue Tua, they return aboard a commandeered Star Commuter 2000 shuttle and intercept the minister. However, the Empire tracked her communications and knew she was planning to defect, and the shuttle she takes explodes in an act of Imperial sabotage, which Kallus blames the rebels for in order to turn public opinion against the rebellion.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 4 BBY :: With Lothal on lockdown, and no ship leaving the surface of the planet without Imperial authorization, the rebels are trapped and forced to hide within Capital City. They infiltrate the Imperial Complex in order to steal a ship and escape the planet. They find a shuttle, as well as a number of military-grade shield generators to steal on behalf of the rebellion, but as they prepare to escape, are confronted by Darth Vader, who engages Kanan Jarrus and Ezra Bridger in a lightsaber duel. Vader quickly overpowers them but allows them to leave, as the shuttle has a tracking device on it intended to find the rest of the rebel fleet . The rebels contact Lando Calrissian for help getting off of Lothal, who in exchange for three of their shield generators, gives the rebels a number of masking transponders that can duplicate the shuttle's signal and scatter the Imperial forces. The Empire, meanwhile, rounds up the citizens of Tarkintown , a refugee settlement the rebels helped in the past, and burns the town to the ground as punishment for the rebellion's activities. Vader tracks them to the fleet using his TIE Advanced x1 and engages the rebel A-wing starfighters of Phoenix Squadron in combat, and mortally damages the rebel command ship, Phoenix Home . The Lothal cell along with Ahsoka Tano boarded the Ghost and during the fighting, Jarrus and Tano use the Force in an attempt to learn more about the Sith Lord's identity. What Tano senses overwhelms her, while Vader realizes that her former Padawan is still alive. The command ship is destroyed and the entire fleet retreats.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 4 BBY :: Darth Vader dispatches the Fifth Brother and Seventh Sister to hunt down the Jedi Ahsoka Tano, Kanan Jarrus, and Ezra Bridger.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 4 BBY :: The Spectres undertake a series of tactical missions on Lothal, Garel, and an infiltration of an Imperial I -class Star Destroyer .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 4 BBY :: The Empire launches an assault on the planet Seelos , against the Ghost 's crew and former clone troopers Rex , Gregor and Wolffe . The two groups, having recently met, defeat the Imperial forces and escape Seelos. Rex joins the Phoenix Cell.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 4 BBY :: Kanan Jarrus and Garazeb Orrelios go on a mission to the Sienar Fleet Systems factory on Absanz to acquire intelligence regarding Sienar's bomb updates on their newest TIE prototypes.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 4 BBY :: Members of the Lothal rebel cell fight against two Inquisitors aboard an abandoned Republic Haven -class medical station . They manage to escape and warn their fellow rebels that the Empire has more Inquisitors.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 4 BBY :: Members of the Spectres, temporarily allied with the pirate Hondo Ohnaka , fight the crime lord Azmorigan and his forces in Nixus Hub 218 on the planet Nixus .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 4 BBY :: Imperial forces blockade the planet Ibaar , cutting the Ibaarian people off from vital supplies. The rebellion, through the Phoenix Cell and the Spectres, launch operations to deliver supplies to an underground network of Ibaarian resistance forces.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 4 BBY :: A prototype B-wing created by the Mon Calamari engineer Quarrie on the planet Shantipole is given to Phoenix Squadron after they use it in the blockade of Ibaar.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 4 BBY :: Two former Imperial officers, Swain and Cogon , flee to the planet Thrad seeking to defect to the rebels. They are met by Beneda , a Thradian assistant of Senator Nadea Tural , who agrees to aid them in return for the intel they bring with them. Agent Alexsandr Kallus, who was conducting an investigation into Imperial personnel on Lothal, suspecting rebel sympathizers, indentifies them and has their shuttle tracked. He arrives aboard the Star Destroyer Relentless , and descends to the planet with and escort of stormtroopers. After a fight in a spaceport, he caughs up to the two fugitives, capturing both of them and retrieving the intel.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 4 BBY :: Members of the Phoenix Cell, led by Sabine Wren, join forces with Black Sun bounty hunter Ketsu Onyo to retrieve the droid EG-86 , carrying valuable intelligence for the rebellion, at the Garel City Spaceport . They deliver the droid to R2-D2, working on behalf of Senator Bail Organa, at the rebel rendezvous known as Havoc Outpost .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 4 BBY :: Kanan Jarrus and Rex rescue Commander Jun Sato , Ezra Bridger, and other rebels of the Phoenix Cell from a experimental Imperial Interdictor , who had been captured when the Interdictor used gravity well projectors to pull their ship out of hyperspace. The rebels destroy the experimental vessel to prevent it from disrupting the movements of the rebel fleet.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 4 BBY :: The Phoenix Cell fights Imperial forces in deep space. The rebels are victorious, but lose Phoenix Three to a TIE fighter.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 4 BBY :: The rebel cell known as the Maya Pei Brigade is ambushed , leading to devastating losses among the group, including 40 fighters, 6 transports, and their leader, Maya Pei . The survivors become stranded on the moon Yavin 4 .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 4 BBY :: Cassian Andor infiltrates Sienar Test Facility 73 on the snowy Sienar Fleet Systems planet of Sienar TF73 and steals a TIE Avenger prototype .

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 4 BBY :: Director of Advanced Weapons Research Orson Callan Krennic hosts a secret conference between various Imperial officers and employees at a clandestine Imperial facility on the planet Maltheen Divide . Attended by members of the Imperial Navy , Imperial Security Bureau , Ministry of Enlightenment , and Tarkin Initiative , the conference ses the planning and discussion of how to justify and execute the planned suppression of Ghorman and it's people to allow Imperial gouge mining of the planet for a rare form of the mineral Kalkite to coat reactor lenses in the Emperor's " Energy Initiative ", which in reality is Project Celestial Power, a project part of Project Stardust that focuses on the development of the Mk I Superlaser . Following the conference, the already running anti-Ghorman whispering campaign continues, and the suppression of Ghorman begins under the command of ISB Supervisor Dedra Meero .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 4 BBY :: A a group of Maya Pei Brigade survivors has a series of disagreements on the moon Yavin 4 . The dispute turns violent when Crab shoots and kills Rika , causing the entire group to begin firing upon one another. They eventually split into two sides, and send out individuals to scout in the jungle. Cassian Andor is caught in the midst of the turf war, which broke out after he was taken prisoner by the brigade, who believed him to be an Imperial pilot. After a long standoff, both sides emerge from hiding, lower their weapons, and agree to settle the conflict through a Roski rules variant of the game Five Hands . Their game is interrupted when a Yavinian doodar charges through the clearing and swallow members of the brigade. Through the commotion, Andor escapes capture and flees in his stolen TIE Avenger prototype.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 4 BBY :: The Empire initiates an Imperial Supply Census on Mina-Rau to audit agricultural output and to verify the visas of the workers on the agriworld . A communications blackout is enforced , isolating illegal refugees that included Ferrixians Bix Caleen , Brasso , Wilmon Paak , and the droid B2EMO , who had been hiding among undocumented laborers. In visit to their mobil-haus , Lieutenant Krole targets Caleen during inspection. After Krole attempts to sexually assault Bix in the dwelling, she defends herself, killing him with a tool. When Krole's companion Corporal Pyke tries to arrest her, Caleen kills him after Pyke and Paak exchanged blaster fire. As Brasso escapes the Imperial forces that had arrested him, Cassian Andor arrives in the stolen TIE Avenger prototype to extract the survivors. Andor kills several Imperial Army troopers and stormtroopers, but Brasso is fatally wounded during the escape.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 4 BBY :: Lieda Mothma and Stekan Sculdun have their arranged wedding on Chandrila. Mon Mothma attempts and fails to talk her daughter out of it. At the wedding, Rael and Mothma determine that Mothma's business partner Tay Kolma is a liability to the network, and Rael has him assassinated by agent Cinta Kaz .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) :: 3 BBY
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 3 BBY :: Ubese bounty hunter Brene is exiled from her people's homeworld of Uba IV , eventually joining a crew led by Boushh .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 3 BBY :: Leia Organa performs her Day of Demand , an Alderaanian ceremony held on the sixteenth anniversary of her original Name Day , where she demands her right to the throne of Alderaan .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 3 BBY :: The Rebellion attacks the Calderos Space Station , an Imperial deep-space waypoint, to conceal rebel shipping to an outpost on the planet Crait .

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 3 BBY :: A bombing is carried out by Saw Gerrera's Partisans on Onoam , one of Naboo's moons, to assassinate Imperial Moff Quarsh Panaka . Panaka had just been visited by Queen Dalné of Naboo and Princess Leia Organa of Alderaan, the two leaving his chalet mere minutes before it was bombed. On her way into the chalet, Organa took note of a civilian worker wearing a breath mask, and she sees the worker fleeing the scene after the bombing. Panaka's death prevents him from relaying to Palpatine his suspicions that Leia, adopted daughter of the House of Organa, is the biological daughter of the long-deceased Senator Padmé Amidala of Naboo.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 3 BBY :: By this point, Dalné became Queen of Naboo, though the position was made ceremonial by the Galactic Empire.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 3 BBY :: The Empire arrests many officials of the government of the planet Christophsis after discovering that they had backed rebel activity, also cracking down on the city of Tophen and orbitally bombarding the capital city of Chaleydonia .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 3 BBY :: A fleet of rebel ships evacuate the planet Paucris Major after learning from Leia Organa that the Empire intended to send a fleet to the Paucris system .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 3 BBY :: Leia Organa travels to the planet Garel with a data-tape holding information on the whereabouts of bases held by the Empire. Slipping past her Imperial escorts, she is able to contact Sabine Wren and lead her to the tape's location.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 3 BBY :: Inquisitors attempt to kidnap Force-sensitive younglings at Takobo but are stopped by the Spectres and Ahsoka Tano.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 3 BBY :: Kanan Jarrus and Ezra Bridger duel the Seventh Sister and the Fifth Brother on the planet Bilzen .

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 3 BBY :: A fight takes place on the planet Imvur between two Rebel cells and the Empire. The Imperials target the Rebel base on the planet in retaliation for a previous raid on an Imperial base , launching a ground assault which initially knocks out the base's defenses, but are stopped by three MLC-3 light tanks and the shuttle Phantom working in conjunction. The rebel base is safely evacuated and destroyed during the retreat.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 3 BBY :: The imprisoned revolutonaries Mira and Ephraim Bridger stage a breakout in an Imperial prison on Lothal. The Bridgers, who had been imprisoned years earlier for broadcasting anti-Imperial messages on Lothal, are inspired to organize the attempt after they hear their son, Ezra Bridger, transmit a message of rebellion across the galaxy. Dozens of prisoners escape, including former Lothal Governor Ryder Azadi , but the Bridgers themselves are killed.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 3 BBY :: The armed forces of the Empire launch an attack against Phoenix's Squadron's hidden base on the planet Garel . The Empire learned of the rebellion's location in the Garel City Spaceport after Ezra Bridger was overheard talking about the rebels' location. Agent Kallus and Admiral Kassius Konstantine of the Star Destroyer Relentless lead the assault against the rebels, while the Inquisitors known as the Seventh Sister and the Fifth Brother seek out Bridger and Kanan Jarrus. At least one rebel transport is downed and destroyed during the rebellion's retreat from Garel, but, due to the piloting of Hera Syndulla of the Ghost , the Empire's tractor beam is disabled and the Phoenix Cell is able to escape from the planet and into hyperspace.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 3 BBY :: The Spectres raid an Imperial depot on the planet Lothal in an attempt to hijack three Sphyrna -class Hammerhead corvettes . These corvettes were delivered to Lothal by Princess Leia Organa of Alderaan, under the guise of a mercy mission, for use by the rebellion. The rebels fight against an Imperial garrison under the command of Lieutenant Yogar Lyste , who believe Organa to be a loyal Imperial and unable to prevent the ships from being stolen. With the help of Leia and the former Lothalian Governor Ryder Azadi , the rebels are able to steal the three corvettes, which bolsters Phoenix Cell .

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 3 BBY :: As rebels of Phoenix Cell try to pass through the Concord Dawn system as part of a new hyperspace route to the planet Lothal , they are engaged in a dogfight by the Protectors of Concord Dawn , led by Fenn Rau and allied to the Empire. Three pilots of Phoenix Squadron are killed and the rebels are forced to retreat.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 3 BBY :: Kanan Jarrus and Sabine Wren travel to the third moon of Concord Dawn , intending to secure an alliance with Fenn Rau . Sabine invokes the Mandalorian code to challenge Rau to single combat, eventually capturing him, and he agrees to let the rebels pass through the system.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 3 BBY :: Phoenix Squadron gains safe passage through the Concord Dawn system from the Mandalorian Protectors .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 3 BBY :: Rescue of a rebel prisoner .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 3 BBY :: Mission to a mining planet .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 3 BBY :: A group of Lasat who survived the destruction of Lasan rediscover Lira San , the ancient homeworld of the Lasat, helped by the crew of the Ghost including Garazeb Orrelios, former captain of the Lasan High Honor Guard .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 3 BBY :: The Spectres conduct a heist at a Mining Guild Asteroid Belt Gas Refinery on an unidentified planetoid to obtain fuel for the Ghost , and the Phoenix Cell. The Gas Refinery is destroyed by Spectres with the help of a purgill herd.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 3 BBY :: Rescue of Imperial cadets .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 3 BBY :: The Spectres, with the help of Ryloth freedom fighters led by Cham Syndulla, hijack an Imperial bomber carrier and destroy a light cruiser as a symbol to rally the people of Ryloth.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 3 BBY :: The Wookiee chief Wullffwarro and his son Kitwarr , who where liberated two years before from Kessel by the Spectres, travel to the heavily forested planet of Oliu in order for Kitwarr to discover if he is Force-sensitive. At some point, Wullffwarro and Kitwarr become separated and Wulffwarro reaches out to the Spectres for help locating him. During a hard rain storm, Kitwarr is eventually found by the crew. Soon after, the storm clears and they are quickly discovered by the Ghost and Wullffwarro's Auzituck anti-slaver gunship .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 3 BBY :: The crew of the Ghost board an Imperial construction module orbiting Geonosis , following intelligence reports from the rebellion suggesting the Empire is constructing something over the planet. Unknown to the rebels, this is the Death Star , which has already been moved. When the rebels board, they are ambushed by Agent Kallus and several stormtroopers. The rebels fight their way back to the Ghost , but Garazeb Orrelios remains locked in combat with Kallus. The Ghost depart while Orrelios heads for an escape pod, but he is followed by Kallus, who also boards the departing pod. The Ghost and Imperial forces engage in a firefight outside of the module, which damages the escape pod and sends it crashing onto Bahryn , an icy moon of Geonosis. Orrelios and Kallus are forced to work together in order to survive. In the process, they learn more about one another's pasts and motivations, and Kallus begins to recognize the cold realities of serving in the Empire.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 3 BBY :: Kanan Jarrus and Ezra Bridger are ambushed by two Inquisitors on Oosalon while scouting for a rebel base.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 3 BBY :: Commander Jun Sato of Phoenix Cell and the crew of the CR90 corvette Liberator rescue Ezra Bridger from the Syvik Delegation , a band of Dug pirates, from their base on the planet Galzez .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 3 BBY :: Kanan, Ezra, and Ahsoka Tano head to the Lothal Jedi Temple to seek guidance on from Master Yoda to defeat Darth Vader and his servants
