Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 3 BBY :: The Spectres steal fuel from the Imperial-controlled Horizon Base for Phoenix Cell. Chopper is accidentally left behind at the depot during the raid.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 3 BBY :: Admiral Kassius Konstantine launches an attack on the Phoenix Cell fleet, while its waiting for fuel to be delivered by the Spectres before it can jump into hyperspace in route to the Yost system , where the rebels intend to set up a base. Meanwhile, Chopper infiltrates the Imperial Cargo transport 241 , and with the help of an Imperial inventory droid named AP-5 , takes control of the ship. They learn that the Yost system is under Imperial control, and the rebels will be jumping into a trap, but they are able to discover a new location for their base, the planet Atollon . After receiving the coordinates, the rebels jump away from the attack and arrive safely to Atollon.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 3 BBY :: Phoenix Squadron establishes Chopper Base on Atollon; Rex and the Spectres secure the base against krykna .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 3 BBY :: Sabine Wren travels to the Black Sun base on the planet Smarab to rescue her old friend, Ojo . She infiltrates the facility and locates Ojo in his prison cell, and explains that she has changed her ways since their previous encounter, where she captured him as a bounty hunter after he betrayed the syndicate. She convinces him to accept her help and fight for the Rebellion. She signals the Phantom for pickup, and Chopper blows the entrance up using its laser cannons. Ojo and Wren exit the building and board the shuttle, escaping to hyperspace.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 3 BBY :: Sabine Wren and Garazeb Orrelios travel to Thune City to recover cargo containers full of weapons and ship parts for the rebellion, fighting for them with the gangster Tomith Garbis of the Garbis family and his forces.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 3 BBY :: Mission in the Kinrah system .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 3 BBY :: Mission to Malachor

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 3 BBY > Mission to Malachor :: Kanan Jarrus, Ezra Bridger, Ahsoka Tano, and Chopper travel to Malachor. There, they forge a temporary alliance with the former Sith Maul to fight several Inquisitors and to obtain a Sith holocron containing knowledge on how to defeat the Sith.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 3 BBY > Mission to Malachor :: Ahsoka Tano and the Jedi are confronted by Darth Vader.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 3 BBY > Mission to Malachor :: Destruction of the Malachor Sith temple ; Jarrus, Bridger and Chopper escape back from Vader and the exploding temple to Atollon; Ahsoka Tano is saved from Vader by Bridger through the World Between Worlds , afterwhich the former Jedi parts ways with Bridger and embarks on a spiritual journey that changes the course of her life as she returns to Malachor and ventures beneath its Sith temple to find Morai , who leads her down into another portal of the World Between Worlds.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 3 BBY :: ISB agent Dedra Meero, at the direction of ISB Major Lio Partagaz , begin their effort to occupy Ghorman for the Empire where they begin construction of an armory and attempt a propaganda campaign to muddy its image among the wider galaxy. Meero uses her lover and Bureau of Standards member Syril Karn to infiltrate and manipulate the Ghorman Front rebels. Cassian Andor is sent by Luthen Rael to investigate the state of the planet's local rebels who are secretly influenced by imperial spies and information.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 3 BBY :: Saw Gererra and his partisans steal rhydonium from a pipeline on a planet with the help of Axis network agent Wilmon Paak.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 3 BBY :: Unhappy with Andor's report on Ghorman, Rael sends Vel Sartha and Cinta Kaz to help to gather information and assist the Ghorman Front instead. They lead a heist to steal weapons from the Empire. Due to the incompetence and insubordination of one of the Ghorman Front grunts, Kaz is accidentally killed, though the heist is successful.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 3 BBY :: Chandrilan businessman Davo Sculdun holds a party at his office on Coruscant for Senate Investiture week. Senator Bail Organa is leaving as senator Mon Mothma and her spouse, Perrin Fertha arrive at the party. Major Lio Partagaz of the Imperial Security Bureau is invited but passes his invitation to two of his subordinates, Lonni Jung and Avril Heert . During the party, Sculdun invites a select group of party guests to tour his art collection . During the tour, Orson Krennic and senator Mothma trade barbs over the Battle of Carmeen . At the same time as the tour, Kleya Marki is able to remove a listening device she had previously planted in the Tinian Codex , an artifact in Sculdun's collection.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 3 BBY :: Imperial doctor Gorst has his interrogation methods extended into a full program by Imperial Intelligence, convincing the Emperor he is too valuable to be left as a one-man Imperial Security Bureau asset. The Imperial Navy donates a facility on Coruscant to the ISB for the training, and Major Lio Partagaz assigns the work on the program to ISB Supervisors Avril Heert and Lonni Jung , who is, unknown to all, an undercover agent for the Axis network . The network dispatches Bix Caleen and Cassian Andor, who are living undercover on Corsucant, to assassinate Gorst. He enters the interrogation room and is surprised by Bix, who strapps him to the same torture device she was strapped on two years prior. Not long after, Cassian destroys his office with explosives.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) :: 2 BBY
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 2 BBY :: Brene joins Boushh's crew .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 2 BBY :: A coalition of rebel groups on the planets Batonn , Denash , and Sammun of the Batonn sector begin an insurgency against the rule of the Empire. They are led by Nightswan , a smuggler and insurgent leader working to overthrow the Empire.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 2 BBY > A coalition of rebel groups on the planets Batonn , Denash , and Sammun of the Batonn sector begin an insurgency against the rule of the Empire. They are led by Nightswan , a smuggler and insurgent leader working to overthrow the Empire. :: The Denash insurgents fight Admiral Kinshara 's One Twenty-Fifth task force of the Empire. The 125th encounters little opposition since the Denash insurgents have evacuated most of their ships and materiel to the planet Batonn , as the Imperials learn after preliminary interrogation of captured insurgents. Kinshara reports his findings to Fleet Admiral Donassius and the other Imperial admirals, including Thrawn and Durril .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 2 BBY > A coalition of rebel groups on the planets Batonn , Denash , and Sammun of the Batonn sector begin an insurgency against the rule of the Empire. They are led by Nightswan , a smuggler and insurgent leader working to overthrow the Empire. :: The Imperial 96th Task Force , led by Commander Karyn Faro , acting under orders of Admiral Thrawn, fights the Sammun insurgents , who are defeated. Her forces destroy two enemy ships and capture four vessels and a considerable range of small arms ordnance.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 2 BBY > A coalition of rebel groups on the planets Batonn , Denash , and Sammun of the Batonn sector begin an insurgency against the rule of the Empire. They are led by Nightswan , a smuggler and insurgent leader working to overthrow the Empire. :: On the planet Batonn, local dissatisfaction with the rule of Governor Restos fuels the emergence of an insurgency led by Nightswan. The Batonn insurgents seize the Imperial garrison on Scrim Island .

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 2 BBY > A coalition of rebel groups on the planets Batonn , Denash , and Sammun of the Batonn sector begin an insurgency against the rule of the Empire. They are led by Nightswan , a smuggler and insurgent leader working to overthrow the Empire. > On the planet Batonn, local dissatisfaction with the rule of Governor Restos fuels the emergence of an insurgency led by Nightswan. The Batonn insurgents seize the Imperial garrison on Scrim Island . :: Fleet Admiral Jok Donassius dispatches Admiral Durril to take back the island. However, Donassius underestimates the tactics of the Batonn rebels and his One Oh Third Task Force sustains considerable damage. Admiral Thrawn and Commander Eli Vanto infiltrate Batonn and obtain intelligence on the Scrim Island insurgents' tactics and their leader, Nevil Cygni. Using this information, Thrawn's 96th Task Force neutralizes the rebels' defenses and forces their surrender.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 2 BBY > A coalition of rebel groups on the planets Batonn , Denash , and Sammun of the Batonn sector begin an insurgency against the rule of the Empire. They are led by Nightswan , a smuggler and insurgent leader working to overthrow the Empire. > On the planet Batonn, local dissatisfaction with the rule of Governor Restos fuels the emergence of an insurgency led by Nightswan. The Batonn insurgents seize the Imperial garrison on Scrim Island . :: Following the battle of Scrim Island, the Batonn insurgents relocate to the Creekpath Mining and Refining complex near Paeragosto City . Admiral Thrawn's attempts to end the siege peacefully are rebuffed by Nightswan, who distrusts Governor Restos. Thrawn's management of the siege is also complicated by an Imperial Security Bureau operation by Agent Gudry to plant explosives inside the mine. The siege comes to an end when the Lothalian Governor Arihnda Pryce remotely blows up the mine's explosives and deflector shield generator to conceal Gudry's murder. The explosion destroys the complex and ends the Batonn sector insurgency, but kills many civilians.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 2 BBY :: With Thrawn's help, Pryce rises to the rank of Governor of the Lothal sector .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 2 BBY :: With Pryce's help, Thrawn is promoted by the Emperor to the rank of Grand Admiral and is given command of the Imperial Starfleet's Seventh Fleet .

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 2 BBY :: Eli Vanto and Thrawn discover that a steady stream of Imperial transports containing enslaved Wookiee continually traveled to an unknown location. Thrawn also discovers that extremely large amounts of Imperial resources have disappeared and been pooled into an unknown project.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 2 BBY :: Thrawn sends Eli Vanto to Admiral Ar'alani to assist the Chiss in return for his services to the Empire.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 2 BBY :: Governor Pryce solicits the services of Thrawn and his Seventh Fleet in dealing with the growing rebellion on Lothal.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 2 BBY :: Ezra Bridger, Sabine Wren and Garazeb Orrelios rescue the pirate Hondo Ohnaka from an Imperial prison on the planet Naraka .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 2 BBY :: A team of rebels from the Phoenix Cell, led by the newly appointed Lieutenant Commander Ezra Bridger, raids the Imperial Reklam Station . Despite losing the shuttle Phantom , they steal six BTL-A4 Y-wing assault starfighter/bombers and liberate several Ugnaught laborers. Under orders from Grand Admiral Thrawn, Governor Arihnda Pryce's Imperial forces allow the rebels to escape into hyperspace.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 2 BBY :: Maul kidnaps the crew of Ghost in order to secure their holocrons; after experiencing a vision revealing that his nemesis, Obi-Wan Kenobi, still lives, Maul parts ways.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 2 BBY :: A rebel GR-75 medium transport and flight of A-wings from Green Squadron are destroyed by Imperial forces led by Captain Vult Skerris , while en route to the planet Teralov .

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 2 BBY :: The Imperial cadets Wedge Antilles and Derek Klivian escape from Skystrike Academy with the help of Sabine Wren and the rebels of Phoenix Cell. The escape begins after Antilles, Klivian, and Rake Gahree express sympathies towards the rebellion against the Empire, which is reported to Phoenix Squadron by Agent Kallus, secretly a rebel informant operating under the codename Fulcrum . Phoenix Squadron tasks Wren, a former Imperial cadet herself, with infiltrating Skystrike Academy and making contact with the would-be defectors. During the first escape attempt, Rake is killed and Wren and the cadets are captured. They make their way through the facility with the covert help of Agent Kallus, and are picked up by Kanan Jarrus and return to Chopper Base on Atollon , where Antilles and Klivian join Phoenix Squadron.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 2 BBY :: Hera Syndulla, Ezra Bridger, and Chopper infiltrate the Syndulla residence on the planet Ryloth , which had become the new Imperial headquarters in the Tann Province , to recover the Syndulla Kalikori . Meanwhile, the Free Ryloth Movement leader Cham Syndulla , Gobi Glie , Numa , and the other Spectres stage a diversionary attack in the Tulara Ravine . Although initially captured by Grand Admiral Thrawn, Hera gets Chopper to plant explosives throughout the building, and the subsequent explosions allow the two rebel factions to escape with the Kalikori .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 2 BBY :: Captain Rex and the Spectres travel to the planet Agamar to obtain proton bombs from a Separatist supply ship , where they are captured by a holdout of the Confederacy of Independent Systems by still-functioning battle droids led by the super tactical droid Kalani . The droid general takes Garazeb Orrelios hostage, and Rex, Ezra Bridger and Kanan Jarrus manage to overpower the droids and confront Kalani, but Imperial forces arrive and attack the Separatist supply ship , so rebels and droids join forces to defeat the Empire.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 2 BBY :: After losing contact with the Mandalorian Protectors, Fenn Rau, Sabine Wren, and Ezra Bridger travel to the third moon of Concord Dawn, finding the Protectors massacred by Imperial Mandalorians under Viceroy Gar Saxon .

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 2 BBY :: After learning that the Empire was about to launch a crackdown on dissidents on the planet Mykapo , the Phoenix Cell and the Spectres evacuate rebel sympathizers and Iron Squadron of the Mykapo insurgents.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 2 BBY :: The Empire initiates a blockade over Synistahg .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 2 BBY :: The Spectres joined with the pirate Hondo Ohnaka and the crime lord Azmorigan loot an Imperial cargo ship drifting in an atmospheric storm on the planet Wynkahthu , recovering several cases of proton bombs for the rebellion.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 2 BBY :: Rebels Morad Sumar , Kanan Jarrus and Ezra Bridger infiltrate the Imperial Armory Complex on Lothal, obtaining the blueprints for a prototype TIE fighter , and escaping with the help of disillusioned ISB Agent Kallus.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 2 BBY :: Maul returns to the Spectres and attempts to take Ezra Bridger as his apprentice; after a skirmish on Dathomir proved Bridger's Jedi compassion, a disappointed Maul parts ways in order to find Obi-Wan Kenobi alone. From Maul's lair, Sabine Wren claims the Darksaber.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 2 BBY :: Hera Syndulla, Garazeb Orrelios and Rex rescue Ketsu Onyo from the Starlag XIX space station, a private intergalactic prison.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 2 BBY :: The Spectres rescue the argora , sacred bird to the Xendekians , while is being transferred to Palpatine's personal zoo in a cargo ship . After infiltrating the ship, the rebels return the bird to the Xendekians.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 2 BBY :: Saw Gerrera and a team of Partisans travel to the planet Geonosis investigating a major Imperial construction project previously posted in the Geonosis system . They descend into an underground labyrinth inhabited by Klik-Klak , sole survivor of the Sterilization of Geonosis . Believing them a threat, he kills all partisans except Saw. Bail Organa and Jun Sato later dispatch the Spectres and Rex to Geonosis to find Saw and investigate the apparent disappearance of the Geonosian species. There they discover poison canisters used to exterminate the Geonosians.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 2 BBY :: As Grand Admiral Thrawn deploys E-XD-series infiltrator droids to several Outer Rims, one of them, EXD-9 , lands on Atollon , home of Phoenix Cell's Chopper Base . After the droid is damaged by several krykna spiders and knocked out, it's found by Garazeb Orrelios, who brings it to the base. To prevent the Empire from discovering the location of Chopper Base, the rebels send it back to its Star Destroyer with its proton warhead set to detonate when reconnected to the Imperial network. EXD-9 destroys the Star Destroyer, but the incident helps Trhawn narrow down his search for the rebel base to the 94 systems where the droids had been sent.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 2 BBY :: Sabine Wren undergoes training with the Darksaber in the fields of Lothal, under the tutelage of Kanan Jarrus.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 2 BBY :: Sabine Wren and the Spectres travel to the planet Krownest in the Mandalore sector with the objective of recruiting Mandalorian Clan Wren into the Rebellion. Together they battle the Imperial-allied Mandalorian Clan Saxon , led by Viceroy Gar Saxon and his Imperial Supercommandos . Sabine kills Saxon in single combat, and a Mandalorian Civil War , between revolting clans and Clan Saxon backed by the Empire, begins.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 2 BBY :: Members of Phoenix Cell launch a mission to rescue Agent Alexsandr Kallus after learning that Grand Admiral Thrawn is close to uncovering him as the rebel informant Fulcrum . Ezra Bridger, AP-5 and Chopper steal a shuttle and allowed it to be captured by Lieutenant Yogar Lyste 's Arquitens -class command cruiser. After Thrawn summoned Lyste and Kallus for an audience aboard the Chimaera , Kallus and the rebels work to prevent Thrawn from discovering Chopper Base . To avoid being discovered as Fulcrum, Kallus frames Lyste. After the rebels escape, Thrawn and Colonel Wullf Yularen deduce Kallus is Fulcrum, but keep it secret to manipulate Kallus into helping them find the rebel base.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 2 BBY :: Ghorman Massacre occurs when Imperial troops respond to peaceful protestors on the planet Ghorman with violence. They successfully complete their takeover of the planet and deploy mining equipment to mine kalkite for the Death Star. After failing to assassinate the ISB's Dedra Meero during the event, Cassian Andor retrieves a damaged Imperial security droid and escapes.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 2 BBY :: Ghorman senator Dasi Oran is arrested in the Senate chamber on Coruscant for challenging the Imperial narrative on Ghorman, spurring senators Mon Mothma and Bail Organa to action.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 2 BBY :: Senator Mon Mothma delivers a speech in the Senate Chamber, denouncing the Empire's actions at the Ghorman Massacre as a genocide and declaring Emperor Palpatine a monster. After the ISB immediately puts out a warrant for her arrest, she flees Coruscant and the Imperial Senate with the help of Cassian Andor and the Axis network. Mothma is branded a traitor to the Empire.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 2 BBY :: Phoenix Squadron dispatches Hera Syndulla and her crew to protect Mothma, who destroy a tactical infiltration pod sent to find her. They meet up with Mothma's shuttle, the Chandrila Mistress , and her escorts from Gold Squadron , part of the Massassi Group of rebels. After a brief skirmish with Imperial warships, the rebel teams travel through the Archeon Nebula using the Archeon pass , a dangerous hyperspace route they hope will dissuade the Empire from pursuing them. They are tracked, however, by a prototype TIE/d "Defender" Multi-Role Starfighter and its TIE/IN interceptor escorts. The rebels manage to elude their TIE pursuers and two Star Destroyers, commanded by Governor Arihnda Pryce and Admiral Kassius Konstantine , by firing proton torpedoes into the nebula's gas clouds to damage the Imperial ships. Mon Mothma is safely delivered to Dantooine.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 2 BBY :: Declaration of the Rebel Alliance ; Mon Mothma forms the Alliance to Restore the Republic above Dantooine , joining together scattered rebel cells to challenge the growing tyranny of the Galactic Empire in one of the most important moments of the Galactic Civil War; the war officially begins.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 2 BBY :: Imperial security droid K-2SO is reprogrammed by the Rebellion and speaks with Cassian Andor for the first time.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 2 BBY :: The Phoenix Cell dispatches the Spectres to recover Imperial codes from the Imperial Security Bureau station on Killun 71 in preparation for the Alliance's attack on Lothal.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 2 BBY :: Ezra Bridger and Chopper travel to the planet Tatooine to stop Maul from locating Obi-Wan Kenobi. Unknown to Ezra, Maul had lured him here with the intent of using him to draw Kenobi out of hiding. After being attacked by Tusken Raiders, Ezra and Chopper get lost in the desert, and are rescued by Kenobi. Maul attacks them but falls to Obi-Wan Kenobi's blade and dies.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 2 BBY :: A battle breaks out between the nascent Alliance to Restore the Republic with the Empire and the Force-sensitive being known as Bendu on and above the planet Atollon . It starts when Grand Admiral Thrawn discovers the location of Chopper Base , the headquarters of the Phoenix Cell. Thrawn's 7th Fleet blockades Atollon, trapping both Phoenix Squadron and General Jan Dodonna 's Massassi Group . Thrawn's fleet inflicts heavy casualties on the two rebel forces. However, Commander Jun Sato 's decision to ram Admiral Kassius Konstantine 's Interdictor vessel allows Ezra Bridger and Chopper to escape and seek help. As his fleet holds the blockade, Thrawn launches a ground assault following an orbital bombardment of the base, while Governor Arihnda Pryce assumes command in space. Meanwhile, on Mandalore , Ezra succeeds in getting the help of Sabine Wren, Fenn Rau , and several members of Clan Wren in destroying Thrawn's second Interdictor vessel. Despite Thrawn attacking Chopper Base, Hera Syndulla and General Dodonna manage to escape with the surviving rebel fleet into space. The rebels and Imperials are both attacked by an angry Bendu, an enigmatic Force wielder, who uses his Force powers to destroy Chopper Base before being subdued by Thrawn. The surviving rebel forces flee to Yavin 4 . A number of members of the Rebel Alliance, including Mon Mothma herself, see the defeat at Atollon as proof their organization is not yet ready for open war.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) :: 2–1 BBY
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 2–1 BBY :: Cassian Andor and K-2SO embark on a raid on an Imperial freighter where they free three Wookiees.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 2–1 BBY :: The Sixty-First Mobile Infantry , nicknamed Twilight Company, fights a warrior group that had captured fellow rebel soldiers on Ferrok Pax , trekking through the ruins on the surface for several days in order to flank the enemy, eventually winning the ensuing battle and freeing the prisoners.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 2–1 BBY :: Twilight Company, led by Captain Micha Evon , confronts the Empire's forces on Allst Prime led by Governor Magé . During the battle, soldier Brand abandons her old life as a bounty hunter and is recruited into the 61st.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 2–1 BBY :: The 61st fights a series of battles at the planet Mygeeto, and an Alliance starfighter squadron earns the name Riot Squadron during the battles.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) :: 1 BBY
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY :: Alliance High Command sets up base on Yavin 4.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY :: After sensing a disturbance in the Force on Batuu, on the edge of the Unknown Regions, Darth Sidious dispatches Darth Vader and Chiss Grand Admiral Thrawn to investigate the area.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY :: K-2S0 leads a Rebel Alliance group to infiltrate an Imperial facility on Mustafar for intelligence vital to the survival of the Rebellion.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY :: The rebel mission to Mandalore occurs. Sabine Wren and Ezra Bridger rescue the former's father, Alrich Wren , from an Imperial prison. Aided by the Spectres, several Mandalorian clans unite and eliminate the forces of Imperial-allied Mandalorian Governor Tiber Saxon . Sabine Wren passes on the Darksaber to Bo-Katan Kryze, who leads as Mand'alor .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY :: At some point afterward, the Empire would launch the Great Purge of Mandalore , destroying much of the planet and its people, with survivors scattering throughout the galaxy. The Imperial forces were led by Gideon , who eventually seizes the Darksaber.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY :: Ezra Bridger, Sabine Wren and Chopper are send to to the planet Jalindi with the objective of hijacking the Empire's Jalindi Station in order to eavesdrop on Imperial transmissions sent through there. They sky-dive onto the station's dish from the Ghost to install a spike, but after the arrival of the Arquitens -class command cruiser Marauder , Saw Gerrera arrives and destroys both the dish and the Marauder .

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY :: Bridger and Wren, along with Chopper, wound up with Gerrera after the mission to Jalindi, as Gerrera invites them on board his U-wing . After Gerrera's U-wing and the Ghost are forced to separately flee into hyperspace due to the arrival of a Star Destroyer, and before Hera Syndulla can dock with Gerrera's ship to pick up her people, Gerrera asks them to assist with investigating a mysterious Imperial shipment passing through Faos Station , which Gerrera is convinced has a connection to the Empire's sterilization of Geonosis. Bridger, Wren, Gerrera, and Chopper sneak on board Freighter 2716 at the space station, and discover the supposed civilian freighter is actually an undercover Imperial vessel. They find a group of technicians, all specializing in power generators and reactors, imprisoned on board. A further search reveals the ship's special cargo: a giant kyber crystal . Gerrera, who wants to ride the freighter to its destination in the empty Tonnis sector , turns on Bridger and Wren, who want to get the prisoners to safety, and rigges the crystal up to the freighter's hyperdrive to create a bomb. Upon discovering that the freighter is only rendezvousing with a Star Destroyer , Gerrera damages the hyperdrive, beginning the process that will lead to the crystal's detonation, before releasing Bridger and Wren and departing in his U-wing. The rebels escape in a shuttle with the prisoners before the explosion, which destroys the freighter and the Star Destroyer. The freed technicians decide to join the Rebel Alliance.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY :: Imperial forces led by Vader and Thrawn battle the Grysk Hegemony . Thrawn speculates that the Grysk would become a major threat to the Empire, and is concerned that the Chiss are on the verge of civil war as some Chiss had aligned themselves with the Grysk. Vader sees that Thrawn's true loyalty lies with Chiss, but despite their differences, Vader makes recommendations on Thrawn's TIE Defender program, which rivalled against Krennic's Death Star project that was endorsed by Tarkin, the Emperor, and Vader himself. Thrawn and Vader return to Coruscant, and Thrawn relays Vader's recommendations to Lothal.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY :: The Imperial Security Bureau conducts a raid on a suspected rebel cell on Lothal. During the raid, Old Jho attempts to help the cell escape, but is caught by the ISB and later executed. After Jho's death, the Empire gives his cantina, Old Jho's Pit Stop , to famous pilot Baron Valen Rudor .

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY :: The Spectres conduct a mission to Lothal to obtain intelligence about the Empire's prototype TIE Defender Elite and, if possible, prepare to aid Ryder Azadi and the Lothal resistance group in liberating the planet. They smuggle themselves onto the planet on Cikatro Vizago 's starship, the Broken Horn , and quickly meet up with Lothal resistance member Jai Kell , who help them escape the town of Jhothal . The next day, Ryder takes Ezra Bridger, Sabine Wren and Garazeb Orrelios to stake out the remote airfield where the Defender Elite is supposed to have a test flight. After the prototype arrives, Sabine and Ezra decide to steal its flight data recorder, sneaking onto the base as Ryder and Zeb keep watch, but with the arrival of Thrawn and Governor Arihnda Pryce , Sabine decides to steal the entire prototype. She and Ezra are pursued by TIE Interceptors, and Pryce activates the prototype's kill switch, causing two of its wings to detach. It crashes and Ezra and Sabine escape into the wilderness with the flight recorder, eventually bringing it to Rebel Command.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY :: Ezra sees a Loth-wolf , the first sighting of one for over 100 years.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY :: Ezra Bridger, confident that Hera intends to return for the Spectres and lead an attack, insists to the Lothal resistance group to get in touch with the Alliance. When the Rebels detect an ore crawler in the vicinity of their new base , they decide to hijack it to use its long-range communications equipment. Ezra, Sabine Wren, Kanan Jarrus, and Garazeb Orrelios board the crawler, confronting its Trandoshan captain, Seevor , who activates an alarm and halts the vehicle. As Kanan and Zeb investigate the crawler's reactor room, Sabine works on activating the vehicle's old long-range transmitter. Kanan and Zeb discover several slaves, as well as Cikatro Vizago, who had been arrested by the Empire after smuggling them onto Lothal. Sabine attempts to activate the transmitter but discovers that Seevor has shut down the crawler's reactors. Ezra fights Seevor in the reactor room, and the captain eventually dies by falling into the smelter. The Rebels are able to contact Hera and Rebel Command, and discover that the Alliance leaders have authorized an attack to prevent the manufacture of Defender Elites on Lothal.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY :: Hera Syndulla is able to persuade the rebel leadership to authorize a fighter assault on Lothal, to attempt to destroy the Imperial Armory Complex , where the Empire is planning on producing the TIE/d "Defender" Multi-Role Starfighters. She is promoted to General and given command of a force of 24 fighters and bombers, a mixed group of T-65B X-wing starfighters and BTL-A4 Y-wing assault starfighter/bombers , with the goal of hitting the Lothal City fuel depot . On the ground, the Spectres and members of the Lothal resistance group destroy the anti-air defenses protecting the factories. At the space battle, the rebels sustain casualties at the hands of Imperial ace Commander Vult Skerris , before Hera lures him into a trap and shoots him down, triggering a chain of events that leads to the destruction of a Star Destroyer, and giving Hera's forces the opening they need to head for their targets. However, Thrawn had been anticipating this, and has another wave of TIE fighters at the ready. The entire strike force is shot down, several of the fighters crashing into Lothal City. Among the few survivors are Hera, Chopper, and Mart Mattin . Hera rescues Mart from stormtroopers attempting to arrest him, but they are pursued by Rukh, who has been sent to capture Syndulla alive. She is captured as Mart and Chopper flee on her orders. They escape the city and reunite with Kanan Jarrus.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY :: Grand Moff Tarkin informs Thrawn that his TIE Defender program has been halted in favor of Krennic's Project Stardust. Considering that the loss of his TIE Defender program would outweigh the loss of Hera Syndulla, Thrawn leaves Lothal to the incompetence of Governor Pryce to plead his case to Emperor Palpatine.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY :: Thrawn agrees to handle a gralloc problem for Krennic's Project Stardust in return for the continuation of funding for the TIE Defender program. While handling the gralloc infestation, Thrawn and the Chimaera traces a missing freighter to find that it had been raided by the Grysks. While battling a Grysk warship at the scene, the Chimaera is joined by Chiss Ascendancy forces led by Admiral Ar'alani. Thrawn reunites with the Chiss as well as Eli Vanto.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY :: From the remains of the battle, Thrawn finds Un'hee, a Chiss girl who was kidnapped by the Grysk for her Force-sensitivity and was forced to serve as a navigator. Eli Vanto finds that many of the missing Imperial transports carried foodstuff which is used to feed Wookiee prisoners working on Project Stardust. They deduce that Imperial Governor Haveland had purposely arranged for similar disappearances as part of Project Stardust. To be rid of Brierly Ronan , a troublesome Imperial officer, Thrawn sends him and Vanto investigate Haveland's Aloxor system .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY :: Dayja Collerand , sent by the Security Bureau's Colonel Yularen, joins Vanto on Aloxor . Processing gathered intelligence, Thrawn deduces that Grand Admiral Balanhai Savit was responsible for stealing supplies from Project Stardust in the guise of grolloc infestations. When confronted, Savit explains that he did so because he believed that Stardust was a waste of resources, and that he hoped to funnel the stolen supplies to aid the Imperial Starfleet.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY :: Grand Admirals Thrawn and Savit thus commit their forces in a non-lethal battle. Savit is defeated and captured by the Chimaera , Collerand, Ronan, Captain Boulag of the Firedrake , and Captain Gilad Pellaeon of the Harbinger . With the help of Thrawn's TIE Defenders, the Chiss defeat the Grysks.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY :: The Spectres mount a rescue of Hera Syndulla from the Imperial Complex on Lothal. Despite rescuing Hera, Kanan Jarrus is killed when he sacrifices his life to protect his fellow rebels from being incinerated by the exploding Lothal City fuel depot . Ezra Bridger enters the World Between Worlds and saves Ahsoka Tano from Darth Vader's blade at Malachor. With the former Jedi's guidance under the eyes of Morai, he then lets go of his master as Tano did hers, resisting the temptation to prevent Kanan Jarrus' death and thus Darth Sidious and the dark side of the Force. Parting ways with Tano, Bridger returns from the mystical realm and rejoins the Spectres. Governor Arihnda Pryce's decision to fire on the fuel depot is deemed a catastrophic mistake; the destruction of the sector's fuel supply, combined with damage to the factories, derails the TIE Defender project, leading to the project being suspended and funding being redirected to Project Stardust.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY :: Ronan gives an unfavorable report of Thrawn to Governor Tarkin, and Project Stardust becomes the sole recipient of funding over the TIE Defender program until Stardust is completed. Before such point, Vader is assigned to protect Stardust. Having received news that the situation on Lothal is "critical," Thrawn returns to the world with the escort of Captain Pellaeon aboard the Harbinger . Before leaving for Lothal, Thrawn takes the gamble of convincing Ronan to travel with Ar'alani back to the Chiss Ascendancy despite Ar'alani's warning that growing chaos amongst the Chiss threatened civil war. Thrawn also recommends Commander Faro to take command the Eleventh Fleet .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY :: With Thrawn's loyalty to the Empire in serious doubt, the Emperor orders him to return for a long talk after handling the rebellion on Lothal. Palpatine also tells Thrawn to reconstruct a part of the Lothal Jedi Temple within the Chimaera to tempt Ezra Bridger, and further instructs the Chiss to persuade Bridger to join him.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY :: The Liberation of Lothal occurs. Ezra Bridger lets go of a Force illusion of his parents, defying Darth Sidious' ploy before sacrificing himself to free Lothal, the planet, and the people he cared about, from Imperial forces under the command of Thrawn. Together with Thrawn, Bridger is taken by a pod of purrgil away to Peridea, in another galaxy.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY :: Jacen Syndulla is born on Lothal to Hera Syndulla and Kanan Jarrus.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY :: Armitage Hux is born on Arkanis to Commandant Brendol Hux and a kitchen woman , making Hux the commandant's bastard son.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY :: Farnay's mother dies.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY :: The Alliance sends a Special Forces team to infiltrate the planet Iakar to steal medical supplies from the Empire.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY :: Two months before the Battle of Yavin, the the Sixty-First Mobile Infantry fights Imperial forces on the planet Crucival . The Empire arms civilians to help them defend the Crucival transmitter tower . However, soon after the battle begins, they abandon the armed civilians and hide under their shield, using the civilians as fodder for the rebels. After decimating the civilian mercenary force, the 61st Company shredds through the tower's defense. After the battle and the death of his friend Pira Ten , one of the civilian mercenaries named Hazram Namir joins Twilight Company, recruited by the Besalisk Gadren .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY :: Around this point, Bansu Ro , having lived on Coruscant as the seventh generation of his family since they became established as a family of pilots, sets off to train at the Imperial Academy on Carida.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY :: Rebel spy Lonni Jung leaks information about the Death Star to Luthen Rael, who is later confronted by ISD Supervisor Dedra Meero during a raid on his shop. Luthen attempts to commit suicide, but is kept alive by Imperial medics and taken to Lina Soh Hospital . In the aftermath, various members of the ISB, including Dedra Meero, are arrested due to the leaked information about the Death Star.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY :: Kleya Marki infiltrates Lina Soh Hospital and disconnects Luthen Rael from medical equipment keeping him alive to prevent any secrets from getting out before being evacuated to Yavin 4.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY :: After discovering Marki to be behind Rael's death at Lina Soh Hospital, the ISB tracks her to a Axis network safehouse and raid it in attempt to capture her. Rebel agents Cassian Andor, Ruescott Melshi , and K-2SO defy Alliance orders to rescue her and bring her back to Yavin 4 to tell the Alliance about the existence of the Death Star. With the ISB having failed to prevent the Death Star information leak from being contained, ISB Major Lio Partagaz commits suicide.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY :: Alliance General Davits Draven sends Cassian Andor to investigate claims of an Imperial superweapon, after first receiving intel from Kleya Marki . Andor is contacted by Tivik , his source within the Partisans, and they meet in the Ring of Kafrene , where Tivik informs Andor that Imperial defector Bodhi Rook has information regarding an upcoming weapons test .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY :: Jyn Erso is freed from the Imperial Detention Center & Labor Camp LEG-817 in Wobani by Rebel Extraction Team Bravo , led by Ruescott Melshi. The Alliance hopes to use Jyn Erso to gain access to Saw Gerrera, after Bodhi Rook is captured by the Partisans on Jedha.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY :: A battle is fought in Jedha City , when Saw Gerrera's Partisans attack an Imperial convoy transporting Kyber crystals in Tythoni Square . Cassian Andor and Jyn Erso, along with Guardians of the Whills Chirrut Îmwe and Baze Malbus , after getting involved in the battle are taken prisoner by the Partisans and brought to the Catacombs of Cadera .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY :: Jedha City is destroyed by the Death Star. Saw Gerrera is killed and his Partisans' campaign to end the imperial occupation of the planet ends.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY :: Operation Fracture occurs.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY :: Cassian Andor and Jyn Erso travel to the planet Eadu with the objective of capturing Galen Erso. Unbeknownst to Jyn, Andor had received orders from General Draven to assassinate Galen on the spot, fearing Galen knows too much about the situation. While Andor hesitates in shooting him, Draven sends the pilots of Blue Squadron , who destroy the Eadu Energy Conversion Laboratory and kill Galen.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY :: Battle of Scarif . Jyn Erso, Baze Malbus, Chirrut Îmwe, Captain Cassian Andor, and Bodhi Rook die while on a mission to steal the blueprints for a secret Imperial superweapon called the Death Star. Destruction of the Imperial Scarif Deployment , Surface Command , and Orbital Command ; first major Alliance victory against the Empire. The Death Star plans are transferred to Princess Leia Organa's Tantive IV
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY > Battle of Scarif . Jyn Erso, Baze Malbus, Chirrut Îmwe, Captain Cassian Andor, and Bodhi Rook die while on a mission to steal the blueprints for a secret Imperial superweapon called the Death Star. Destruction of the Imperial Scarif Deployment , Surface Command , and Orbital Command ; first major Alliance victory against the Empire. The Death Star plans are transferred to Princess Leia Organa's Tantive IV :: Calys dies during the Battle of Scarif.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY :: Secret mission to Tatooine . The Tantive IV escapes with the vital information for Obi-Wan Kenobi on Tatooine at the earlier behest of Bail Organa, yet with the CR90 corvette 's malfunctioning hyperdrive, the ship's signature is leaked and allows for Darth Vader's personal upgraded Imperial I -class Star Destroyer Devastator to trace the Alderaanian craft; Above Tatooine, the Tantive IV is captured along with all its crew, including Leia Organa. However, the plans escape with R2-D2 and C-3PO planetside, the former of which was tasked by Organa to seek Obi-Wan Kenobi.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY > Secret mission to Tatooine . The Tantive IV escapes with the vital information for Obi-Wan Kenobi on Tatooine at the earlier behest of Bail Organa, yet with the CR90 corvette 's malfunctioning hyperdrive, the ship's signature is leaked and allows for Darth Vader's personal upgraded Imperial I -class Star Destroyer Devastator to trace the Alderaanian craft; Above Tatooine, the Tantive IV is captured along with all its crew, including Leia Organa. However, the plans escape with R2-D2 and C-3PO planetside, the former of which was tasked by Organa to seek Obi-Wan Kenobi. :: Owen and Beru Lars are murdered by Imperial stormtroopers; the Lars Homestead and its droid inhabitants are destroyed.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY > Secret mission to Tatooine . The Tantive IV escapes with the vital information for Obi-Wan Kenobi on Tatooine at the earlier behest of Bail Organa, yet with the CR90 corvette 's malfunctioning hyperdrive, the ship's signature is leaked and allows for Darth Vader's personal upgraded Imperial I -class Star Destroyer Devastator to trace the Alderaanian craft; Above Tatooine, the Tantive IV is captured along with all its crew, including Leia Organa. However, the plans escape with R2-D2 and C-3PO planetside, the former of which was tasked by Organa to seek Obi-Wan Kenobi. :: Luke Skywalker leaves Tatooine and begins training to become a Jedi.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY :: Leia Organa is taken to the Death Star, where she is interrogated by Darth Vader and Grand Moff Wilhuff Tarkin in the hopes of extracting the location of the Rebel base from her, but she refuses.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY :: One week before the Battle of Yavin, Grand Moff Tarkin sends his protégé, Ellian Zahra , to the planet Ikkrukk to assassinate the warlord and terrorist Burnium Ro , who has connections with the Nihil of old. She destroys a substantial group of terrorists and believes her mission successful, but later finds out that Ro himself had never been present, and that the man she believed to be her target was simply a decoy.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY :: The Imperial Senate is dissolved by a decree of Emperor Palpatine.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY :: Tarkin threatens to use the Death Star to destroy Alderaan if Organa does not deliver the rebel base. She provides a false location, and although Tarkin believes her, he nonetheless destroys Alderaan to demonstrate the station's power to the galaxy, and in doing so, the price of any type of dissidence. Alderaanians off-world at the time will call Alderaan's destruction The Disaster .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY :: The Sixty-First of the Rebel Alliance fights forces of the Empire in the honey fields of the planet Vir Aphshire . The news of Alderaan's destruction are given without hesitation by the 61st's Captain, Micha Evon .

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY :: Protests erupt in Alderaanian immigrant areas of Coruscant, as citizens become angered at rumors of the Empire's part in the destruction of Alderaan. The Empire kills many rioters and arrests many more immigrants.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > "The Dark Times" (19–1 BBY) > Early Galactic Civil War—before the Battle of Yavin (4–1 BBY) > 1 BBY :: The Millennium Falcon travels to Alderaan so Obi-Wan Kenobi and Luke Skywalker can deliver the stolen Death Star plans to the Alliance. The Falcon is captured by the Death Star while in the remains of Alderaan, leading the ship's crew to hide with their passengers. In order to escape, Solo and Skywalker pose as stormtroopers, allowing the group to arrive to a hiding place within the Death Star. After Kenobi leaves to shut down the station's tractor beam, R2-D2 discovers that Princess Leia is aboard the station, held within Detention Block AA-23 . The group decides to rescue the princess and make their way to the detention center, retrieving Organa and fighting their way back to the Millennium Falcon , while Kenobi successfully disables the tractor beam. Kenobi encounters Darth Vader, and sacrifices himself to give Skywalker and the others a chance to escape. The Millennium Falcon is able to flee the station and is pursued by a group of TIE/ln space superiority starfighters, which the crew manages to destroy.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Battle of Yavin :: The Battle of Yavin . The Death Star is destroyed by the Rebel Alliance.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) :: 0 ABY
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY :: During the battle on Vir Aphshire, the 61st gets news of the destruction of the Death Star, and it boosts morale. The 61st defeats the Imperial forces that day, but the stormtroopers destroy the honey hives to minimize looting from the rebels.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY :: A medal ceremony is held in the Great Temple on Yavin 4 the morning after the Battle of Yavin, to honor Luke Skywalker, who fired the shot that destroyed the Death Star, as well as Han Solo and Chewbacca, who helped Skywalker during the battle. Skywalker and Solo are presented with medals of Bravery by Princess Leia Organa. Following the presentation of medals, the ceremony honors those who died when the Death Star destroyed Alderaan.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY :: At the conclusion of the ceremony, General Dodonna announces that the Rebellion will be evacuating its base and finding a new one.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY :: The Imperial credit encounters some turbulence.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY :: One day after the Battle of Yavin
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY > One day after the Battle of Yavin :: Imperial Security Bureau's Alecia Beck ambushes the Shrikes ; special reconnaissance personnel who identified, secured, and prepared new Rebel Alliance locations, on the planet Tanaab , killing five of them, and only Lieutenant Caluan Ematt is able to escape. He contacts the Alliance on Yavin 4 for help as he travels to the planet Cyrkon .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY > One day after the Battle of Yavin :: Leia Organa sends Han Solo and Chewbacca to extract Ematt.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY > One day after the Battle of Yavin :: Against Jan Dodonna 's orders, Leia Organa and Evaan Verlaine leave Yavin 4 overnight to search the galaxy for surviving Alderaanians .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY :: Organa and Verlaine travel to the planet Naboo , where they rescue a group of Alderaanian musicians called Melodic Order , before heading for Sullust . Unknown to Organa and her followers, the Empire is tracking their movements. Under duress, the Alderaanian Imperial officer Tula solicits information on Organa's whereabouts from her unwitting sister Tace . As a result, Jora Astane 's Alderaan Enclave on Sullust is attacked, but the Imperials are defeated with the help of Organa, R2-D2, and Verlaine, who unleashed a pack of rockrenders . The rebels then depart with the Alderaanians aboard the Lord Junn .

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY :: Organa and her followers discover that the Empire is using Tula to solicit information from Tace. When Tula's Commander Dreed threatens to execute her, Organa agrees to surrender herself in order to save Tace's sister. She also dispatches Uwa Pareece and Astane on a mission to win over the Alder-Espirions , a hybrid species of Alderaanians who had settled on the planet Espirion . However, the mission falls apart due to Astane's xenophobic remarks towards Chief Beon Beonel . Meanwhile, Organa and Verlaine engineer a successful rescue mission which frees Tace and leads to the death of Dreed.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY :: Dreed's deputy Venk attacks the Alderaanian refugee fleet that has gathered above Sullust under Organa's leadership. In desperation, Organa appeals to Chief Beonel for help. Beonel committs the Espirion warship Espirion Multi , which destroys the attacking Imperial Star Destroyer. While Organa returns to the Rebel Alliance, Verlaine is elected "Princess" of the Alderaanian refugee diaspora fleet.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY :: Han Solo and Chewbacca successfully conduct the Cyrkon Extraction , earning the trust of Caluan Ematt.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY :: Solo is given his seventeen thousand credits by the Rebellion as promised by the late Obi-Wan Kenobi, though he loses it during a mission done as a favor for Luke Skywalker. Solo is then continually redirected by rebels across the galaxy to save lives.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY :: A few years before Operation Yellow Moon
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY > A few years before Operation Yellow Moon :: The Empire garrisons Basteel with a token presence in response to rumors of a resistance movement there.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY :: Chewbacca accidentally crashes his A-wing on Andelm IV , a world taken over by the gangster Jaum , who forces his workers to mass-produce dedlanite to secretly supply the Empire's war machine for profit. The Wookiee Chewbacca is persuaded by a local girl named Zarro to free Andelm IV from slavery.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY :: Recalling his experience of being enslaved by Trandoshan hunters alongside other Wookiees, Chewbacca frees the enslaved miners on Andelm IV.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY :: While Chewbacca and Zarro are captured by Imperials, the latter are misled into thinking that Jaum is a member of the Rebellion and no longer desire to expand their reach to Andelm IV. After making their escape, Chewbacca and Zarro part ways on Andelm IV, though not before the Wookiee gives Zarro the medal he himself earned at the Battle of Yavin.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY :: With his A-wing repaired, Chewbacca proceeds to Kashyyyk to return the bandolier of a fallen Wookiee before leaving with the Millennium Falcon .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY :: Several weeks after the Battle of Yavin
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY > Several weeks after the Battle of Yavin :: Luke Skywalker emerges from hyperspace in the Llanic system with the intent of finding a smuggler on Llanic to open a secret weapon supply line to the planet Rodia for the Alliance. He sees one of Moff Abran Balfour 's Star Destroyers, too far to cause harm to his Desert Jewel , but it had launched several TIE starfighters who are firing on an unidentified Kupohan starship . A although the ship's deflector shields are raised, the ship is close to being destroyed. Since the Kupohans had helped the Rebel Alliance in the past, Luke engages the Jewel's sole laser cannon and shoots down a TIE fighter, the remaining ones take evasive maneuvers but resume attacking the Kupohan ship instead of Luke's Desert Jewel . Luke tries to hail the Kupohan ship, but before he can establish a signal, the Kupohan ship flees into hyperspace. Luke leaves as well before putting the Desert Jewel on any Imperial watchlists.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY > Several weeks after the Battle of Yavin :: The Desert Jewel arrives with Luke Skywalker and rebel pilot Nakari Kelen to Rodia. At the planet, the Chekkoo clan , which makes weapons and armaments, has a rivalry with the ruling Chattza clan , which is allied with the Empire, so Leia Organa thinks that the Chekkoo clan might be willing to help the Rebel Alliance in order to overthrow the Chattza clan. Skywalker is greeted by Laneet Chekkoo , who guides them to her clan secret base at the Toopil complex and introduces them to Taneetch Soonta , sales executive for armament manufacturer Utheel Outfitters . Upon seeing Luke's lightsaber, Soonta tells him about her uncle, Huulik , a Jedi Knight during the Clone Wars who was killed by his clone troopers during Order 66. They visit Huulik's tomb, and when the pair are ambushed by a wild ghest near the mausoleum, Soonta retrieves Huulik's old lightsaber from his sarcophagus and gives it to Skywalker. He uses it to kill the ghest and the two return to the Chekkoo Enclave . Skywalker focuses on analyzing Huulik's lightsaber and trying to learn telekinesis, but a planetwide alert for the Desert Jewel forces them to leave Rodia immediately and meet up with Ackbar and Organa on the Promise .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY > Several weeks after the Battle of Yavin :: Luke Skywalker and Nakari Kelen go on a mission to the moon Fex , in order to find the lost collection crew of the Kelen Biolabs and their ship, the Harvester , and bring back any interests on the moon to Pasher . They find one survivor aboard the Harvester and successfully return with the ship, bringing along dead Fexian skullborer samples. They are paid from Fayet Kelen to upgrade the Desert Jewel in preparation for their mission to Denon.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY > Several weeks after the Battle of Yavin :: Nakari Kelen and Luke Skywalker travel to the planet Denon to liberate the Givin Drusil Bephorin , a cryptologist working for the Empire against her will, after it captured her family. They meet with their contact Sakhet , a Kupohan spy working for the Alliance. They are able to liberate Bephorin from Imperial custody, escaping ISB agents and leaving the planet.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY > Several weeks after the Battle of Yavin :: mission to Pasher .

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY > Several weeks after the Battle of Yavin :: Mission to Omereth .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY :: Han Solo and Chewbacca take a step back from the rebel cause and return to smuggling. However, when given an offer too good to refuse by Leia Organa, Solo agrees to rescue Alliance intelligence chief Airen Cracken 's rebel spies—a network of informants assembled in the span of years with only one objective who, after completing that objective one month ago, began to be murdered by a traitor .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY :: For the mission, Solo is to rescue the spies and capture the traitor while under the guise of competing in the Dragon Void race with the Millennium Falcon . With all of the spies aboard the Falcon , Solo competes in the race, though it is attacked by Imperial forces. Nevertheless, the Falcon perseveres, and its rebel crew discover the mole amongst them and detain the brainwashed traitor . While he could have gone through the race's endpoint—a gate that created a wormhole leading to the race's starting point—Solo instead allows Loo Re Anno , the last of her kind , to return home using the gate, which was created by Anno's kind for their exodus from the wider galaxy.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY :: Returning Han Solo's favor, Anno leads her people from between the seams of time and space to attack the Imperials pursuing the Dragon Void racers, allowing the latter to escape. Anno's people intends their return to be the last; before they leave the world forever.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY :: Having accomplished his mission, Solo returns to the Rebellion and realizes that he could not lie to himself and deny reality any longer; that he had to decide who he really is and what is worth living for. Han Solo thus chooses to remain with the Rebellion, reconciling his friendship with Leia Organa and renewing the spark of romance between them.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY :: A wedding is held at the mansion of Imperial Moff Jaccun Pereez between his daughter Famma and a rising young star of the Empire, Commander Yendiv Bensek .

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY :: Newly formed Inferno Squad undertakes a mission to the Tellik Four Station in order to recover and extract Bokk Naarg , a defecting member of the Partisans who offered intel to the Empire. They are unsuccessfull as three other partisans carry out a suicide bombing that kills Naarg. However Inferno Squad member Del Meeko 's ID10 seeker droid manages to recover Naarg's intel on a datachip.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY :: Inferno Squad infiltrates the Dreamers , a faction of Saw Gerrera's Partisans.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY :: The Dreamers undertake a mission to Affadar to ruin the reputation of Emoch Akagarti , ephor of the continent of Pammur on Affadar . He and his government had struck an alliance with the Empire to fund a mining operation on the planet, despite it having a detrimental effect on the quality of its water. He is brought to the Affadar water purification plant and recorded admitting the water was unsafe for consumption, with the plant not actually operating.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY :: Seyn Marana and Sadori Vushan of the Dreamers bomb an Imperial munitions factory on Anukara . The mission is planned to coincide with the grand opening of the factory, attended by Moff Rys Deksha and General Ivel Toshan , with both dying at the attack.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY :: Inferno Squad undertakes a mission to locate and neutralize individuals who were blackmailing an Imperial Moff .

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY :: The heroes of Yavin infiltrate the Imperial Weapons Factory Alpha on Cymoon 1 , with Han Solo posing as an emissary for Jabba the Hutt in a weapons deal with the Empire, and Luke Skywalker and Leia Organa posing as his bodyguards. Once inside the factory, the Rebels reveal themselves, attack the Imperials, free a number of slaves, and set the facility's power core to overload. Their plans change when Darth Vader arrives and orders the facility into full alert. The Rebels hijack an AT-AT to use in their escape, while Skywalker confronts Vader in lightsaber combat before the walker arrives. Solo crashes the AT-AT through the factory's walls, giving them an opening to escape the facility. The Rebels engage in combat with Vader and the Imperial forces, and the Dark Lord kills a number of the freed slaves. Skywalker takes out some of the Imperial forces using the weapons on a speeder bike, while Solo uses the AT-AT to fire on and injure Vader. The assault ends with the destruction of Weapons Factory Alpha, causing a significant setback in the operations of the Imperial Military, and the Rebels are able to escape.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY :: An Imperial Lambda -class shuttle carrying weapons and equipment supplied by Jabba the Hutt is attacked by a robotic CR90 corvette belonged to a group of pirates funded by the Crymorah Syndicate . Darth Vader arrives to save the shuttle, but it self-destructs as the crew killed themselves rather than let the pirates take the Empire's property. Vader, along with two other TIE fighters , succeeds in disabling the robotic pirate ship, and takes it captive in order to find the larger organization behind the attack.
