Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY :: With information recovered from the corvette, Vader and his forces identify and attack the pirate's base , destroying it and all its inhabitants. During the attack is discovered that the pirates are backed by the Crymorah Syndicate, and Vader is able to plant false evidence in the station's computer incriminating Lieutenant Oon-ai , who was serving as Grand General Cassio Tagge 's spy to Vader, as a traitor .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY :: Chelli Aphra steals the Triple-Zero protocol personality matrix stored in Quarantine World III on a mission from the Droid Gotra. Vader, who was tracking her after identifying her as the provider of droidekas owned by the crymorah-backed pirates , finds and recruits her.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY :: Having recruited Chelli Aphra and the assassin droids BT-1 and 0-0-0 to assist him in building an army of unquestionably loyal battle droids, Vader and his new team go on a mission to infiltrate the lair of Karina , a Geonosian queen who had utilized a droid factory to produce battle droids as her " children " after she was injured and left unable to produce offspring during the sterilization of Geonosis . They capture the droid factory and began producing BX-series droid commandos for Vader.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY :: Vader and his secret forces attack Doctor Cylo 's research base in order to discover Vader's rivals; soldiers engineered by Cylo to replace Vader, by orders of Palplatine.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY :: A few months after the Battle of Yavin
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY > A few months after the Battle of Yavin :: The Rebel Alliance launches a mission to rescue underground rebel leaders from the planet Giju , before Imperial agents arrive. Red Squadron , led by Commander Arhul Narra , escorts a transport away from the planet, and fights several TIE fighters, destroying some before making the jump to hyperspace.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY > A few months after the Battle of Yavin :: Luke Skywalker embarks on a mission to recover Imperial communication logs , but he is ambushed in the Tertiary Usaita system , forcing him to postpone his mission and return to Devaron to repair his ship.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY > A few months after the Battle of Yavin :: At Devaron , Luke Skywalker engages in his first duel with a lightsaber in the Temple of Eedit .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY :: Hired by Vader to uncover Luke Skywalker's identity, Boba Fett travels to Tatooine and duels the aspiring Jedi there.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY :: Vader attacks the Son-tuul Pride on the planet Son-tuul , storming its base with stormtroopers and executing its leadership. The imperals discover a fortune amassed over the years, which they confiscate. Afterwards, Vader met with Son-tuul Pride competitor Sutha , who served Jabba the Hutt. They agree upon a mutually beneficial relationship between the Empire and the Hutt Cartel, with the Empire eliminating competitors to open up new markets in return for the Hutts supplying the Empire.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY :: The Imperial light cruiser transporting the fortune confiscated to the Son-tuul Pride is Son-Tuul Pride robbed by Aphra and bounty hunters Krrsantan , Bossk , IG-90 , and Beebox . Aphra is send by Vader in order to use the money for his own forces.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY :: Imperial forces led by inspector Thanoth attack the Plasma Devils , an Alliance Starfighter Corps squadron, in the Anthan system . Almost all rebels are killed.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY :: Luke Skywalker, Han Solo, and Chewbacca travel to the planet Rion to negotiate supplies for the Rebellion from the Besalisk Razian . Solo wins the Rion Swoop race and use the winnings to give to Razian for the supplies and future commissions.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY :: Vader travels to the mining planet of Shu-Torun in order to ensure that ore quotas for Imperial building projects would be fulfilled. During the visit the King of Shu-Torun try to have Vader killed, but dies himself at the hands of BT-1 and 0-0-0. Vader then crownes the king's youngest and only surviving child, Princess Trios as the new queen, leaving her to see that future quotas are met.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY :: Leia Organa and Luke Skywalker go on diplomatic mission to the planet Tibrin . Posing as diplomats from Naboo's House Berenko seeking to negotiate a trade deal, they aim to rally the native Ishi Tib to the Rebellion's cause.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY :: Leia Organa, Han Solo and Sana Starros escape naval forces of the Galactic Empire during a dogfight in the Monsua Nebula .

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY :: Luke Skywalker is rescued from the custody of Grakkus the Hutt at Hutta Town on Nar Shaddaa, and Imperial forces led by Sergeant Kreel seeking to capture him, by the Heores of Yavin and Sana Starros.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0 ABY :: The Battle of Vrogas Vas is fought between Vader and his secret forces , Imperial forces led by Commander Karbin , and the Alliance to Restore the Republic. Using intelligence provided by Aphra, Vader travels and crash-lands on the planet Vrogas Vas in search of Luke Skywalker. After inflicting heavy casualties on the rebels, Vader is attacked by Karbin and his forces, who wanted Skywalker for himself in order to curry the Emperor's favor. With the help of Aphra, Vader manages to defeat and kill Karbin, meanwhile the rebels escape offworld.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) :: c. 0–1 ABY
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > c. 0–1 ABY :: Rebel spy Eneb Ray leads a failed plot to assassinate Palpatine, after Ray infiltrates the Arrth-Eno Prison Complex on Coruscant to free a group of Rebel-sympathetic senators scheduled for execution, and learns the Emperor was planning on being at the prison. Ray and six other Rebel spies on Coruscant attack and kill a man who was in reality a body double ; the plot was a trap by the Emperor to kill the Rebel spies on Coruscant and the senators, and achieve a propaganda victory against the Rebellion. All the spies and senators are killed except for Ray.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > c. 0–1 ABY :: Eneb Ray carries out a prison siege at the Alliance's Sunspot Prison in retaliation for the deaths of the pro-rebel senators and Alliance operatives during the Arrth-Eno Mission, seeking to kill all Imperial prisoners holded in the station. He and his droids are thwarted by Leia Organa, Sana Starros, and Chelli Aphra. However, numerous Imperial prisoners are killed and the Alliance abandons the prison.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > c. 0–1 ABY :: Queen Trios , the recently appointed monarch of the Kingdom of Shu-Torun , controlled by the Galactic Empire, faces an uprising among the ore-dukes of her homeworld, leading to a planetary rebellion known as the Shu-Torun War .

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > c. 0–1 ABY > Queen Trios , the recently appointed monarch of the Kingdom of Shu-Torun , controlled by the Galactic Empire, faces an uprising among the ore-dukes of her homeworld, leading to a planetary rebellion known as the Shu-Torun War . :: Imperial forces battle a delving citadel controlled by the rebellious ore-dukes. By orders of Vader, the cyborg Tulon Voidgazer destroys the citadel using explosive drones, much to the horror of Queen Trios.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > c. 0–1 ABY > Queen Trios , the recently appointed monarch of the Kingdom of Shu-Torun , controlled by the Galactic Empire, faces an uprising among the ore-dukes of her homeworld, leading to a planetary rebellion known as the Shu-Torun War . :: Forces of the rebellious ore-dukes attack Queen Trios and her Imperial allies in the ancestral retreat of the Shu-Torun royalty . Vader boards the dukes' vessel, the Lava Leviathan , and sinks it.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > c. 0–1 ABY > Queen Trios , the recently appointed monarch of the Kingdom of Shu-Torun , controlled by the Galactic Empire, faces an uprising among the ore-dukes of her homeworld, leading to a planetary rebellion known as the Shu-Torun War . :: In the final battle of the war, Queen Trios leads her loyalist forces in an attack on the delving citadel of Duke Rubix , leader of the ore-dukes rebelling against her. With Imperial reforces, the side of Trios quickly claims victory. After Rubix surrenders, Trios executes him and names his daughter Bixene as successor to his position.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > c. 0–1 ABY :: Doctor Cylo and his fleet attempt to hijack the Executor , an Executor -class Star Dreadnought , while it is still in construction in the shipyards of Kuat Drive Yards .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > c. 0–1 ABY :: On the Ghost Moon , Imperial Task Force 99 led by Sergeant Kreel shoots down a Rebel Taylander shuttle , crashing it on the moon. The surviving Rebels are attacked and killed except for Admiral Verette , who is captured for interrogation.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > c. 0–1 ABY :: Rebel Alliance members, including the Heroes of Yavin and Red Squadron , take control of the Imperial I -class Star Destroyer Harbinger , after attacking it and forcing it's crew to abandon ship.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > c. 0–1 ABY :: After learning that the people of Tureen VII was providing aid to the Alliance, the Empire launches a siege on the planet by setting up a blockade in orbit and establishing bases on the planet's twin moons. The Alliance attempts three times unsuccessfully to break through the Imperial blockade.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > c. 0–1 ABY :: Leia Organa escapes from the Empire on the planet Skorii-Lei with the help of the engineer Pash Davane .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > c. 0–1 ABY :: Luke Skywalker undertakes a mission to the stonepower-strong world Yoda once journeyed to following the writings of Obi-Wan Kenobi; the Jedi-aspirant confronts Garro , a selfish rockhawker left bitter, power-hungry, and hoarding stonepower following Yoda's visit. Skywalker convinces Garro to let go of his power and sacrifice for his planet, restoring the balance of life.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) :: c. 1 ABY
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > c. 1 ABY :: Aphra raids the Great Temple on Yavin 4 at the behest of her father, Korin Aphra , in order to obtain coordinates which he believed would lead to the lost Ordu Aspectu at the Citadel of Rur . However, when Aphra's crew arrives at the Massassi Site , they find it occupied and under investigation by Imperial forces under the command of Captain Magna Tolvan .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > c. 1 ABY :: The twilight of the Ordu Aspectu occurs; Chelli Aphra obtains the Rur Crystal , which contains the artificial intelligence of Rur.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > c. 1 ABY :: Aphra and Luke Skywalker embark on a mission to the Citadel of Ktath'atn in order to seek an audience with the Queen of Ktath'atn .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > c. 1 ABY :: Aphra's auction of Rur occurs; the artificial intelligence of Rur, the self-proclaimed "Eternal Rur," escapes from captivity.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > c. 1 ABY :: The Imperial skirmish aboard the Sorca Retreat occurs; many wealthy and influencial parties are slaughtered by Rur and Imperial forces. Darth Vader confronts Rur as Aphra escapes.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > c. 1 ABY :: Leia Organa and Luke Skywalker escape from an ocean planet , after they sought to draw Imperial forces away from a peaceful species that were caught in the middle of the war.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > c. 1 ABY :: Han Solo and Chewbacca deliver Grakkus the Hutt to a Alliance holding facility on the planet Akiva .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > c. 1 ABY :: Task Force 99 goes on a mission to Horox III to destroy a rebel outpost , killing all rebels there.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > c. 1 ABY :: R2-D2 concludes his successful mission to rescue C-3PO from an Imperial Star Destroyer , after he was captured by SCAR Squadron during the siege on Tureen VII .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > c. 1 ABY :: Imperial stormtroopers on Zeitooine arrest a local family after the parents violate a ban on independent publications, forcibly dragging them out of their home. When the mother pleads for the stormtroopers not to imprison her daughters, the captain slamms his blaster rifle into her face while another trooper cuffes her.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > c. 1 ABY :: The Imperial Star Destroyer Devastator conducts an evacuation of the Imperial garrisons on Ivarujar , a planet undergoing a massive volcanic eruption. Lieutenant Commander Ciena Ree evacuates a stormtrooper garrison in the capital city and manages to evacuate forty civilians before the city is completely overrun by lava.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > c. 1 ABY :: The battle for Jedha occurs.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) :: 1 ABY

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 1 ABY :: The Alliance considers Crait as its potential central base, and the mission to Crait occurs.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 1 ABY :: Assault on the Mako-Ta Space Docks occurs. Rogue Squadron is formed, and a large portion of the Alliance Fleet is lost when Darth Vader's Death Squadron attacks the Mako-Ta Space Docks . Alliance High Command suffers heavy casualties.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) :: 1–3 ABY
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 1–3 ABY :: The Heroes of Yavin are stranded on the moon Hubin , and are taken in by Thane Markona and his clan ; they eventually escape, leading to an attack on an Imperial Impound Yard .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 1–3 ABY :: The rebels conduct a mission to Shu-Torun ; seeking revenge, Leia Organa plans to bring the traitor, Queen Trios , to ruin. Beginning with Trios' Empire-allied world, Organa found her and her own forces' mistakes after dueling Trios, who died fighting against the vengeful Partisans' threat of destroying her world. Ultimately, Organa's team acts to save Shu-Torun, and the Heroes of Yavin emerge victorious.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 1–3 ABY :: Han Solo and Leia Organa undertake a mission to the Core World Lanz Carpo to trick the Empire into suspecting the Carpo Crime Syndicate is working with the Rebellion.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 1–3 ABY :: Luke Skywalker and R2-D2 undertake a mission to protect a rebel refueling station on Sergia in the Inner Rim.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 1–3 ABY :: Chewbacca and C-3PO undertake a mission to K43 on the edge of Wild Space to divert the Imperial Starfleet.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 1–3 ABY :: Members of the Rebel Alliance gather on a new base on Hoth , the sixth planet in the Hoth system . The rebels' Echo Base is constructed over the course of two years up until the Battle of Hoth in 3 ABY. Months before the battle, Rogue Squadron begins to be stationed at Echo Base.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) :: 0–3 ABY
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 0–3 ABY :: Following an outbreak at a secret Imperial research facility on the southern continent of the planet Dandoran , where a bioweapon nicknamed the Sickness had been created, an undead stormtroopers invasion starts.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) :: 2 ABY
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Renewed hope (0–2 ABY) > 2 ABY :: Poe Dameron is born to rebels Shara Bey and Kes Dameron .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) :: 3 ABY
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Echo Base is established inside a mountain on Hoth by the Rebel Alliance. The base is expanded after scouting and surveying work was done on several locations within Hoth's temperate zone.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: The Rebel Alliance launches a campaign against the Empire in the Mid Rim Territories . The rebels bring the fight to numerous worlds in the area, but are forced to halt when their fleet becomes overstretched and their army commanders fail to sufficiently coordinate on a sector level.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > The Rebel Alliance launches a campaign against the Empire in the Mid Rim Territories . The rebels bring the fight to numerous worlds in the area, but are forced to halt when their fleet becomes overstretched and their army commanders fail to sufficiently coordinate on a sector level. :: The Alliance's 61st Mobile Infantry , led by Captain Micha Evon , fights in the factory-deserts of the planet Phorsa Gedd .

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > The Rebel Alliance launches a campaign against the Empire in the Mid Rim Territories . The rebels bring the fight to numerous worlds in the area, but are forced to halt when their fleet becomes overstretched and their army commanders fail to sufficiently coordinate on a sector level. :: The Empire regains territory in the Mid Rim, pushing out the Alliance. The rebels decide to stop their offensive, and the Mid Rim Retreat begins. Some Mid Rim revolutionaries follow the Alliance, though many stay to continue their struggle locally.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > The Rebel Alliance launches a campaign against the Empire in the Mid Rim Territories . The rebels bring the fight to numerous worlds in the area, but are forced to halt when their fleet becomes overstretched and their army commanders fail to sufficiently coordinate on a sector level. :: Imperial forces attack Alliance personnel on Ord Tiddell as they evacuate the planet. Rebel agent Caluan Ematt makes it out on the last transport. As they lift off, he sees the Empire light up the launchpad with plasmic artillery.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > The Rebel Alliance launches a campaign against the Empire in the Mid Rim Territories . The rebels bring the fight to numerous worlds in the area, but are forced to halt when their fleet becomes overstretched and their army commanders fail to sufficiently coordinate on a sector level. :: The Sixty-First takes control of the Ducal Palace and the Chenodra spaceport on the planet Bamayar.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > The Rebel Alliance launches a campaign against the Empire in the Mid Rim Territories . The rebels bring the fight to numerous worlds in the area, but are forced to halt when their fleet becomes overstretched and their army commanders fail to sufficiently coordinate on a sector level. :: Attack on Haidoral Prime ; Imperial Governor and Ruling Council member Everi Chalis defects to the Rebel Alliance.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > The Rebel Alliance launches a campaign against the Empire in the Mid Rim Territories . The rebels flee the battle before Imperial reinforcements can arrive.

The rebels bring the fight to numerous worlds in the area, but are forced to halt when their fleet becomes overstretched and their army commanders fail to sufficiently coordinate on a sector level. :: The 61st is send by the Rebel Alliance to protect the Coyerti and secure their world in an effort to distract Imperial forces from the rebel withdrawal.

The rebels bring the fight to numerous worlds in the area, but are forced to halt when their fleet becomes overstretched and their army commanders fail to sufficiently coordinate on a sector level. > The 61st is send by the Rebel Alliance to protect the Coyerti and secure their world in an effort to distract Imperial forces from the rebel withdrawal. :: Two squads, led by First Sergeant Hazram Namir and Sergeant Zab , launch an attack on an Imperial scout post . They manage to overrun the defending Imperial forces and take the camp before retreating to avoid incoming TIE bombers.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > The Rebel Alliance launches a campaign against the Empire in the Mid Rim Territories . The rebels bring the fight to numerous worlds in the area, but are forced to halt when their fleet becomes overstretched and their army commanders fail to sufficiently coordinate on a sector level. > The 61st is send by the Rebel Alliance to protect the Coyerti and secure their world in an effort to distract Imperial forces from the rebel withdrawal. :: The 61st assaults a strategic Imperial fortification. Consisting of at least a dozen squads, the plan is devised by one of the squad leaders, Carver . Following a lengthy effort to take out an Imperial AT-ST, Namir and his squad watch the flashes of the battle erupt from afar. The attack subsequently ends in a victory for the Sixty-First.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > The Rebel Alliance launches a campaign against the Empire in the Mid Rim Territories . The rebels bring the fight to numerous worlds in the area, but are forced to halt when their fleet becomes overstretched and their army commanders fail to sufficiently coordinate on a sector level. > The 61st is send by the Rebel Alliance to protect the Coyerti and secure their world in an effort to distract Imperial forces from the rebel withdrawal. :: Twilight Company attacks Coyerti's Distillery, a biotoxin and chemical refining facility and hub of the Empire's production on the planet. Rebel soldier Brand plants a microdetonator on one of the chemical barrels. When it explodes, it causes a sufficient distraction for the members of the 61st to attack, killing the remaining stormtrooper guard. The rebels enter the Distillery and clear the bunkers, placing detonators as they go. Once the rebel troops exite the distillery, they set off the detonators and destroy it.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > The Rebel Alliance launches a campaign against the Empire in the Mid Rim Territories . The rebels bring the fight to numerous worlds in the area, but are forced to halt when their fleet becomes overstretched and their army commanders fail to sufficiently coordinate on a sector level. > The 61st is send by the Rebel Alliance to protect the Coyerti and secure their world in an effort to distract Imperial forces from the rebel withdrawal. :: During the final actions of the campaign, Sergeant Maximian Ajax 's squad is pinned down by an AT-ST while fighting the planet's Imperial garrison , the X-wings unable to fly low enough enough to shoot the vehicle. They are saved by the Coyerti people, who storm in great numbers and destroy the Imperial garrison.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > The Rebel Alliance launches a campaign against the Empire in the Mid Rim Territories . The rebels bring the fight to numerous worlds in the area, but are forced to halt when their fleet becomes overstretched and their army commanders fail to sufficiently coordinate on a sector level. :: Rebel groups are sent throughout the Mid Rim to conduct missions designed to distract Imperials from the Mid Rim Retreat. The Twenty-First travel to Bestine IV on one such mission.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: At some point before the Battle of Hoth
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > At some point before the Battle of Hoth :: The Hidden Hand crime syndicate puts together a crew—amongst them Arr-Nine-Nineteen , Dengar , Chio Fain , Gita , Honnah , and Beilert Valance—to hunt for Vader . The crew fails with the betrayal of Dengar and the Hidden Hand is silenced.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > At some point before the Battle of Hoth :: Valance arranges for his people on Chorin , including his lover, Yuralla Vega , to be sheltered by rebels on Lowik in exchange for supplying the Hand's weapons to the Rebellion.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Weeks before the Battle of Hoth
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Weeks before the Battle of Hoth :: Luke Skywalker begins to fly snowspeeders with Dak Ralter at Hoth's Echo Base.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Weeks before the Battle of Hoth :: Death Squadron is misled in their search for the Rebellion's main base of operations by Chelli Lona Aphra to the long-lost Jedi world of Tython . With the Empire redirected from probing Hoth, the Rebel Alliance is given an extra few weeks to prepare for an imminent Imperial intrusion.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Weeks before the Battle of Hoth :: An Imperial Star Destroyer, following a hypermatter trail from the 61st's corvette Thunderstrike , ambushes the rebels in a lifeless star system in the Metatessu sector , but they are able to escape.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Weeks before the Battle of Hoth :: An Imperial light cruiser, backed by a squadron of TIE fighters, attacks the starships of the 61st. However, the 61st obliterates the Imperial forces, wiping out the entire crew of the cruiser.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Weeks before the Battle of Hoth :: The 61st starships are attacked by around a dozen TIE Interceptors, who damage the starboard side of the corvette before the 61st leaves the system, discovering the hypermatter leak.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Weeks before the Battle of Hoth :: The Sixty-First, in order to obtain the resources needed for the repairs of the Thunderstrike , travel to the Redhurne system , where they raid an Imperial heavy freighter by launching boarding pods at the ship, each one carrying a squad of Rebel troopers. They tear through the defending stormtroopers while Sergeant Fektrin leads a trio of engineers to extract the resources they need. An Imperial Gozanti-class cruiser arrives in the system, forcing the rebel soldiers to get back to their pods. Once all of the survivors have returned, the Thunderstrike and Apailana's Promise jump out of the system.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Darth Vader leads an invasion aimed at destroying the Rebel Alliance's Echo Base hidden on the remote ice world of Hoth and capturing Luke Skywalker. The base's location was discovered when a Viper probe droid , deployed by Darth Vader's Death Squadron , landed on the planet, prompting the Rebels to begin an evacuation. The Imperial attack force consisted of primarily AT-AT walkers , commanded by General Maximilian Veers . His army is tasked with destroying Echo Base's main power generator to allow orbital bombardment of the planet and for Darth Vader to arrive and lead ground forces into the base. Spearheading the defense of the generator are the T-47 airspeeders of Rogue Squadron , commanded by Luke Skywalker. However, the snowspeeders do not have the necessary firepower to bring down the walkers, so Skywalker suggests to trip the walkers with the tow cables issued to every snowspeeder. The power generator is destroyed by Veers' main AT-AT . The remaining base personnel proceedes to evacuate, as the 501st Legion , led by Vader, enters the base. The overwhelming Imperial invasion forces the Rebellion to scatter their fleet across the galaxy.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Following the Battle of Hoth, General Octavion Sorin of the Imperial Army is tasked with hunting down the remaining rebels left behind on the planet. One squad manages to fend off Sorin's forces and escape the planet on a stolen Imperial shuttle they stole from Sorin. The Rebel squad flees to Haven , a village on the planet Ison , but are tracked by Sorin. Believing the defenseless village to be the rebel stronghold, Sorin prepares to leave Hoth and jump to Ison. Finding an Imperial transceiver on the shuttle, the rebels discover Sorin's preparations to attack Haven. The group returns to Echo Base and operate the still functioning ion cannon in an attempt to destroy Sorin's Star Destroyer before it leaves for Haven. They temporaly disable the shields, so are able to fly the shuttle to the Star Destroyer's exposed launch bay, crashing along the hangar floor. However, the squad is too late and the ships jumps to hyperspace.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Above Ison, the rebels make their way to the bridge, where they confront Sorin. He escapes as the squad takes control of the bridge and crash the Star Destroyer onto the planet's surface. Having landed outside the gates of Haven, the rebels exit the ship and take one final stand against General Sorin and his forces. The group fendesd off waves of Imperial troops, including Sorin's experimental Mark III Repulsor Tank. They eventually defeat Sorin's forces, and kill the General, saving Haven from destruction.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: The Thunderstrike and the Apailana's Promise arrive at a rendezvous in the Elochar sector with a flotilla of a dozen rebel ships, including the Sixmoon . The light freighter Trumpet's Call jumps to the flotilla from a battle, where Imperial spies have secretly replaced the crew. The ship's life support had failed along the way leaving the rebel crew dead with an Imperial infiltration team disguised as injured crew. Members of the 61st rescue the Imperial spies and take them onto the Thunderstrike . Inside, the spies break loose and storm the bridge, killing Lieutenant Sairgon , Commander Paonu and the rest of the bridge crew. However, Sairgon had already sent a message about the infiltration and all of the other ships except the Apailana's Promise scatter to other star systems. Brand and other twilight members take back control before the Imperial II -class Star Destroyer Herald arrives, and the 61st flees the system.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: By around this point, Lando Calrissian had freed the enslaved people held at the Fantanine mining colony .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Following advice from the Force spirit of Obi-Wan Kenobi, Luke Skywalker heads to Dagobah to begin his training with Jedi Master Yoda.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Darth Vader hires bounty hunters Boba Fett, Bossk, Dengar, IG-88B , Zuckuss , and 4-LOM to track down the Millennium Falcon , which travels across the Anoat sector , eventually reaching Bespin 's Cloud City from the Hoth asteroid belt .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Vader sets a trap for Skywalker on the Cloud City. He captures Han Solo and Leia Organa to torture them, hoping to lure Skywalker out of hiding. He sees a vision of his friends' suffering while on Dagobah, while training with Master Yoda, and rushes to Cloud City to help them, despite the objections of both Yoda and Obi-Wan Kenobi.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Darth Vader had planned to put Skywalker in carbonite , in order to be delivered to the Emperor personally. Hoping to avoid his death, Vader chooses Solo as a test subject for the modified freezing chamber . Solo, Organa, Chewbacca, and C-3PO are brought to the carbon-freezing facility, despite Chewbacca's attempt to set him free, Solo insists there will be another time to rescue him. Solo is then frozen, and found to have survived. Afterwards, Vader is informed that Skywalker's X-wing has just landed.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Skywalker makes his way to the carbon-freezing chamber and finds Vader waiting for him, and the two engage in a lightsaber duel throughout the chamber. The duel progresses through the city's processing vane and onto a maintenance catwalk, where Vader disarmes Skywalker by slicing off his weapon hand. He then temptes the Jedi apprentice to join him and embrace the dark side. When Luke refuses, Vader reveals that he is the boy's own father, Anakin Skywalker. Rather than allowing himself to be captured, Skywalker throws himself off the platform where they had fought, before landing and hanging helplessly at the bottom of the city.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Han Solo is taken by Boba Fett to be delivered to Jabba the Hutt. Calrissian issues a Code Force Seven and arranges for the Bespin Wing Guard to ambush the Imperial team escorting himself, Organa, Chewbacca, and C-3PO to Vader's ship. They rush to intercept Fett, reaching his landing platform just in time to see the Slave I take off, so decide to go for the Falcon and escape the city. Leia hears Luke pleading for help and orders the ship around. Finding Skywalker dangling from the vane, they pull up beneath it, and Calrissian pulls him to safety.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Before leaving, Calrissian manages to warn the citizens of Cloud City about the recent arrival of Imperial forces, causing many to panic and flee. The city is occupied by the Empire, with several trades, such as cybernetic implants, being suppressed, and a skeleton garrison stationed there.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Darth Vader embarks on a quest for revenge against those who hid his son Luke Skywalker from him. With the forensics droid ZED-6-7 , Vader returns to the Lars moisture farm and later to Padmé Amidala's apartment on Coruscant for clues.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: " The Contessa " is faced with rebuilding her rebel Corona Squadron after it fought at the Battle of Hoth and regrouped aboard the Liberty . Tasked by Mon Mothma to organize the starfighter defense at Rendezvous Point Delta-Three , "the Contessa" assigns Wedge Antilles to reform Red Squadron from Rogue Squadron veterans and other individuals.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Under Commander Wedge Antilles, Red Squadron attacks a pirate base , whose proximity to the Alliance Fleet's rendezvous point following the evacuation of Hoth threatened their safety. The base and all pirate forces are destroyed.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Dengar gambles with and is indebted to the Ohnaka Gang , whose original leader, Hondo Ohnaka , had been replaced by Skragg .

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Having cracked the Rebel Alliance's codes, which were top secret and known only to a few individuals, such as R2-D2, the Empire tracks down rebels regrouping from the Battle of Hoth who initiated communications with other Alliance divisions.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: The Falcon arrives at a battle at Rendezvous Point Delta-Three . The new arrivals aide in the rebels' escape from Imperial forces led by Ellian Zahra, who was reassigned by Darth Vader and the Emperor from a non-essential post at the spice mines of Kessel to hunt down remnants of the Rebellion. Following the incident, Commander Zahra received explicit instructions from Lord Vader: Luke Skywalker was not to be harmed.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: After taking a fork-nosed ice worm fossil from Imperial forces on Hoth to sell to the Shadow University through Sava Estat , Chelli Lona Aphra embarks on a job to find the Rings of Vaale, which allegedly grants eternal fortune, immortality, and abilities to rival the most powerful Force user when used together. Doctor Aphra, archaeology student Detta Yao , the bounty hunter Black Krrsantan, and the mercenary " Just Lucky " pursue the rings against Ronen Tagge , scion of the Empire's aristocratic Tagge family . After losing years of research to Tagge at the Ruins of Kolkur , Professor Eustacia Okka and her droid, TA-418 , who had done research on the rings, join Aphra aboard the Ark Angel III , traveling to Vaale, Dianth , the home of the The Architects of Vaale who made the rings. They discover murals of High Republic iconography warning that when used independently, one ring would grant eternal life at the cost of the user's madness, while another would grant fortune at the cost of hastening the user's eventual demise.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: With the assistance of Just Lucky, Tagge's mercenaries trace Aphra's crew to Vaale, taking the Ring of Fortune before destroying the city of living bone with Aphra and her crew captured. Aphra's crew is taken to Ronen Tagge's Private Penthouse in Canto Bight , where they defeat Ronen, who was wearing the two rings, and escape from after stealing several priceless artifacts.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Nakano Lash resurfaces near Kessel. Having been informed by Aphra and Black Krrsantan on Burnin Konn that she has resurfaced, Bossk races to find Lash. The latter is also sought by her former protégé, Beilert Valance, as well as Boba Fett, who deviated from delivering Han Solo in carbonite to settle a personal score with Lash. Lash had torn apart the group of hunters years ago on Corellia, when she killed their client and the sole heir of the Mourner's Wail Syndicate , Khamus , as well as causing the death of Krynthia , the sole heir of the Unbroken Clan , instigating a continuation of violence between the two groups, which formed the three crime syndicates of Corellia with the addition of Lady Proxima 's White Worms.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Leaving her lover, Losha , and her peaceful life on the Moon of Logal Ri behind, T'onga pursues Lash to avenge the death of her brother, T'ongor , while Thisspiasian hunter Ooris Bynar pursues Lash with the help of the bondsman Syphacc .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Amanaman agrees to notify Lando Calrissian should Boba Fett appear at Jabba's Palace. Jabba himself forces Calrissian to agree to spy on the Rebellion on his behalf with the threat of death.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Having deduced alongside Commander Grek's Fourth Division they have saved at Rendezvous Point Delta-Three that the Empire was using Alliance codes to hunt down rebels at rendezvous points, the crew of the Millennium Falcon leave the EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate Redemption at Backup Rendezvous Point Gamma-Nine to return to Cloud City.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: While the Liberty and Corona Squadron, previously at Rendezvous Point Delta-Three, did not join the rebels at Backup Rendezvous Point Gamma-Nine. Instead, Corona Squadron undertake a series of reconnaissance missions for the Rebellion, charting the planet D'Qar shortly before the Battle of Endor. D'Qar would house only a small Alliance outpost during the Galactic Civil War before becoming the base of the Resistance decades later.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Luke Skywalker seeks and fails to find his lost lightsaber, whilst Leia Organa, in her resolve to rescue Han Solo, infiltrates and is captured by the local Imperial garrison in her quest to learn more of the carbon-freezing mechanism. Meanwhile, Lando Calrissian finds his friend Lobot and sabotages the surrounding area of refined tibanna.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Skywalker lets go of his weapon following a vision through the Force. He then joins with Calrissian and Lobot to rescue Leia Organa from the Empire. In doing so, they accidentally free the Bespin Wing Guard , who are admitted into the Rebel Alliance.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Luke Skywalker parts ways with the crew of the Millennium Falcon . With R2-D2, he travels to the planet he envisioned earlier in search of a woman , whom Skywalker also witnessed in his vision, for help in his Jedi future and the fate of the Rebellion.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Skywalker meets Verla, a former disciple the Jedi Ferren Barr who rejected the Jedi, the Sith, and the Force in order to live her life. On Luke Skywalker's promise that he would not disturb her again be it the will of the Force, Verla gives the aspiring Jedi the location of a yellow-bladed lightsaber for him to claim for the extinct Jedi Order. After a duel of wills against the phantom Grand Inquisitor, who found himself tied to a fate worse than death as he is bound by Darth Vader to the mortal realm despite dying years ago over Mustafar, Skywalker asserts his understanding that the Jedi believed in self-sacrifice; compassion—dying for a greater cause.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Darth Vader and ZED-6-7 trail a transmitter from Padmé Amidala's apartment and travel to Vendaxa after being led to a false finding of Luke Skywalker. On Vendaxa, the pair encounter a group of Amidalans —individuals seeking to avenge the mysterious death of Padmé Amidala — being attacked by native fauna whilst investigating the cause of Amidala's death.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Darth Vader and his droid finds the group's sole survivor: Sabé, a handmaiden of the late Padmé Amidala. Sabé's group intent on avenging Amidala's death, Vader tells the handmaiden that he as well sought revenge on those who hid the cause of Amidala's death, and Sabé joins Vader and leads him to Naboo, where she had hidden the security recordings taken from Amidala's apartment.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Now on a hunt for the truth of Padmé Amidala's death, Darth Vader comes across Sabé's associates, former Royal Naboo Security Forces guards of Amidala Captains Gregar Typho and Tonra, who long suspected the senator was murdered after leaving for Mustafar during the last moments of the Clone Wars. With their presumption that Darth Vader had killed Padmé Amidala and Anakin Skywalker confirmed by the Sith Lord himself, Sabé's group ambush him, though their confrontation is interrupted by an elder sando aqua monster .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Darth Vader slays the sando aqua monster after fleeing with ZED-6-7, thereby increasing the species' chances of extinction within a century to eighty-three percent. Vader then leads his death trooper unit to battle the Amidalans, who guarded the tomb of Padmé Amidala under the leadership of Ric Olié . With memories of the group's leader teaching Anakin Skywalker to operate Amidala's Royal Naboo Starship surfacing, Vader spares Olié and proceeds to Amidala's tomb, although he is stopped by Sabé and other handmaidens of the former Naboo Queen and senator. Unable to kill them, he immobilizes Padmé Amidala's bodyguards and enters her tomb.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Darth Vader and ZED-6-7 trace a med implant at Amidala's tomb to Polis Massa, which had been attacked and abandoned by this point. Vader and his droid are followed and attacked by the Amidalans, who are defeated. Finding a holorecording of his wife's death at the maternity ward, Vader destroys ZED-6-7 in his rage before wallowing in grief, hearing his lover's last words that there is still good in Anakin Skywalker as he returns to the Imperial Palace on Coruscant, where he submits to the Emperor.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Mourner's Wail kills Unbroken Clan soldiers in a bombing on Chorgad . In retaliation, General Vukorah of the clan attacks a Mourner's Wail spice run in the Kessel Sector , prompting the latter group to bomb a Caviness IV settlement.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Ooris Bynar is killed by Nakano Lash after tracing the latter to Ruusan , and Bossk is left on the graveyard world of Galmerah by Boba Fett. Valance and T'onga reach Lash, who reveals that she only killed Khamus because they were sent to murder a pregnant Krynthia. Lash further reveals that she had been raising Khamus and Krynthia's child, Cadeliah , in the hope that she would unite the two warring syndicates as their joint heir.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: While T'onga's lets go of her desire for vengeance, she is mortally wounded by Boba Fett, who fulfills his desire to arrange Lash's death by leaving her on her starship , which is destroyed by Vukorah of the Unbroken Clan. Fett escapes aboard the Slave I , and Valance vows to protect Cadeliah as per Lash's dying wish, taking his ship, the Broken Wing , alongside Cadeliah.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Vukorah finishes purging those loyal to the old line of the Unbroken Clan and hires Zuckuss and 4-LOM to hunt down Valance and Cadeliah to secure her own place as leader of the clan.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Remy delivers a replica of the Nihil Path engine to De'Rruyet Industries on Corellia before assisting Sana Starros with a drop-off on the world.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: The Gortus 4 colony signs over rights to their moon, prompting the Tagge Construction Division to be tasked with establishing an ore factory within a week.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: The strikes on the Keshk lunar colony continue for the eighth day as a result of wage disputes with the Keshk Corporation .

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Beol De'Rruyet reveals that his De'Rruyet Industries has found a way to replicate the Nihil Path engine with the potential to completely change the galactic economy or end the galactic war.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Lucky reports Ronen's fall to Lady Domina Tagge , who captures Aphra to Tagge Space after bounty hunter pursuits through Taris , Burnin Konn , and Arveesh Station . The head of Tagge Corporation hires Aphra to gather more information on the De'Rruyet Path engine replica.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Aphra enlists the help of Sana Starros to gain an audience with Lady Proxima at the Den of the White Worms on Corellia, who directs them to the Unbroken Clan. After clan leader Vukorah finished interrogating Remy, Aphra and Starros steal a datapad containing a transcript of Remy's interrogation, following a lead to an ancient battlefield on Dol'har Hyde . There, Aphra and Starros are attacked by Vukorah and the Unbroken Clan.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Working alongside "Just Lucky" in Canto Bight, Ariole Yu offers to let Koz go if the latter pays his debt to Wen Delphis , leader of the Sixth Kin , although Kin enforcers are ambushed by traitors. With treason apparent amongst the ranks of the Kin, Wen Delphis sends "Just Lucky" and Ariole Yu to eliminate the alleged traitor Gallin Crae and his contact off-world.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Emperor Palpatine instructs Domina Tagge to capture Beol De'Rruyet's Nihil Path engine replica. Aided by Lapin Tagge , Domina Tagge discovers that slicers had stolen confidential data from TaggeCo as well as its competitors.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: For wallowing in grief, Vader is deposited by Darth Sidious to the black sands of Mustafar, on the spot where the Sith apprentice was immolated decades prior. To turn Vader's anger back into fear and the fear into pain, Sidious sends Ochi of Bestoon , assassin of the Sith , as a reminder.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: The Eye of Webbish Bog calls out to Vader, reminding the cyborg of his inner conflicts between his personal desire for power, his current servitude to the Sith and destined servitude to the Force, and his personal needs for family. After receiving a wayfinder to Exegol from the Eye, Vader defeats Ochi's droid army and departs Mustafar holding Ochi captive in an escape pod retrofitted onto his green Jedi starfighter .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Darth Vader and Ochi reach the vicinity of Exegol, where they fly through an Imperial blockade led by Administrator Sly Moore as well as a creature of a summa-verminoth sub-species . After receiving a vision of his son, Luke Skywalker, striking him down and taking his place at the Emperor's side, Vader makes a forbidden use of the Force to subdue the beast, thereby violating his master's command. Through fear of the Emperor's plans, Vader had become angered and was fueled by hatred, leading to a demonstration of power whereby the cyborg gained control over the beast and rode it to Exegol's Sith Citadel .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Vader confronts his master but is subdued by pain. Vader's beast lies dead, and Ochi loses his eyes; overwhelmed by the power of the Emperor, Vader chooses to yield to his Master. Vader, the Emperor, Ochi, Sly Moore, and Mas Amedda then leave Exegol—leaving its Sith cultists , minions, experiments, and a growing fleet of planet-destroying Star Destroyers . Dathan escapes from Exegol by hiding in Vader's shuttle.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: The Rebellion's Fourth Division joins forces with the Seventh Division after battling Ellian Zahra's assault group at Elessia .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Operation Starlight begins; the Fourth Division aims to reassemble the scattered Alliance Fleet.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Operation Starlight begins; the Fourth Division aims to reassemble the scattered Alliance Fleet. :: The Pathfinders of the Rebel Alliance conduct a heist at Coruscant's Imperial Museum with help from Lando Calrissian and Lobot to steal the Autonomous Translator Module, Mark II droid, through which the Fourth and Seventh Divisions intend to establish new secret communications with other rebels.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Operation Starlight begins; the Fourth Division aims to reassemble the scattered Alliance Fleet. :: Starlight Squadron is established. Consisting of Wedge Antilles, Freyta Smyth , L'ulo L'ampar , Shara Bey, Evaan Verlaine, and Mart Mattin, it attempts to contact other rebel groups and establish new secret communications through the codes provided by the Autonomous Translator Module, Mark II linguistics droid , which is kept operational by Lobot.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Operation Starlight begins; the Fourth Division aims to reassemble the scattered Alliance Fleet. :: Starlight Squadron finds the ruins of the Sixth Division instead and is ambushed by Imperial probe droids . Amidst the chaos, Lobot begins to die in his efforts to keep the rebels' linguistics droid operational. While Lando Calrissian remains adamant in telling Lobot to cease maintaining the droid, Kes Dameron, fearing for his love, Shara Bey, demands Calrissian to let Lobot die in exchange so that Bey may be saved.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Operation Starlight begins; the Fourth Division aims to reassemble the scattered Alliance Fleet. :: Calrissian is detained by order of Leia Organa. After secretly learning its language, C-3PO deactivates the Rebellion's linguistics droid in its refusal to yield its self-interest to save Lobot. With command of the droid's language, communications are reestablished with Starlight Squadron, which escapes from Zahra and her flagship, Tarkin's Will , by exploiting the Empire's disregard for its own droids.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Operation Starlight begins; the Fourth Division aims to reassemble the scattered Alliance Fleet. :: However, Shara Bey, Starlight's commanding officer, is captured by Zahra's forces. While a despaired Kes Dameron is counseled by Leia Organa, Lando Calrissian is reunited with Lobot and, seeing that the Rebellion would not care for them, offers to pay off his debt to Jabba the Hutt by stealing the deactivated linguistics droid and its embedded rebel codes.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Operation Starlight begins; the Fourth Division aims to reassemble the scattered Alliance Fleet. :: Having traced the location of Tarkin's Will , the Rebellion plans to strike back at the Empire.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Aphra, Starros, and the Unbroken Clan attempt to seize the replica on Midarr . The engine is destroyed during the resulting firefight, but Aphra steals the crystal core of the original Path engine and gives it to Domina Tagge. In refusing to let Aphra and Starros go yet again, Vukorah loses an eye while chasing them.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Valance and Cadeliah flee to meet Valance's lover, Yuralla Vega, at a rebel outpost on Lowik. Valance leaves Cadeliah in the care of Yuralla Vega as Zuckuss and 4-LOM attack the Lowik rebels, who are separated from Rebel Command as the Empire had broken the rebel security codes. While Valance distracts the hunters by visiting random ports throughout the galaxy, the Lowik rebels evacuate before the Empire's arrival, though they ask Valance to save a lone transport that was lost in the process. Valance does so to settle his debt to Han Solo, but is shot down by the Ohnaka Gang, which is led by Skragg, advised by a captive Dengar, and in pursuit of the locations of the rebel armada.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Working for Jabba alongside the Hutt's majordomo, Bib Fortuna, the hunter Bossk joins the hunted in the Great Hunt of Malastare , sparing Grummgar and killing the InterGalactic Banking Clan Vice Chair Exum Jermit , who had been refusing to do business with Jabba.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Hunt for Han Solo
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Hunt for Han Solo :: While en route transporting Han Solo to Jabba on Tatooine, Boba Fett loses Solo to mysterious figures on Nar Shaddaa after killing the Kanji Criminal Enterprise 's pit fighting champion, Wyrmen Lictor , in the Garkata Fighting Arena .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Hunt for Han Solo :: Crimson Dawn resurfaces under Lady Qi'ra. They steal Han Solo from Boba Fett with the help of Deva Lompop , who in doing so betrays Jabba. Qi'ra aims to bring the great factions of the galaxy together in an auction for Han Solo —hosted by Crimson Dawn on Jekara for a frozen figure wanted by all.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Hunt for Han Solo :: Jabba places a large bounty on Boba Fett, believing that he had betrayed him by selling Solo to another party. T'onga is thus motivated to take up arms again against Fett, and T'onga's wife, Losha, follows.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Hunt for Han Solo :: Valance battles Skragg's Ohnaka Gang, saves the rebel transport, and secures the rebel locations for the Rebel Alliance's Private Blanch Sproull . Hearing of Han Solo's capture by Boba Fett from Dengar, Valance takes Dengar captive to rescue Solo. Aboard a Corona -class armed frigate stolen from the Ohanak Gang, the two hunters pursue Boba Fett and his Slave I .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Hunt for Han Solo :: Vader, believing that any attempts to rebel against the Emperor is futile, changes his strategy of keeping Luke Skywalker and his friends alive; Vader decides that killing his son is the only way, and sets out to hunt for Han Solo using the services of Bokku the Hutt and Ochi of Bestoon.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Hunt for Han Solo :: Aboard Jabba's yacht , the Star Jewel , alongside Bib Fortuna, Bossk learns of Jabba's bounty on Boba Fett.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Hunt for Han Solo :: Intent on killing Fett and rescuing Solo, Valance is tracked down by Zuckuss and 4-LOM in the Y'Toub System . Dengar arranges a deal between the four of them to postpone pursuing the Unbroken Clan's bounty on Cadeliah and instead hunt down the wanted Fett.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Hunt for Han Solo :: Boba Fett is confronted by Zuckuss and 4-LOM. They are defeated and left for dead on Nar Shaddaa and Tatooine, respectively.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Hunt for Han Solo :: Chewbacca hears from Sagwa a lead on Han Solo's location.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Hunt for Han Solo :: Working with Dengar to trail Boba Fett, Valance seeks to help Han Solo since the latter saved his life. The pair run into Chewbacca and C-3PO on Nar Shaddaa. Chewbacca warns Valance not to cross paths with him again on their search for Solo, and they part in peace. Valance and Dengar learn from Devono Vix that Crimson Dawn had returned and stolen Solo from Fett, but Vix is immediately killed by the assassin Deathstick .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Hunt for Han Solo :: Valance and Dengar are pursued by Deathstick at Qi'ra's behest. In a brief interlude, Dengar contacted Bib Fortuna, seeking reassurance that his love interest, the Aruzan Manaroo , remained safe under Jabba's captivity while the bounty hunter remained indebted to the Hutt.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Hunt for Han Solo :: Valance and Dengar quietly escape to Canto Bight. While he remains indebted to Wen Delphis of the Sixth Kin, Dengar reclaims his ship, the Punishing One , on the condition that he and Valance stir up trouble at the Crimson Dawn gathering. Before they leave, Dengar promises to give Deathstick the location of Cadeliah.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Hunt for Han Solo :: Crimson Dawn attacks the Mourner's Wail. T'onga and Losha are cuaght amidst the attack. After repelling the Dawn, T'onga informs Mourner's Wail leader Khamdek that he has a granddaughter—Cadeliah. Khamdek asks T'onga to keep Cadeliah safe from the Dawn.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Hunt for Han Solo :: T'onga and Losha recruit gladiator champion Tasu Leech , who seeks a worthy challenge, and Zuckuss, who was left bitter by the fact that his own friend, 4-LOM, had lost memory of him and attempted to kill him.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Hunt for Han Solo :: On Nar Shaddaa, General Vukorah is invited to the Crimson Dawn gathering by Deathstick.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Hunt for Han Solo :: R2-D2, C-3PO, Luke Skywalker, Chewbacca, and Sagwa are ambushed by the Kanji gang on Nar Shaddaa, and the rebels escape aboard the Falcon . The gang reports their sighting of Luke Skywalker to Imperial Garrison on Vandor.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Hunt for Han Solo :: R2-D2 informs Skywalker of the locations of dozens of Jedi outposts. Sagwa joins the Rebellion, and the Falcon is recalled to the Rebel Fleet by General Leia Organa after an unknown party contacted the Alliance regarding Solo.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Hunt for Han Solo :: Invitations are sent out to various individuals throughout the galaxy, including Jabba the Hutt, Black Sun's Boss Gyuti , and Imperial General Hurst Romodi .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Hunt for Han Solo :: Sly Moore and her court hire IG-88 to destroy Vader in the fear that he could overpower the Emperor. After defeating IG-88 at Zee-Nine City Seven in Hutt Space, Vader, Ochi, and Bokku find a Crimson Dawn lead to Han Solo. Bokku is tasked by Vader with obtaining Solo, as is Sly Moore.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Hunt for Han Solo :: Bounty hunter Boushh and his crew, hired by Crimson Dawn, attempt to assassinate the corporation's executive board. In the ensuing fight, Domina Tagge roots out Tagge Corporation's mole, Silas Tagge , and kills him. She then persuades Boushh's crew to work for her, promising as payment to take them back to their homeworld, Uba IV, from which they had been exiled by Ubese society.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Hunt for Han Solo :: Domina Tagge sends Aphra and Starros to track down Ebann Drake , who had left TaggeCo for Crimson Dawn's auction. Aphra and Starros betray the bounty hunter Durge , who had also taken a bounty on Drake, and take Drake's invitation to Crimson Dawn's auction, which they attend per Domina Tagge's instructions.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Hunt for Han Solo :: Amilyn Holdo , who had been working amongst the crime syndicates, informs Leia Organa that Solo was no longer with Fett, and is instead held at an auction hosted by Crimson Dawn. Organa asks Chewbacca to reach out to Lando Calrissian about the matter, and C-3PO accompanies the Wookiee to handle urgent tasks relating to Starlight Squadron's efforts to find the remainder of the Alliance Fleet.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Hunt for Han Solo :: The Imperial II-class Star Destroyer Ultima II under the command of Admiral Kalaxo traps the Alliance Eleventh Division on the surface of Ab Dalis . The Rebel Ground Defense Command under Colonel Chouch organizes the surface defense against Imperial walkers and tanks. Following Ground Defense Command's destruction, Starlight Squadron arrives and destroys Ultima II by causing an eruption of the volcano directly below it, saving High Command's Admiral Gial Ackbar and Alliance Chancellor Mon Mothma.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Hunt for Han Solo :: Calrissian agrees to help rescue Solo, although he also promises Jabba via Fortuna that his package would be delivered over Jekara. Upon the Falcon ' s arrival over the world, Calrissian secretly jettisons the rebels' linguistics droid into space. As the droid drifts away, it attempts to bargain with Calrissian, temporarily repairing Lobot's implants and restoring his personality.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Hunt for Han Solo :: Calrissian regrets his actions and vows to revive Lobot, although Chewbacca, Organa, and C-3PO pilot the Falcon down to Jekara.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Auction for Han Solo and aftermath
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Auction for Han Solo and aftermath :: On Jekara, Boba Fett leaves Bossk for dead after the Trandoshan attempted to capture him for Jabba.
