Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Auction for Han Solo and aftermath :: Fett infiltrates the auction venue , and Jabba successfully outbids everyone for Solo with one million credits. Back stage, Crae battles Ariole Yu and Lucky. Darth Vader arrives claiming Solo as his own.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Auction for Han Solo and aftermath :: Qi'ra duels Lord Vader while Imperial General Romodi takes Han Solo aboard an Imperial shuttle headed for the Executor .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Auction for Han Solo and aftermath :: Wishing to maintain the Hutts' relationship with the Empire, Jabba lets his prize go before leaving the auction halls himself. Dissatisfied that the Hutts' reputation is at risk, Bokku leads dissident Hutt Council members to take back Solo from Romodi's Imperial shuttle with a Hutt fleet.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Auction for Han Solo and aftermath :: The rebels pursue the same shuttle aboard the Millennium Falcon .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Auction for Han Solo and aftermath :: Wishing to save Han Solo, Valance teams up with his hated rival, Boba Fett, to pursue the same shuttle aboard the Slave I , leaving Dengar stranded on Jekara.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Auction for Han Solo and aftermath :: Vader, sensing the presence of Luke Skywalker above Jekara, pursues the Jedi-trainee in orbit.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Auction for Han Solo and aftermath :: Chelli Lona Aphra and Sana Starros steal a Black Sun necklace containing data crystals from the Crimson Dawn gathering and make their escape. While escaping, work alongside Ariole Yu and Lucky, and Aphra kills Crae. Aphra's electro-tattoos are fried after activating the Thought Dowser to incapacitate a pursuing Deathstick.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Auction for Han Solo and aftermath :: Ariole Yu and Just Lucky are stranded at the Crimson Dawn venue by Aphra and Starros, who escape and find that a network of Crimson Dawn spies had infiltrated Black Sun, the Empire, the Rebellion, the Sixth Kin, as well as the Tagge Corporation. Ariole Yu and Just Lucky eventually depart as well, back to Canto Bight.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Auction for Han Solo and aftermath :: Most guests leave the venue after fighting broke out over Han Solo, but General Vukorah remains with Crimson Dawn, impressed with their confrontation with Vader.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Auction for Han Solo and aftermath :: T'onga, Losha, Tasu Leech, and Zuckuss track Dengar and Valance to Jekara, where they find Bossk, who frees himself using Zuckuss' knife.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Auction for Han Solo and aftermath :: Dengar is recued by Deathstick, to whom he hands the location of Cadeliah as Deathstick promises to pay off Manaroo's debt to Jabba.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Auction for Han Solo and aftermath :: Qi'ra continues pulling her strings as Crimson Dawn's leader, anticipating the coming of the "end" while holding the a Sith holocron .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Auction for Han Solo and aftermath > Qi'ra continues pulling her strings as Crimson Dawn's leader, anticipating the coming of the "end" while holding the a Sith holocron . :: At the Dawn's urging, the Hutt Council armada attacks the Executor , although the shuttle containing Han Solo is safely delivered to the Imperial flagship.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Auction for Han Solo and aftermath > Qi'ra continues pulling her strings as Crimson Dawn's leader, anticipating the coming of the "end" while holding the a Sith holocron . :: Certain that his son's fear is keeping him from being a Jedi, Darth Vader leaves Luke Skywalker and, at Emperor Palpatine's command, personally executes the Hutt Council, including Bokku, over Jekara. Vader thus also learns that Palpatine wants Skywalker alive and is fearful of losing the boy.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Auction for Han Solo and aftermath > Qi'ra continues pulling her strings as Crimson Dawn's leader, anticipating the coming of the "end" while holding the a Sith holocron . :: Ochi apprehends Sly Moore for attempting to save Skywalker from Vader, but the Sith Lord lets Moore go to fulfill his next mission from the Emperor—to hunt down Crimson Dawn's operatives within the Empire.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Auction for Han Solo and aftermath > Qi'ra continues pulling her strings as Crimson Dawn's leader, anticipating the coming of the "end" while holding the a Sith holocron . :: Crimson Dawn also arranges for the crew of the Millennium Falcon to board the Executor and rescue Solo. However, he is instead taken by Boba Fett, who injures Valance and leaves him for dead aboard the Executor .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Auction for Han Solo and aftermath :: Crimson Dawn, the rebels, and Boba Fett depart Jekara.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Auction for Han Solo and aftermath :: Working for Crimson Dawn, Deva Lompop has IG-88 restored to take Solo back from Boba Fett. While IG-88 fails, the droid retreats to a junk world in order to bide his time and improve his systems.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY > Auction for Han Solo and aftermath :: Boba Fett sells Han Solo to Jabba the Hutt on Tatooine, where Fett remains. Having become the sole leader of the Hutt clans, Jabba remains unfettered in regard to the Hutts' relationship with the Empire.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Declaring the success of the "dawn," Qi'ra gathers warriors such as Ren's Knights of Ren in preparation for the reign of her organization.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Empire ascendant (3 ABY) > 3 ABY :: Having had her group of Amidalans killed by Imperial forces, Sabé infiltrates Crimson Dawn, and on Polis Massa, the former handmaiden reactivates a repaired ZED-6-7 so that the droid could work for her in uncovering Vader's interest in Padmé Amidala.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) :: 3–4 ABY
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: By this point, the Archivist had visited Yoda on Dagobah, having been tasked by Qi'ra to find him so that the Jedi Master could assist in her plans. However, Yoda taught the Archivist, and she reports to Crimson Dawn the lie that Yoda was dead.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Ochi is ordered by Vader bring his assassin associates under the Sith Lord's fold. However, on Saki , Ochi kills his fellow master assassins to free an imprisoned Lady Qi'ra, joining Crimson Dawn.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Vader restores Valance and presses him into his service, tasking him with killing Admiral Tarl Sokoli .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Vader and Ochi hire three Assassin's Guild members on Vincorba and recruit three anti-Dawn Revengers on Calior , and they are joined by Valance on Laecor .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Domina Tagge tasks Aphra and Starros with recovering Ascendant technology similar to the Thought Dowser, but the pair run into rogue archaeologist Kho Phon Farrus , who works for the Archivist of Crimson Dawn and has been assembling Ascendant technology as well, in the hopes of finding the Spark Eternal . Aphra, Starros, Kho Phon Farrus, and Iglan'tine Nos enter the Unyielding Heart on Bar'leth , where the Spark Eternal possesses Aphra.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Starros and Kho Phon Farrus inform Domina Tagge of the Spark Eternal, and Tagge tasks them with retrieving both the artifact and Aphra. An ambitious Ronen Tagge also wishes to acquire the Spark Eternal.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Vader leads his group to attack Laecor's Crimson Dawn forces and retrieve a list of their collaborators in the Empire. Unbeknownst to them, Sabé and ZED-6-7 had planted the list there.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Vader and his group purges the suspects on Coruscant and on the Executor .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Sabé and ZED-6-7 question Ochi and Sly Moore—fellow Crimson Dawn collaborators—about Vader's motives. In a later mission to Tranchar , Vader uncovers Ochi's betrayal—intending to use him as a pawn against the Dawn—and follows him to Sabé's place on Naboo.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Vader and Sabé join together, united by their belief in making the galaxy orderly— Sabé concludes that Vader was Anakin Skywalker, who had fathered Luke Skywalker alongside Padmé Amidala.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Ochi also helps General Romodi, who has been unhappy about some of his soldiers being killed by Vader's crew, and informs Romodi's forces about the location of Vader's crew. While Romodi's forces attack the crew, they are occupied by Crimson Dawn forces called in by Ochi, allowing the treacherous assassin to take Vader's crew away from the site. Vader then orders Romodi's forces to battle the Dawn until their end, and a pulse bomb annihilates all the combatants.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: On Gabredor III , Vader and Sabé reunite with Kitster Banai and Wald and target the corrupt Imperial Governor Tauntaza , a Crimson Dawn collaborator; while she escapes, Vader, Sabé, and Ochi discover that she had been developing technology that drains power from living things to the extent of entire planets—the world Karolia had already been devestated.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Believing that Sabé has been trying to bring him back to the light side, Vader pursues the governor himself in anger. He orders the Executor to incinerate Tauntaza's fortress but makes efforts to save his allies. While the governor escapes again, Sabé saves Vader in turn. They discover that Tauntaza had been acting upon direct orders from the Emperor, who is testing his apprentice again.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Vader, accompanied by Sabé, confronts Darth Sidious. However, Vader, still troubled by his suffering in his former self as Anakin Skywalker, turns her over to the Emperor, who asserts that the former handmaiden could be a powerful asset. She and Vader are sent to quell an uprising on Skako Minor .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Luke Skywalker and R2-D2 search the galaxy for Jedi sites, and on Al'doleem , they find a holocron by Yoda that leads them to Gazian , where Skywalker speaks with an imprint of Jedi Elzar Mann and obtains a sacred Jedi text .

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Lady Qi'ra and Crimson Dawn initiate the Syndicate War in order to disrupt the Empire and specifically target the Sith—Darth Sidious and Darth Vader—in a war against the Sith .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY > Lady Qi'ra and Crimson Dawn initiate the Syndicate War in order to disrupt the Empire and specifically target the Sith—Darth Sidious and Darth Vader—in a war against the Sith . :: Chanath Cha and the Orphans sow mistrust and chaos among the galaxy's leading criminal syndicates by secretly attacking their facilities.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY > Lady Qi'ra and Crimson Dawn initiate the Syndicate War in order to disrupt the Empire and specifically target the Sith—Darth Sidious and Darth Vader—in a war against the Sith . :: Qi'ra informs the surviving Rebel Fleet—assembled from the Fourth, Seventh, and Eleventh Divisions—that Zahra's hunter fleet had destroyed all but one remaining rebel division—the Second Division on Panisia . The Rebellion also strikes a deal with Crimson Dawn to organize its supplies, which have been disrupted by the Syndicate War, through Leia Organa's friend Amilyn Holdo.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY > Lady Qi'ra and Crimson Dawn initiate the Syndicate War in order to disrupt the Empire and specifically target the Sith—Darth Sidious and Darth Vader—in a war against the Sith . :: Kes Dameron leads Starlight Squadron and the Pathfinders on an unauthorized mission to rescue Shara Bey from Zahra's flagship, Tarkin's Will , which the Pathfinders infiltrate.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY > Lady Qi'ra and Crimson Dawn initiate the Syndicate War in order to disrupt the Empire and specifically target the Sith—Darth Sidious and Darth Vader—in a war against the Sith . :: Battle of Panisia
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY > Lady Qi'ra and Crimson Dawn initiate the Syndicate War in order to disrupt the Empire and specifically target the Sith—Darth Sidious and Darth Vader—in a war against the Sith . > Battle of Panisia :: Deathstick alerts the Empire to Panisia's rebel base, captures Cadeliah from the rebels, and brings her to Qi'ra.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY > Lady Qi'ra and Crimson Dawn initiate the Syndicate War in order to disrupt the Empire and specifically target the Sith—Darth Sidious and Darth Vader—in a war against the Sith . > Battle of Panisia :: The Tarkin's Will is destroyed by the Pathfinders, who also rescue Shara Bey successfully, but not before the Star Destroyer eliminated the entire rebel base and several of the incoming Rebel Fleet's vessels—Zahra had relished forcing the rebels to kill some of their own members in their hopes of saving the rebel base.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY > Lady Qi'ra and Crimson Dawn initiate the Syndicate War in order to disrupt the Empire and specifically target the Sith—Darth Sidious and Darth Vader—in a war against the Sith . > Battle of Panisia :: Leia Organa duels Ellian Zahra and leaves her to the mercy of Panisia's animals, stating that the Rebellion only kills as a necessity and always for justice.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY > Lady Qi'ra and Crimson Dawn initiate the Syndicate War in order to disrupt the Empire and specifically target the Sith—Darth Sidious and Darth Vader—in a war against the Sith . :: Ochi assassinates ten of the Emperor's Royal Guards in the Imperial Palace.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY > Lady Qi'ra and Crimson Dawn initiate the Syndicate War in order to disrupt the Empire and specifically target the Sith—Darth Sidious and Darth Vader—in a war against the Sith . :: The Knights of Ren raid Fortress Vader to acquire the Screaming Key .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: The Alliance organizes a robbery on Westhill Palace , a mansion in the Westhill cliffs of the city of Canto Bight on Cantonica owned by the Zerek Besh criminal syndicate leader Sliro Barsha , to rescue the captured rebel operative Asara Deyn . During the fight, Kay Vess steals the Trailblazer and escapes Cantonica.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Imperial Governor Thorden stages a plot along with Crimson Dawn and the Pyke Syndicate traitor Tizlak Sharr to ovethrow the Pyke Syndicate underboss Gorak Palas , in an attempt to seize control over the moon of Toshara .

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Kay Vess and the mechanic Waka , under the commission of Crimson Dawn, infiltrate the Imperial Fuel Station Visavi , with the objective of deleting debt records to increase tension between the Pyke Syndicate and the Empire, and extract Bosnok from the facility.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Crime lord Jaylen Vrax gathers a group of outlaws to steal beskar ingots from Sliro Ruback 's vault at Canto Bight. However, with the real intent to reunite with Ruback, who was his brother, to get revenge on him.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY > Crime lord Jaylen Vrax gathers a group of outlaws to steal beskar ingots from Sliro Ruback 's vault at Canto Bight. However, with the real intent to reunite with Ruback, who was his brother, to get revenge on him. :: Kay Vess and her team infiltrate a private Crimson Dawn club known as the Wellspring in Kijimi City to recover a stolen Ashiga Clan artifact, with the hopes of earning Queen Ashiga 's favor, and convince her to free Ank Parako , the bombmaker selected for their heist.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY > Crime lord Jaylen Vrax gathers a group of outlaws to steal beskar ingots from Sliro Ruback 's vault at Canto Bight. However, with the real intent to reunite with Ruback, who was his brother, to get revenge on him. :: Crimson Dawn leader Qi'ra, along with the associate Krisk Ashiga , request Kay Vess to infiltrate the Ashiga weapons factory on Kijimi as a part of their plot in taking over the Ashiga Clan. With the aid from Crimson Dawn, she is able to infiltrate and rescue Ank Parako from the factory.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY > Crime lord Jaylen Vrax gathers a group of outlaws to steal beskar ingots from Sliro Ruback 's vault at Canto Bight. However, with the real intent to reunite with Ruback, who was his brother, to get revenge on him. :: Kay Vess' crew and Crimson Dawn attack the Ashiga Clan stronghold on Kijimi to take control of the Ashiga Clan with the origin strand , the genetic code used by the Ashiga Clan to create more Melitto for the hive , and remove the Clan's relations with the Empire. Kay Vess and Nix initially infiltrate and disable the outer defenses of the stronghold to assist Crimson Dawn, but later become independent after realizing Crimson Dawn's true intentions.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY > Crime lord Jaylen Vrax gathers a group of outlaws to steal beskar ingots from Sliro Ruback 's vault at Canto Bight. However, with the real intent to reunite with Ruback, who was his brother, to get revenge on him. :: Retrieval of krayt dragon pearl .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY > Crime lord Jaylen Vrax gathers a group of outlaws to steal beskar ingots from Sliro Ruback 's vault at Canto Bight. However, with the real intent to reunite with Ruback, who was his brother, to get revenge on him. :: Kay Vess recovers Nix , her merqaal taken from her by the Sullustan gunslinger Hoss and sold to the Hutt Cartel, by infiltrating Jabba's Palace and freeing the imprisoned merqaal from the palace menagerie, but is later intercepted by Jabba the Hutt and his forces. Along with Hoss and the Zerek Besh bounty hunter Vail Tormin , Kay Vess and Nix are ordered to be executed by massifs and later the rancor Pateesa . Though Vess, Nix, and Tormin are able to survive, they are unable to escape from the Palace. However, Jabba the Hutt is impressed by the demonstration of strength, returning Nix to Vess, alongside constraining the group to work for him in exchange for redemption.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY > Crime lord Jaylen Vrax gathers a group of outlaws to steal beskar ingots from Sliro Ruback 's vault at Canto Bight. However, with the real intent to reunite with Ruback, who was his brother, to get revenge on him. :: Jabba the Hutt coerces Vail Tormin and Kay Vess to infiltrate Fort Sunfire , an Imperial stronghold, in order to identify a Zerek Besh spy planted within the Hutt Cartel. They are able to find the data containing the information of the spy within the fortress, but are discovered and pursued by the Empire after an alarm is triggered. Vess, Tormin, and Nix escape with speeder bikes and reach Mos Algo to regroup with the Hutt Cartel, but the place is abandoned by the Cartel after they find themselves tracked by Imperial forces. They are overwhelmed by the Imperial forces, but ND-5 pilots the Trailblazer to push back the Imperials and rescues the trio. They are able to successfully transfer the data to Jabba, the Zerek Besh spy is killed, and Kay Vess' crew is redeemed by Jabba.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY > Crime lord Jaylen Vrax gathers a group of outlaws to steal beskar ingots from Sliro Ruback 's vault at Canto Bight. However, with the real intent to reunite with Ruback, who was his brother, to get revenge on him. :: Kay Vess and her team rescue the Rodian droidsmith Gedeek Obaz from the Imperial facility of Research Station 57 , in order to recruit a crewmember that can surpass the droid gatekeeper of Westhill Palace . After arriving on Akiva to gain intel from the Sullustan crime lord Surat Nuat , Vess and Nix make their way towards Research Station 57. Near the facility, Vess and Nix are able to breach the defenses of the exterior, and eventually infiltrate inside the station. After navigating past the Imperial forces on-site, the two are attacked by a turret model designed by Gedeek Obaz, which they deactivate. Ultimately, Vess confronts Obaz in his workshop at the facility, when she enters a standoff against a protocol droid reprogrammed by the droidsmith. Nevertheless, Vess convinces Obaz to join the crew, and later they escape from the facility.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY > Crime lord Jaylen Vrax gathers a group of outlaws to steal beskar ingots from Sliro Ruback 's vault at Canto Bight. However, with the real intent to reunite with Ruback, who was his brother, to get revenge on him. :: Kay Vess' crew infiltrates the Derosha Six space station to steal access codes needed for breaking into Director Sliro Barsha's vault at Canto Bight. At their time there, Kay Vess and Riko Vess learn that Sliro Ruback is Sliro Barsha, an ISB Director, and that Zerek Besh is an undercover intelligence gathering division of the Imperial Security Bureau charged with infiltrating the galactic underworld, to gather intelligence from various criminal organizations and uncover the Rebel Alliance.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY > Crime lord Jaylen Vrax gathers a group of outlaws to steal beskar ingots from Sliro Ruback 's vault at Canto Bight. However, with the real intent to reunite with Ruback, who was his brother, to get revenge on him. :: The heist on Sliro Barsha's vault on Cantonica is conducted. Afterwards, Jaylen Vrax reveals that they had really stolen Sliro's database, not unmarked beskar ingots, and betrays the crew.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: A major battle is fought in the Akiva system , where the Imperial -class Star Destroyer Revelator is infiltrated by Kay Vess' crew. Inside, they rescue ND-5 and Asara Deyn , and in the subsequent battle both Sliro Barsha and Jaylen Vrax are killed. Later, the Destroyer is attacked and destroyed by the Alliance to Restore the Republic.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: The Rebel Alliance and Kay Vess' team attack the Imperial facility of Kassina Outpost on the planet Akiva, with the aim of removing the Empire's data on the Alpha Droid model of the Viper probe droid , as well as reprogramming the model to aid the Akivan Rebels.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: A sabacc tournament is held aboard the casino cruiser Morenia . The tournament's grand prize is the coordinates to the legendary hidden moon of Okala Five , said to contain two point eight trillion tons of pure cyphrite ore. Participants include Brayzak , EY-E9 , Kay Vess, Lando Calrissian, Mosh , Rauvin Vashiri and Ultus . The prize is stolen by the Starfallers , a gang of shipjackers led by the bandit Aksali Noll .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Forces for the Galactic Empire raid a workshop used by the Starfallers in the Lost Steppe region on the moon Toshara. Governor Thorden acquires the encrypted coordinates of Okala Five.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: The Rebel Alliance and Kay Vess' crew hunt for the coordinates to Okala Five, in an effort to recover it from the Starfallers and later Governor Thorden. After Thorden manipulates Kay Vess to allow the Starfallers to steal the map to Okala Five from the sabacc tournament, Lando Calrissian convinces Kay Vess' crew to join him in intercepting the map from being delivered to Thorden. By gaining intel from the former Imperial bookkeeper, Bosnok, Kay Vess and Nix start by looking for Aksali Noll on Toshara. They discover that the Starfallers are attempting to sell the map to the Galactic Empire, and find the location of the exchange through an Imperial listening outpost. Vess arrives during the Imperial raid , and the Empire extracts the map away from Vess and the Starfallers. She is able to learn the new location of the map after rescuing the captured Noll from the Empire. At the Tosharan capital of Mirogana , Calrissian executes a plan to arrange a handover meeting with Thorden, and smuggles Vess inside the Mirogana Spaceport within a crate claimed to be volatile Tolium gas. As the Rebel stalls the Governor in an extensive conversation, Vess progresses through the Spaceport and later infiltrates Thorden's bunker. Arriving at the terminal of the bunker, Vess extracts the map from the data archive, and detonates the entire facility to create a distraction for Calrissian. She escapes from the base and entrusts the map to Calrissian.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: The Rokana Raiders and later Kay Vess' crew occupy and plunder the Khepi Tomb . Near the High Republic Era, a species known as the Khepi constructed a burial chamber hidden inside an asteroid amid the Sayma Nebula , in reponse to the foretold destruction of their world . Eventually, pirates around the galaxy would spread rumors of an immense treasure left behind in the Tomb. Under the guidance of the Imperial Doctor Jessik , a pirate group known as the Rokana Raiders are the first to reach the Tomb, establishing a base inside the asteroid, but they are ultimately unable to open the tomb through force. The Rokana Raiders' leader, Stinger Tash , discovers the security of the Tomb is controlled by the Architect's Scepter , consisting of two parts of a hilt and a shank. While the Raiders own the shank, the hilt is kept by the pirate Hondo Ohnaka , who attempts to pursuit the treasure himself but is captured by the Rokana Raiders. Ohnaka previously delivered a message to Jet Kordo , but is recieved instead by Kay Vess. While the Rokana Raiders capture Kay Vess' crew during their initial mission to the system, they are able to escape from the Tomb with Ohnaka, with Vess agreeing to create an alliance with Ohnaka. Ohnaka and Vess operate near the Kijimi system , Khepi system , and Tatoo system to acquire a disguise, the R93 refractor Cutlass , and the hilt to ultimately infiltrate the base. After returning to the base with the disguise, Ohnaka and Vess attempt to uncover Stinger's plan and win the favor of the crew through a vote , but they accidentaly activate the dormant Khepi guardian droids provoking the death of Tash and the escape of the Rokana Raiders. ND-5 arrives with the Trailblazer allowing Vess' crew to escape the Tomb.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: T'onga, Losha, Tasu Leech, Bossk, Zuckuss, and 4-LOM attempt to rescue Cadeliah from Crimson Dawn. However, Cadeliah chooses to stay with Qi'ra, who further offers T'onga's crew employment.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Realizing the purposeful threat to the Sith, the Emperor instructs the Imperial Military to target Crimson Dawn; in response, Lady Qi'ra instructs her agents to cause chaos in the Empire.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY > Realizing the purposeful threat to the Sith, the Emperor instructs the Imperial Military to target Crimson Dawn; in response, Lady Qi'ra instructs her agents to cause chaos in the Empire. :: Palpatine speaks with the leadership of the Hutts, Black Sun, the Pykes, the Droid Gotra, and Zerek Besh and instructs them to cease their infighting and defeat Crimson Dawn.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY > Realizing the purposeful threat to the Sith, the Emperor instructs the Imperial Military to target Crimson Dawn; in response, Lady Qi'ra instructs her agents to cause chaos in the Empire. :: T'onga's crew accept a Crimson Dawn mission to Bestine IV , and Vader tasks Valance to stop the Dawn there. T'onga reveals that the entire rebel base containing Valance's loved ones had been eliminated by the Empire, save for Cadeliah, turning a guilt-ridden Valance to destroy the Imperial naval base . In retaliation, Vader's forces bomb the Bestine insurgents and sends Inferno Squad to track down Valance and wipe his memory—his cybernetic memories had glimsped plans for a new Death Star.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY > Realizing the purposeful threat to the Sith, the Emperor instructs the Imperial Military to target Crimson Dawn; in response, Lady Qi'ra instructs her agents to cause chaos in the Empire. :: Taking Imperial bounties, IG-88 eliminates Son-tuul Pride leaders and hunts Vukorah, leader of the Unbroken Clan.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: The Spark Eternal, using Aphra, visits Crimson Dawn in search of knowledge and power, and speaks with the Archivist. Starros, Kho Phon Farrus, Eustacia Okka, Detta Yao, Korin Aphra, and Magna Tolvan assemble to retrieve Doctor Aphra. However, they are being tracked by Ariole Yu and Just Lucky, working for Ronen Tagge along with their old crew in the hopes of scoring their last job and retire safely.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Starros' crew finds 0-0-0 and BT-1 on Birukay , while Ariole Yu and Just Lucky find Doctor Aphra and the Spark with Crimson Dawn. Both groups confront the Spark, but it convinces Kho Phon Farrus to let it go and leaves with the Null Blade and 0-0-0 and BT-1 in her service, with the aim of destroying the Sith.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: The Archivist and the Knights of Ren recover the Fermata Cage for Crimson Dawn using the Screaming Key. They are joined by Kho Phon Farrus as they prepare the Fermata Cage to defeat the Sith.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: The Imperial Unity Day parade on Bar'leth is incinerated by a hit-and-run attack by the Rebellion—the event has been broadcast across the entire Empire.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: The Imperial Security Bureau is alerted to an incident on Al'doleem, which Major Sharin , inspecting the second Death Star , intends to investigate.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: At this point, Emperor Palpatine and Darth Vader are reported by the Rebellion to be aboard the Executor in the Mid Rim.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Death Star workers Bevelyn and Jon Melton , as Crimson Dawn agents, kill Major Sharin and take her shuttle in order to inform the Rebellion the existence of the new superweapon. The couple bring with them their children, Reen and Zelly , hail the Rebel Alliance for help, and flee to the Coruscant underworld. They are rescued by Luke Skywalker, and Alliance High Command learns of the new Death Star's construction.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Amilyn Holdo bring Leia Organa, Luke Skywalker, Lando Calrissian, Chewbacca, and Lobot to a resort on Spira for a vacation. They steal a Nihil Path engine from an auction and enter No-Space , in pursuit of much-needed fuel from the long-lost Kezarat Convoy.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: The other former handmaidens of Queen Amidala work alongside Ochi in order to retrieve—or stop—Sabé from Vader's service. However, when Sabé has herself captured by nationalist revolutionary leader Jul Tambor , Vader intercepts the handmaidens and attempts to turn them to his service too. Although he fails, the Sith Lord rescues Sabé, who chooses to join him and leave her sisterhood.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Sabé finds a diplomatic solution; she unveils to the Skakoan refugees Jul Tambor's true colors as a power-hungry individual willing to sacrifice his own people—he has ordered his forces to bomb a city despite being informed that his people were present there along with Vader, discrediting Tambor. His refugees are granted amnesty by the Empire, saving hundreds of lives.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Vukorah resigns from the Unbroken Clan, allowing IG-88 to eliminate its leaders and leaving unscathed herself.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Having been rescued by Ariole Yu and Just Lucky just prior to the Empire's attack, Starros' crew enter the service of Ronen Tagge, who had joined Crimson Dawn. Ronen's Crimson Dawn forces attempt to eliminate Domina Tagge but they are thwarted, with Starros' crew aiding Domina instead and thus clearing their debt to the Tagges.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: The Empire finds and attacks Crimson Dawn's flagship. The Emperor speaks with Qi'ra, who loses the battle but orders the Fermata Cage to be activated, lying that she wishes to free an ancient Sith trapped by the artifact.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Threatened, Sidious and Vader pursue the Fermata Cage. The two Dark Lords arrive at the Amaxine Station aboard the Executor and they are attacked by Crimson Dawn's fleet .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY > Threatened, Sidious and Vader pursue the Fermata Cage. The two Dark Lords arrive at the Amaxine Station aboard the Executor and they are attacked by Crimson Dawn's fleet . :: Starros' crew and Kho Phon Farrus help Doctor Aphra expel the Spark Eternal from her body near the Fermata Cage. Starros successfully forces the "hopelessly emotionally constipated" Aphra and Magna Tolvan to have a real conversation, and the crew part ways afterward.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY > Threatened, Sidious and Vader pursue the Fermata Cage. The two Dark Lords arrive at the Amaxine Station aboard the Executor and they are attacked by Crimson Dawn's fleet . :: Vader joins Sidious before the Fermata Cage. Qi'ra activates it, beginning to trap the Dark Lords, but they are freed by the Knights of Ren.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY > Threatened, Sidious and Vader pursue the Fermata Cage. The two Dark Lords arrive at the Amaxine Station aboard the Executor and they are attacked by Crimson Dawn's fleet . :: Qi'ra acknowledges her failure and scatters the Dawn fleet. She leaves her remaining resources to Cadeliah, her protégé.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY > Threatened, Sidious and Vader pursue the Fermata Cage. The two Dark Lords arrive at the Amaxine Station aboard the Executor and they are attacked by Crimson Dawn's fleet . :: The Fermata Cage's activation causes a great disturbance in the Force, creating a Force Wave across the galaxy.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY > Threatened, Sidious and Vader pursue the Fermata Cage. The two Dark Lords arrive at the Amaxine Station aboard the Executor and they are attacked by Crimson Dawn's fleet . > The Fermata Cage's activation causes a great disturbance in the Force, creating a Force Wave across the galaxy. :: Vader loses control of his powers, and Sabé attempts to calm him down, taking him to an isolated world . However, while Vader demands her loyalty, Sabé refuses to give in to hate, and the Sith Lord casts her away in an ocean. The Emperor tasks his Empire to hunt for the rogue Vader.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY > Threatened, Sidious and Vader pursue the Fermata Cage. The two Dark Lords arrive at the Amaxine Station aboard the Executor and they are attacked by Crimson Dawn's fleet . > The Fermata Cage's activation causes a great disturbance in the Force, creating a Force Wave across the galaxy. :: Luke Skywalker is similarly affected, with his powers intermittently augmented uncontrollably or weakened. Nevertheless, with his help, the rebels recover much of the Kezarat Colony , who give their fuel to the Rebel Fleet, though some of its population decides to remain in No-Space and colonize the former Great Hall of the Nihil . Lando Calrissian and Amilyn Holdo had become romantically involved while in No-Space, and they reaffirm their commitment to the rebel cause.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Inferno Squad successfully wipes Valance's memories.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: As the Alliance prepares for an aerial assault on Coruscant , Luke Skywalker travels to Christophsis to obtain a kyber crystal and build his own lightsaber along with R2-D2. He receives help from Gretta and Dr. Cuata , communicates with a Sith and Yoda, and leaves with a red and a green crystal.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Lando Calrissian, Chewbacca, Nien Nunb, Freyta Smyth, and Bryen Rowne battle Corusant's defenses and attack the Emperor's statue in Monument Plaza . The latter two rebels are lost, but the burning statue is broadcast on the HoloNet.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Vader acquires a shield of kyberite to focus his raw Force powers with Doctor Aphra's help on Ringo Vinda .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: By Imperial mandate, ZED-6-7 leads a group of droids who had been wronged by Vader to hunt down the cyborg Sith. However, ZED helps Vader to defeat the droids and rebuild them to his allegiance, forming a droid army, and Aphra escapes, as does 0-0-0 and BT-1.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Aphra, Luke Skywalker, and R2-D2 find a Jedi temple on Sason and battle the Starweird .

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: T'onga reassembles her crew, wishing to save Valance's deteriorating mind. Boba Fett gives them coordinates to cyborg-fixer Tarr Kligson after they eliminate a rival Black Sun leader .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: An ancient an powerful droid intelligence , that was one locked away in the Fermata Cage by the Sith, is released during the battle at Amaxine Station . It merges with the Spark Eternal, giving birth to the Scourge , an insatiable being wishing to spread and control other minds and bodies.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Emperor Palpatine orders the Imperial forces at the Amaxine Station to retrieve all material and take them to Al'doleem , a moon in the Mid Rim Territories. The Scourge corrupts a KX-series security droid that had been deployed from the Star Destroyer Chelator as part of the recovery mission. Afterward, the Scourge spreads through the Destroyer from the security droid to astromech droids and protocol droids. The 2,518 droids are corrupted, catching the humans by surprise, and kill the 46,785 human crew members. The Scourge renames it the Scourge 01 , that is gradually transformed into a monstrous vessel black in color and with many irregular spikes.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Ajax Sigma's Second Revelation droid movement attempts to destroy the Scourge.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Mon Mothma, Admiral Ackbar, General Madine, Princess Organa, Luke Skywalker, and Commodore Grek convene to discuss the mysterious second Death Star. Meanwhile, C-3PO succumbs to the Scourge, which wishes to control Force-wielders too.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Lando Calrissian and Lobot travel to the palace of Jabba the Hutt on Tatooine to retrieve a droid Lando had previously sold to Jabba, in order to save Lobot after his cybernetics begin failing and threaten to kill him. At the same time, the newly reassembled D-Squad make their way to Jabba's Palace to search for coordinates that can lead them to Ajax Sigma . Lando Calrissian escapes with the talky droid, Lobot is captured by Scourged droids and is also infected, and D-Squad learn Ajax Sigma is on Mechis III .

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Forces of the Scoure attack Fortress Vader on Mustafar in order to inhabit Darth Vader for his knowledge of the Force. The attempt is ultimately a failure after Vader prevents himself from being infected, destroying multiple DT-series sentry droids, spider-droids and MSE-6 series repair droids.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Droids of the Second Revelation attack the Scourge 01 while the Scourge is attempting to corrupt Vader. During the battle, a number of the Second Revelation's droids are almost infected by the Scourge, but they destroy themselves to prevent their minds from falling into its hands. Ajax Sigma , the leader of the Revelation, is forced to escape having failed to destroy the Scourge.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Doctor Aphra and Just Lucky travel to a Tagge Corporation warehouse on Havel Prime to determine whether the prototype battle droids stored at the facility are experiencing a similar malfunction that another of the battle droids have suffered. As the two fight against the droids, which have been infected by the Scourge, they shut down, leading Aphra and Lucky to believe that they were running off of their backup power, explaining why they were able to operate for a short span of time before they powered down.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Aboard the Tagge Corporation research facility ship, the Casus Belli , employees of the corporation fight the ship's droid crew, which has been infected by the Scourge. Ultimately, Lady Domina Tagge deems the Casus Belli to be beyond saving and has it crashed onto the nearby moon Asheris , resulting in the destruction of the ship and all of the Scourged droids aboard.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Magna Tolvan leads a search for the missing Rebel Alliance supply-run ship Silver Arrow .

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: The Executor , flagship of Darth Vader, is targeted by droids corrupted by the Scourge in order to infect Vader himself. The droids, many of whom were part of the droid uprising recently reprogrammed to serve Vader's will, take over the vessel, compromising it, leading to Emperor Palpatine requesting, via Grand Vizier Mas Amedda, that Vader resecure it. During the battle, he is forced to fight the droids affected by the Scourge. Meanwhile, a hostile force of five Star Destroyers surround the Executor , which Amedda deems a lost cause and orders it destroyed to contain the Scourge. However, Admiral Corleque delays the order, giving Vader's own droids long enough to shut down the Executor 's mechanical compliment. The Scourge seeds itself onto Corleque's flagship, the Unbreakable , which Admiral Firmus Piett destroys after giving his fellow admiral a mere four seconds to contain the infection.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: On Mustafar, the Scourge corrupts an octuptarra tri-droid from an abandoned Techno Union droid factory . Having hijacked ZED-6-7 , a droid servant of Vader, after the failed attempted to corrupt Vader aboard the Executor , the Scourge attacks Vader and his other droid servants with ZED and the octuptarra. Through the skirmish, Vader manages to re-establish his control over the Force, which he had struggled to command since the Force Wave returned. With his Force powers his to command once more, Vader destroys the corrupted octuptarra and ZED, who is later rebuilt.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: R2-D2 reassembles D-Squad to combat the Scourge.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: The Scourge attacks Coruscant, brought upon by Vader as part of a test for Emperor Palpatine. As part of his test, Vader collects a number of the Scourge's spider-droids, which it uses to infect droids and cyborgs. Arriving on Coruscant aboard his TIE Advanced x1 and speeding past an Imperial checkpoint looking for any sign of the Scourge, Vader is shot down but crashes into the Imperial Palace , where he is surrounded by a group of stormtroopers from a Lambda -class T-4a shuttle. Vader wipes out every trooper sent against him, including members of his own death trooper guard who were ordered to turn on Vader by the Emperor. The spider-droids escape Vader's TIE and infect the death trooper corpses as Vader struggles with Sidious. While Vader neck snapps each trooper and lifts them into the air to prevent their escape into the Lambda shuttle, he throws the spider-droids out into Imperial City , where the Scourge uses them to find and infect droids to massacre the populace. While Sidious strickes down several droids with his Force lightning, taking several civilians with them, the infected droids make it out into the city, where they quickly kill up to one hundred citizens. To protect his capital, Sidious orders Vizier Amedda to wipe out sector 35 and sector 36 under protocol 23. As laser weaponry levels both sectors, destroying any Scourged droids. Vader is allowed to leave Coruscant aboard the Lambda, where the Scourge uses a surviving spider-droid to tell Vader that, by bonding with it, he will finally be powerful enough to defeat Sidious. Vader willingly allows the Scourge to infect him but quickly separates his armor from the Scourge at large, protecting himself as he begins to create a Scourge infection of his own on Mirgoshirax .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Magna Tolvan is rescued from the Scourge on the galactic relay hub Epikonia by her former lovers Doctor Chelli Lona Aphra and Sana Starros, as well as Just Lucky. Chelli Aphra learns the Scourge plans to strike at the Tagge Corporation.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Tarr Kligson works with the Scourge to suppress Valance's consciousness. The Scourge abaondons Kligson and wishes to experiment with Valance, but T'onga and her crew attempt to kill their former friend.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: A battle is fought in Tagge Space between the Tagge Corporation and the Scourge, who has infected the company's droids and cyborg employees. Lady Domina Tagge is rescued from the Scourge, and Doctor Aphra crashes the Acquisitor into Rocedila , destroying all Scourged droids.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Members of the Rebel Alliance battle the Scourge on Epikonia to rescue Lobot.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Ajax Sigma and the D-Squad carry out a second attack on the Scourge 01. Sigma destroys the Scourge.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Valance's memories are restored.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: The Alliance to Restore the Republic holds a trial of Lando Calrissian in accordance with the Corellian code of military justice for treason, mutiny, desertion and insubordination against the Alliance.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Mon Mothma goes on a funding mission for the Alliance to the planet Uveron . Originally, Lando Calrissian was supposed to join Mothma on the mission, before being arrested.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Mon Mothma is kidnapped by Scarabi mercenaries while on Uveron. In a break in the negotiations, Mothma served as a magistrate for the Trial of Lando Calrissian via hologram. During the trial, the mercenaries, led by Hachi break into Uveron City and capture her. Keeping her hidden at Safe House Four , the Scarabi hold her at ransom for 200 million credits, and threaten to inform other third parties, such as the Empire, if the Rebel Alliance does not respond within a day.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: The Alliance deploys the pathfinders to locate Mon Mothma. Unable to pay the price, a solution comes from Lando Calrissian, who knows the Scarabi leader, Hachi , from previous jobs and provides the Rebel Alliance with locations of various safehouses that can hold Mothma. With the help of Chewbacca, Calrissian is able to join the rebels on the rescue. The first safehouse is booby trapped with droidekas and yields no results for the Rebellion. The pathfinders move on to the second and third , and are finally able to locate Mothma at Safe House Four . Inside the safehouse, Mothma is attempting to convince Hachi on the principals of the Rebel Alliance and freedom. Hachi starts to believe it was a mistake to hold her captive, but she is killed by a fellow mercenary before she can act on it. Calrissian and Chewbacca then reach Mothma and free her from her captures.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Lando is found guilty of treason and insubordination and sentenced to a retributive fine of two hundred and fifty million credits, suspended until the end of the Galactic Civil War and subject to change depending on Lando's future conduct.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: A crew of raiders working with the former Imperial Ellian Zahra attack the Survivor Fleet of Alderaanians who had escaped the destruction of their planet. The raiders destroy several of the fleet's ships and capture the vessel known as the Thranta before the rest of the fleet escapes. Zahra then uses the captured vessel to set up a trap for Leia Organa, which results in the Imperial's death.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Mission to Niraya .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Attack on a rebel base .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: At Zee-Nine City Seven , the Alliance destroys the Imperial -class Star Destroyer Impervious and takes Imperial Lieutenant Enric Pryde prisoner.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Mission to Slandarvia M-9 .

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: The Schism Imperial carries out an assault attempting to challenge Emperor Palpatine's power by attacking the Sith Citadel on Exegol , where he keeps his greatest treasures and secrets.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Skirmish on Caranthanax B .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY :: Skirmish on Endoviara Seven .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) :: 3–4 ABY (continued)
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY (continued) :: By this point, the 61st Mobile Infantry had conducted Operation Ringbreaker , a failed campaign against the Kuat Drive Yards formulated by the ex-Imperial Governor Everi Chalis . The operation involves a succession of attacks on weaker Imperial targets in order to force redeployments from the planet Kuat 's security, weakening the shipyards for a planned final attack.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY (continued) > By this point, the 61st Mobile Infantry had conducted Operation Ringbreaker , a failed campaign against the Kuat Drive Yards formulated by the ex-Imperial Governor Everi Chalis . The operation involves a succession of attacks on weaker Imperial targets in order to force redeployments from the planet Kuat 's security, weakening the shipyards for a planned final attack. :: Twilight Company attacks the Imperial warehouse world Mardona III , where they plant thousands of ion mines in tram rails, junctions, and warehouse entrances throughout the planet, forcing the Empire to shut down the whole system. It would take months for the Empire to clear them all.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY (continued) > By this point, the 61st Mobile Infantry had conducted Operation Ringbreaker , a failed campaign against the Kuat Drive Yards formulated by the ex-Imperial Governor Everi Chalis . The operation involves a succession of attacks on weaker Imperial targets in order to force redeployments from the planet Kuat 's security, weakening the shipyards for a planned final attack. :: The Sixty-First attacks the dockyards of the gas giant Najan-Rovi , which harbored Imperial luxury transports produced on Corellia for high ranking Imperial officers on the Imperial Ruling Council . The loss of these forces Corellia to produce more and detract supplies from Kuat.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY (continued) > By this point, the 61st Mobile Infantry had conducted Operation Ringbreaker , a failed campaign against the Kuat Drive Yards formulated by the ex-Imperial Governor Everi Chalis . The operation involves a succession of attacks on weaker Imperial targets in order to force redeployments from the planet Kuat 's security, weakening the shipyards for a planned final attack. :: By ths point, the Obumubo Imperial garrison had been fighting Obumubo 's sea creatures for months, and prove to be experienced when fighting the Sixty-First on the liquid-metal moon.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY (continued) > By this point, the 61st Mobile Infantry had conducted Operation Ringbreaker , a failed campaign against the Kuat Drive Yards formulated by the ex-Imperial Governor Everi Chalis . The operation involves a succession of attacks on weaker Imperial targets in order to force redeployments from the planet Kuat 's security, weakening the shipyards for a planned final attack. :: The 61st fights in Nakadia , destroying the planet's plastoid factories where farm crops were made into armor-grade polymers and synthetic resins. During the fight, Rebel soldiers are exposed to the same bioweapons they encountered during the Coyerti campaign .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY (continued) > By this point, the 61st Mobile Infantry had conducted Operation Ringbreaker , a failed campaign against the Kuat Drive Yards formulated by the ex-Imperial Governor Everi Chalis . The operation involves a succession of attacks on weaker Imperial targets in order to force redeployments from the planet Kuat 's security, weakening the shipyards for a planned final attack. :: Twilight Company fights in the icy mountains of the planet Naator , a contributor to the Empire's war effort.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY (continued) > By this point, the 61st Mobile Infantry had conducted Operation Ringbreaker , a failed campaign against the Kuat Drive Yards formulated by the ex-Imperial Governor Everi Chalis . The operation involves a succession of attacks on weaker Imperial targets in order to force redeployments from the planet Kuat 's security, weakening the shipyards for a planned final attack. :: The 61st confronts Imperial forces in the canyons of Xagobah , sustaining some casualties but emerging victorious.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY (continued) > By this point, the 61st Mobile Infantry had conducted Operation Ringbreaker , a failed campaign against the Kuat Drive Yards formulated by the ex-Imperial Governor Everi Chalis . The operation involves a succession of attacks on weaker Imperial targets in order to force redeployments from the planet Kuat 's security, weakening the shipyards for a planned final attack. :: 20 soldiers Twilight Company launch an attack on the Kuliquo belt , an Imperial mining asteroid field. The soldiers were personally selected by Captain Hazram Namir to sabotage machinery located in an airless and lightless death trap.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Crimson chaos (3–4 ABY) > 3–4 ABY (continued) > By this point, the 61st Mobile Infantry had conducted Operation Ringbreaker , a failed campaign against the Kuat Drive Yards formulated by the ex-Imperial Governor Everi Chalis . The operation involves a succession of attacks on weaker Imperial targets in order to force redeployments from the planet Kuat 's security, weakening the shipyards for a planned final attack. :: Operation Ringbreaker ends with the Siege of Inyusu Tor on Sullust. The Inyusu Tor mineral processing facility is attacked and captured by the 61st, but they are sieged by the Empire's 97th Legion . The Rebels are victorious and a portion of the world is liberated from Imperial rule, but Operation Ringbreaker is called off following the destruction of the 61st' Thunderstrike .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Return of the Jedi (4 ABY) :: 4 ABY

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Return of the Jedi (4 ABY) > 4 ABY :: After eliminating a rebel cell in the aftermath of the Battle of Hoth, Squadron Five of Shakara Nuress 's 204th Imperial Fighter Wing , also known as Shadow Wing, reclaim the Kudo system from the defecting Admiral Gratloe 's private faction . The 204th's Lyttan Dree and Zin Graw are killed amidst an attack by the Rebel Alliance after Graw, secretly wanting to defect to the rebels, leaked intelligence to the Alliance.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Return of the Jedi (4 ABY) > 4 ABY :: Leia Organa travels to Ord Mantell to find Maz Kanata for assistance in rescuing Han Solo. Boushh attacks Organa, though the bounty hunter was defeated by the latter, who took from Kanata's teachings of patience. Organa wears Boushh's armor as a disguise, thanking Maz Kanata before leaving in the Millennium Falcon with Chewbacca and R2-D2.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Return of the Jedi (4 ABY) > 4 ABY :: Boushh attempts to blackmail Black Sun. He fails and falls to agents of the crime syndicate, though his demise is not well-known.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Return of the Jedi (4 ABY) > 4 ABY :: Leia Organa, disguised as Boushh, rescues Lando Calrissian from bounty hunters on Arkanis. One hunter, Bossk, attempts to capture Chewbacca, although the Wookiee is later rescued. Organa thus cements Boushh's reputation with Bossk.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Return of the Jedi (4 ABY) > 4 ABY :: With the Millennium Falcon , Lando Calrissian and Chewbacca attempt to find Han Solo, eventually arriving at Jabba's Palace .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Return of the Jedi (4 ABY) > 4 ABY :: The After the Rebel Alliance discovers a meeting of the Imperial Navy in the Hudalla system five X-wings of Corona Squadron are sent to survey the system, where they engage in a dogfight with four TIE/IN interceptors sent by Admiral Firmus Piett , who suspected a rebel presence. Piett gave Commander Ciena Ree secret orders to let at least one of the Rebels escape, in order to set the trap at Endor.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Return of the Jedi (4 ABY) > 4 ABY :: Less than one week before Operation Yellow Moon, Bothan operatives obtain footage of the second Death Star at Endor . The Bothans bring the information to the Rebellion's Mon Mothma, Gial Ackbar, and Airen Cracken at the cost of many lives.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Return of the Jedi (4 ABY) > 4 ABY :: Rebel intelligence chief and general Airen Cracken's men report a confirmed sighting of Boba Fett's ship over Tatooine, and that Fett has been paid for the delivery of Han Solo and is continuing to work for Jabba the Hutt.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Return of the Jedi (4 ABY) > 4 ABY :: With the Rebellion's fleet still scattered across the galaxy to prevent another major defeat, the rebel convoy protecting Princess Leia Organa withstands an attack by Imperial forces.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Return of the Jedi (4 ABY) > 4 ABY :: Rebels on Zastiga
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Return of the Jedi (4 ABY) > 4 ABY > Rebels on Zastiga :: Mon Mothma orders members of Rebel High Command to convene on Zastiga. Aboard the Mellcrawler , Nien Nunb transports Leia Organa, Caluan Ematt, and C-3PO to Zastiga, where they join Luke Skywalker and R2-D2.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Return of the Jedi (4 ABY) > 4 ABY > Rebels on Zastiga :: On Zastiga, while Mon Mothma, Gial Ackbar, Airen Cracken, Crix Madine, Leia Organa, Carlist Rieekan, and a number of top admirals and generals—amongst them Nantz and Massa , as well as Veertag and Tantor —meet at a safe house to discuss the second Death Star, Luke Skywalker, Wedge Antilles, and Nien Nunb meet for a discussion of how Nunb saved Antilles at Hagar Secundus .
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Return of the Jedi (4 ABY) > 4 ABY > Rebels on Zastiga :: Luke Skywalker, who had rejected Antilles' offer for him to rejoin Red Squadron and had been delaying his return to Yoda on Dagobah to await news of Han Solo, expects that he would return to the Redemption . Skywalker also tells Leia Organa of his need to master patience to become a Jedi.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Return of the Jedi (4 ABY) > 4 ABY > Rebels on Zastiga :: The next day, taking from Skywalker's lesson, Organa proposes Operation Yellow Moon to buy much-needed time for the Rebellion and the galaxy at the risk of her life. Mon Mothma approves of the proposal after telling Organa not to deny herself another chance at love; duty cannot provide the comfort love would bring.

Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Return of the Jedi (4 ABY) > 4 ABY > Rebels on Zastiga :: Rebels leave Zastiga the following day.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Return of the Jedi (4 ABY) > 4 ABY :: Operation Yellow Moon; Aboard Nien Nunb's Mellcrawler , Leia Organa leads a faux Alliance recruiting mission in the Corva sector , nearly a hundred-thousand light years away from the second Death Star, that is meant to distract the Empire while the Rebellion gathers an armada at Sullust.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Return of the Jedi (4 ABY) > 4 ABY > Operation Yellow Moon; Aboard Nien Nunb's Mellcrawler , Leia Organa leads a faux Alliance recruiting mission in the Corva sector , nearly a hundred-thousand light years away from the second Death Star, that is meant to distract the Empire while the Rebellion gathers an armada at Sullust. :: Imperials led by Captain Khione garrison Basteel and Sesid in retaliation for Organa attempting to recruit for the Alliance on the two worlds, imposing greater fear and oppression on their populace in the goal of instilling absolute obedience.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Return of the Jedi (4 ABY) > 4 ABY > Operation Yellow Moon; Aboard Nien Nunb's Mellcrawler , Leia Organa leads a faux Alliance recruiting mission in the Corva sector , nearly a hundred-thousand light years away from the second Death Star, that is meant to distract the Empire while the Rebellion gathers an armada at Sullust. :: Unwilling to cause more lives of others to be lost while doing their duty for the fate of the Rebellion and the galaxy, Organa's crew decide to go beyong their mission parameters and assist in a civilian uprising against the Empire on Jaresh, ensuring that the local resistance succeeds.
Timeline of galactic history > Imperial Era (19 BBY – 5 ABY) > Galactic Civil War—after the Battle of Yavin (0–5 ABY) > Return of the Jedi (4 ABY) > 4 ABY > Operation Yellow Moon; Aboard Nien Nunb's Mellcrawler , Leia Organa leads a faux Alliance recruiting mission in the Corva sector , nearly a hundred-thousand light years away from the second Death Star, that is meant to distract the Empire while the Rebellion gathers an armada at Sullust. :: Organa's crew proceed to the Yellow Moon , over which rebel recruits from the Corva sector are set to rendezvous as arranged by the Rebel Alliance. The Mellcrawler broadcasts a message to the gathered rebels that they are only a distraction for a major upcoming Alliance battle elsewhere, and telling them to flee. As the rebels leave, Captain Khione's Imperial flagship, the Shieldmaiden , arrives and captures the Mellcrawler .
